economic recession; economic slump

takeaways: 用该替代重复名词：正式书面文体中，第二次提到机构/人物时改用该+简称（该行、该校、该厂），比重复全称更简洁，也比这个更正式。商务报告、新闻稿必备技巧。
因果链式论证：用经济不景气→货币供给量减少→上市公司收益下跌→投资者感到悲观→从而抛售股票→这些因素都会导致股市下跌构建多步因果链，展示严谨的经济学分析。关键连词：从而、导致、造成。
三段式结构化说明：商务汇报和说明性文章中，用主要包括三个方面：第一……第二……第三……将内容框架化，听者和读者清楚知道总量和顺序。可用总之……收尾强化逻辑闭合。
书面语词替换口语词：将口语词换成书面词立即提升文章正式度——只→仅；还/仍然→尚；就像→如同；通过/经过→经；根据/依照→按照；到……为止→截至……。这套替换是从HSK5-6向商务书面语跨越的关键。
用类比解释专业术语：炒菜→炒股和晴雨表两例展示了将抽象专业概念用日常类比具体化的技巧。商务演讲和报告中，一句精准类比比三段定义更有说服力，也更易记。
财经新闻的精确性与生动性并重：财经报道同时使用具体数字（499.19点、4.93%、14.8亿股）和程度动词（暴涨、猛升、跃升、大为沾光）。前者建立可信度，后者建立生动感。两者缺一不可。
用继……之后衔接叙事：叙述连续事件时，继上个交易日剧增320点之后，道指两日内猛升819点比然后又紧凑得多。这个句式在新闻叙事、商务简报、进展汇报中均可使用。
双向权衡句型：展示如果责任范围大就增加费用、但范围过小又得不到赔偿的两难逻辑，适用于商务建议、风险评估、政策分析等文体，体现客观分析能力。
建议文的标准写作框架：定义问题+触发条件（你一旦……就应该……）→分情境对策（五点并列），每条用以免、从而、不仅……还能赋予逻辑。这个框架可直接移植到商务建议报告。
归因句式这主要是因为……：分析问题原因时，先用这主要是因为明确归因，再展开具体原因。比直接陈述原因更有条理，适合商务分析和市场汇报。
托……之福和倍受冷落等书面惯用语：掌握这类略带文言色彩的表达可使文章显得更成熟、地道。托……之福（受益于……）用于正面归因；倍受冷落（加倍受冷遇）用于形容被忽视的状态。两者常见于商业新闻评论。
商务邮件中数字的锚定作用：高东华来信示范了即使是私人商务邮件，也应包含具体数据（167.52点、跌幅0.98%、305亿美元）来支持观点。数字增加可信度，搭配推断性结语（看来我也应该……）展示从数据到决策的完整思路。

essays.title: 商务访问日程介绍 (Presenting a Business Visitor's Itinerary)
essays.genre: Spoken itinerary briefing / business hospitality script
essays.text: 李先生，我来介绍一下您在中国的日程。您的飞机是11月15号十点到，我会去机场接您。我给您订了房间，我先送您去宾馆。中午钱经理请您吃饭，下午在我们公司开会。11月16号您可以休息一下，如果您想去走走，我可以送您去。您的飞机是11月17号早上十一点的，我早上八点去接您，送您去机场。您看这样的日程可以吗？如果您有什么需要，请告诉我，别客气。
essays.summary: A company liaison briefs an incoming business visitor on a three-day itinerary: airport pickup, hotel check-in, hosted lunch, afternoon meeting, free day, and departure send-off. Ends with an open offer to accommodate any needs.
essays.why_study: This is the most business-rich passage in the chunk. It models the standard formula for presenting a 接待 (reception/hospitality) itinerary in spoken Chinese — airport pickup, hosted meal, meeting, departure. The closing formula 如果您有什么需要，请告诉我，别客气 is a set phrase used universally in Chinese business hospitality. The text also densely illustrates 先...再/然后 sequencing and the polite request marker 您看...可以吗？
key_devices: 先...然后...再 sequencing for step-by-step agenda
如果您有什么需要，请告诉我，别客气 — standard hospitality closing
您看...可以吗？ — polite confirmation-seeking tag
给您订了 (booked for you), 请您吃饭 (treat you to a meal), 接您/送您 (pick you up/take you) — core reception collocations
Implicit politeness through all-second-person framing: every sentence centers the visitor's comfort

essays.title: 请假留言 (Sick-Leave Voicemail to Manager)
essays.genre: Business voice message / informal written memo
essays.text: 您好，李经理。我是CTI公司的李好。我生病了，要在家休息一下。下周一不能和您开会了。我星期五上班，我们可以星期五开会吗？请给我回个电话，谢谢。
essays.summary: An employee leaves a phone message for her manager: she is ill, cannot attend Monday's meeting, proposes rescheduling to Friday, and asks for a call-back.
essays.why_study: A compact, complete model of the Chinese business message formula: [greeting + self-identification] → [reason/situation] → [impact on plans] → [proposed alternative] → [action request] → [thanks]. This five-beat structure transfers directly to email and SMS in professional Chinese.
key_devices: Self-ID formula: 我是[公司]的[姓名] — always name your company before your name in formal messages
下周一不能和您...了 — 了 here signals a changed state (plan no longer holds)
请给我回个电话 — standard call-back request phrase
Compact closure: 谢谢 alone (no name) — appropriate for voice message

essays.title: 口头请假申请 (Verbal Sick-Leave Request to Manager)
essays.text: 李经理，您好，我是王欢。我生病了，今天去看了医生。医生说我感冒了，有点儿发烧，让我多休息。我想在家休息两天，可以吗？谢谢您。
essays.summary: An employee calls her manager to report illness: she saw a doctor, was diagnosed with a cold and mild fever, and requests two days of rest at home.
essays.why_study: Models how to frame a leave request in Chinese: lead with the medical fact (doctor's authority), then make the request, then seek approval with 可以吗？. The structure 医生说...让我...（做某事）is a polite way to back a request with professional authority rather than personal preference.
key_devices: 医生说...让我多休息 — cite authority (doctor's orders) to legitimize a request
有点儿发烧 — 有点儿 for mild, sympathy-evoking complaints
我想...，可以吗？ — soft permission-seeking pattern
谢谢您 (not 谢谢你) — 您 maintains formal register even with one's own manager

essays.title: 今天出了很多错 (Narrative: A Day Full of Mistakes)
essays.text: 今天我做错了很多事情。高飞让我买咖啡，我买错了，买了茶。李好让我三点开会，我听错了，两点去的，等了一个小时。王欢让我告诉她王经理的电话，我说错了，说的是李经理的电话。我这是怎么了？
essays.summary: A self-deprecating monologue: the narrator made three successive errors (bought the wrong drink, went to a meeting at the wrong time, gave the wrong phone number), ending with a rhetorical question of self-bewilderment.
essays.why_study: Excellent drill text for V+错 resultative complement — the single most useful pattern for describing workplace errors and corrections. The text also shows the 让 causative in parallel structure and the 是...的 focus construction (两点去的). The closing rhetorical 我这是怎么了？ is a natural idiomatic expression of exasperation.
key_devices: V+错 repeated in parallel: 买错了 / 听错了 / 说错了 — resultative complement pattern
让 causative: [person] 让我 [V] — reporting what you were asked to do
是...的 focus: 两点去的 — stresses the time (unexpected detail)
Anaphoric structure: each sentence follows the same shape, creating rhythm and cumulative comic effect
我这是怎么了？ — rhetorical self-questioning idiom

essays.title: 上班路途比较 (Comparing Commutes to Work)
essays.text: 你们每天怎么去上班？李好的家离公司很近，她每天走路上班，要走十分钟。我家离公司很远，我坐地铁上班，要坐十站。地铁虽然很快，但是人太多了。李经理的家也很远，他开车上班，要开一个小时。张迎的家不太远，她每天坐公共汽车上班，要坐半个小时。
essays.summary: A narrator describes how four people commute to work, contrasting distance, mode of transport, and time taken.
essays.why_study: Clean model of 离 (distance from), 要 (takes/costs time), and 虽然...但是... in a natural descriptive context. The passage shows how to write a short comparative paragraph about multiple people using parallel structure — useful for business reports comparing teams, options, or scenarios.
key_devices: 家离公司 + [近/远] — distance-from construction with 离
要 + [time/stations] — expressing duration or cost
虽然...但是... — concessive structure with real-world consequence
Parallel person-by-person structure: useful template for comparative business writing

essays.title: 怎么去北京医院 (Giving Directions to the Beijing Hospital)
essays.text: 怎么去北京医院？可以坐5路公共汽车，在北京医院站下车就行了，要坐一个多小时。公共汽车站很近，往东走一百多米，左转就到了。你也可以坐地铁，坐地铁比坐公共汽车快，在北京站下车，半个多小时就到了。地铁站要一直往西走，十多分钟就到了。
essays.summary: A speaker explains two routes to Beijing Hospital — by bus and by subway — including walking directions to each stop and estimated travel time.
essays.why_study: A reusable template for giving multi-option directions in Chinese. The passage models the 往 + direction + 走 + distance + turn + 就到了 template, the comparison 比...快, and the quantifier 多 (over/approximately) attached to distances and times. Professional contexts (meeting someone, explaining venue location) frequently require exactly this type of multi-option routing explanation.
key_devices: 往[direction]走[distance]，[turn]，就到了 — complete directions template
一百多米 / 半个多小时 — 多 as approximator (just over / more than)
坐地铁比坐公共汽车快 — direct comparison with 比
就 — immediacy marker (right away, as soon as you...)
一直 往西走 — 一直 for straight-ahead navigation

grammar.explanation: 着 attached to a verb indicates a state resulting from an action that is still in progress (not the action itself). Commonly used to describe what people are wearing or how they are positioned. Distinguished from 在/正在 which describe an active ongoing action.
examples: 站着的那位 (the one standing [over there])
他穿着西装，打着领带 (he is wearing a suit and tie)
戴着白色的眼镜 (wearing white glasses)

grammar.explanation: Standard concessive conjunction pair. 虽然 introduces the conceded fact; 但是 or 可是 introduces the contrasting reality. In informal speech 虽然 is often dropped, leaving just 但是. Elevated register: 尽管...但是...
examples: 虽然工作有点儿忙，但是我很喜欢这个工作。
地铁虽然很快，但是人太多了。

grammar.explanation: 过 after a verb marks that an experience has been had at some unspecified past time. Negated with 没(有)V过. Contrasts with 了 which marks completion of a specific past event. Key for talking about travel, education, and life experience.
examples: 我去过美国和日本。(I have been to the US and Japan.)
你学过汉语吗？(Have you ever studied Chinese?)
我吃过中国菜。(I have eaten Chinese food before.)

grammar.pattern: 是...的 (focus/cleft construction for past events)
grammar.explanation: Used to focus attention on how, when, where, or with whom a past action took place — not whether it happened. The action is presupposed; 是...的 spotlights the circumstance. Extremely common in business Chinese to clarify details.
examples: 我今天是走路来上班的。(I walked to work today — emphasizing mode of transport)
两点去的。(I went at two — emphasizing the unexpected time)
是chuānzhe hēisè kùzi的那位吗？(Is it the one wearing black trousers?)

grammar.explanation: 有点儿 precedes adjectives to express a mild degree, typically with a slight negative or complaint nuance. Contrasts with 挺...的 (quite — positive) and 非常 (very — neutral/strong). Use 有点儿 when the speaker perceives the degree as inconvenient or unfortunate.
examples: 我有点儿累。(I'm a bit tired.)
工作有点儿忙。(Work is a bit hectic.)
有点儿发烧。(A slight fever.)

grammar.pattern: 挺 + adj + 的 (quite/pretty — colloquial positive)
grammar.explanation: 挺...的 expresses a fairly high but not extreme degree, usually with positive or neutral connotation. The final 的 is characteristic of this construction and softens/personalizes the statement. More casual than 非常; warmer than just 很.
examples: 我挺喜欢这个工作的。(I quite like this job.)
这儿挺舒服的。(It's quite comfortable here.)

grammar.pattern: 每 + [time unit] + 都 + V (every [unit], all/always V)
grammar.explanation: 每 (every/each) pairs with 都 (all) to express habitual or universal action. The 都 is nearly obligatory and cannot usually be omitted. Extended: 什么...都... (everything...) and 哪儿...都... (everywhere...).
examples: 我每天都去跑步。(I run every day.)
我什么菜都喜欢。(I like all kinds of food.)
她每天上班前和下班后都去跑步。(She runs both before and after work every day.)

grammar.pattern: A 比 B + adj (A is more [adj] than B)
grammar.explanation: Standard comparison structure. The adjective stands alone — no 更 is needed (though 更 can be added for emphasis). To say 'A is much more X than B': A 比 B + adj + 多了/得多. Negated: A 没有 B + adj (A is not as X as B).
examples: 昨天比今天热。(Yesterday was hotter than today.)
坐地铁比坐公共汽车快。(The subway is faster than the bus.)
北京下雨的天气温度比晴天低。(Beijing's rainy-day temperature is lower than sunny days.)

grammar.pattern: A 没有 B + adj (A is not as [adj] as B)
grammar.explanation: Negative comparison. A 没有 B + adj means 'A does not reach B's level of [adj]'. This is the negative form of the 比 construction and is interchangeable in meaning with B 比 A + adj. Often sounds more natural in spoken Chinese when emphasizing that something falls short.
examples: 温度没有今天高。(The temperature won't be as high as today's.)
今天没有昨天热。(Today is not as hot as yesterday.)

grammar.explanation: 要 as a predicate expressing the cost or duration required. Extremely common for discussing travel time, process time, and quantities. Distinct from modal 要 (want to / will).
examples: 坐公共汽车要多长时间？(How long does the bus take?)
走路要十分钟。(It takes ten minutes on foot.)
要坐五站。(Five stops to go.)

grammar.explanation: Sequence-of-steps structure. 先 (first), 然后 (then/after that), 再 (then again/next). Use for ordered instructions, itineraries, and procedures. 先 is typically tied to the subject; 然后/再 can start new clauses or sentences. Essential for business planning language.
examples: 我先去看医生，然后再上班。(I'll go to the doctor first, then come to work.)
我先送您去宾馆，中午请您吃饭，下午开会。(I'll take you to the hotel first, then lunch at noon, then a meeting in the afternoon.)

grammar.pattern: 正在 / 在 + V (progressive: currently doing)
grammar.explanation: 正在 (or just 在) before a verb marks an ongoing action at the moment of reference. Often paired with 呢 at the end. In business calls, 正在开会 is the standard phrase for 'in a meeting'. 刚才 + 在 + V signals an action that was in progress at a slightly earlier moment.
examples: 他正在开会。(He is currently in a meeting.)
刚才我在开会。(I was in a meeting just now.)
李小姐正在唱歌。(Miss Li is singing.)

grammar.pattern: 让 causative: [A] 让 [B] V (A asks/makes/lets B do V)
grammar.explanation: 让 expresses permission, instruction, or causation. Reading depends on context: let (permission), ask/tell (instruction), make (causation). In workplace Chinese, the pattern is extremely common for reporting instructions received or given. Similar to 叫 but more standard.
examples: 医生让我多休息。(The doctor told me to get more rest.)
高飞让我买咖啡。(Gao Fei asked me to buy coffee.)
李经理让我来接您。(Manager Li asked me to come and pick you up.)

grammar.explanation: Conditional polite request structure commonly used in business Chinese to make open-ended offers of assistance. The 如果 clause sets the condition; 请 + V delivers the polite instruction. This pattern is a standard closing formula in Chinese business correspondence and speech.
examples: 如果您有什么需要，请告诉我。(If there is anything you need, please let me know.)
如果您想去走走，我可以送您去。(If you'd like to take a walk, I can take you.)

grammar.pattern: 快 + [adj/V] + 了 (about to / on the verge of)
grammar.explanation: 快...了 expresses imminent change of state — something is about to happen or a situation is approaching its endpoint. Distinguished from 要...了 which can also be used, but 快...了 implies greater immediacy and is slightly more colloquial.
examples: 我快好了。(I'm almost better now.)
飞机快到了。(The plane is about to arrive.)

grammar.pattern: V + 错 (resultative complement: did V wrongly)
grammar.explanation: 错 as a resultative complement indicates the action produced an erroneous result. Covers a wide range: mishearing (听错), misspeaking (说错), miswriting (写错), misbuying (买错), misdialing (打错). Essential for professional apology and correction language.
examples: 对不起，我写错了。(Sorry, I wrote it down wrong.)
我听错了，我的意思是… (I misheard; what I meant was…)
我买错了，买了茶。(I bought the wrong thing — I got tea instead.)

grammar.pattern: 往/向 + direction + 走 + distance，然后 [turn]，就到了
grammar.explanation: Standard Chinese template for giving walking directions. 往/向 (toward) + compass direction + 走 (walk) + distance, followed by turning instruction, concluded with 就到了 (and you'll be there). 就 signals immediate arrival upon completion of the action.
examples: 您往东走三百米，就到了。(Walk three hundred meters eastward and you'll be there.)
往西走两百米，向左转，就到了。(Walk two hundred meters west, turn left, and you're there.)
一直往西走，十多分钟就到了。(Walk straight west and you'll be there in about ten minutes.)

expr.meaning: to go on a business trip
to hold / attend a meeting
schedule; itinerary
message (n.); to leave a message (v.)
currently in a meeting (standard phone deflection phrase)

expr.pinyin: qǐng gěi wǒ huí gè diànhuà
expr.meaning: please call me back

expr.pinyin: bù néng hé nín jiànmiàn / kāihuì
expr.meaning: unable to meet with you / unable to hold the meeting (formal regret formula)
booked a room for you (hospitality phrase)
to treat you to a meal; to host you for lunch/dinner
to go to the airport to pick you up
to take/drive you to [place]; to see you off to
to complete formalities; to check in (hotel/office)

expr.zh: 如果您有什么需要，请告诉我，别客气
expr.pinyin: rúguǒ nín yǒu shénme xūyào, qǐng gàosu wǒ, bié kèqi
expr.meaning: If there is anything you need, please don't hesitate to let me know (standard hospitality closing)
don't be polite / you're welcome / please don't stand on ceremony
What do you think of…? Does that work for you? (polite confirmation-seeking tag)
business center (building)
business room (hotel room class)
I truly appreciate it; I'm really grateful (warm, emphatic thanks)
thank you very much; I greatly appreciate it (formal thanks)
I'm truly sorry (stronger, more formal than 不好意思)
sorry / I'm embarrassed / excuse me (mild, versatile apology for minor inconvenience)
excuse me (when asking a favor or asking someone to move); more formal than 请问
no problem; never mind; it doesn't matter

expr.zh: 对不起，我说错了，我的意思是…
expr.pinyin: duìbuqǐ, wǒ shuō cuò le, wǒ de yìsi shì…
expr.meaning: I'm sorry, I misspoke; what I meant was… (self-correction formula)
I am [Name] from [Company] — standard self-identification formula for phone calls and messages
not feeling well; feeling unwell (softer than 生病; common in workplace leave requests)
you should get more rest (standard caring advice / doctor's instruction formula)
long time no see (set greeting for reconnecting with acquaintances)
I see / I've got it / understood (acknowledging receipt of information; 了 marks newly acquired knowledge)
commerce/business (general, adj.: 商业中心 commerce center) vs. business affairs/commercial (formal: 商务签证 business visa, 商务舱 business class)
expr.register: business standard — note register distinction between the two

expr.meaning: What happened? / What's wrong? / How come? (inquiring about a changed state)
after work; after clocking off
straight ahead; all along; continuously
and you'll be right there; and you'll arrive immediately (direction closure phrase)
recently; lately (time reference for recent period, not 'nearest location')

takeaways: BUSINESS MESSAGE FORMULA: Chinese professional messages follow a tight five-beat structure — [greeting + company + name] → [situation/reason] → [impact on plans] → [proposed alternative or request] → [polite close + thanks]. Apply this to emails, WeChat messages, and voice messages alike.

CITE AUTHORITY TO SOFTEN REQUESTS: Rather than saying 我想休息 (I want to rest), say 医生让我多休息 (the doctor told me to rest more). Citing a third-party authority — a doctor, a policy, a superior — makes a request feel less personal and more professionally grounded in Chinese business culture.

HOSPITALITY CLOSING FORMULA: 如果您有什么需要，请告诉我，别客气 is a stock phrase that closes nearly every Chinese business welcome/reception context. Memorize and deploy it whenever hosting a visitor, ending a meeting arrangement, or closing a service interaction.

APOLOGY REGISTER LADDER: 不好意思 (mild — for small awkwardnesses) < 对不起 (standard apology) < 真抱歉 (sincere, formal regret) < 非常抱歉 (strong formal apology). Match the level to the severity of the situation. In business, overusing 对不起 can sound either sycophantic or alarmist; 真抱歉 or 非常抱歉 is appropriate for failing to keep a commitment.

PHONE DEFLECTION: 他正在开会 (he's in a meeting) is the single most common reason given in Chinese for why someone can't come to the phone. The follow-up pattern is: 要给他留言吗？/ 您要留言吗？(Would you like to leave a message?). Knowing this sequence from both sides is essential for business phone culture.

SELF-CORRECTION MID-SPEECH: 对不起，我说错了，我的意思是… is the clean formula for correcting a verbal error in Chinese. The V+错 resultative complement (听错/说错/写错/买错) covers the full range of common mistake types and is indispensable for graceful self-correction in meetings and calls.

COMPARISON STRUCTURES — TWO PATTERNS FOR ONE IDEA: Chinese has two standard comparison patterns. Use 比 (A 比 B + adj) to assert A exceeds B; use 没有 (A 没有 B + adj) to assert A falls short of B. Both appear constantly in business Chinese (comparing prices, schedules, speeds, options). Neither uses 更 in the basic form.

SEQUENCE LANGUAGE FOR AGENDAS AND PROCEDURES: 先...然后...再... is the backbone of presenting itineraries, instructions, and processes. It maps directly onto numbered steps in English (first / then / then). Professional contexts — presenting a visit schedule, explaining a procedure, walking through an agenda — all require fluent use of this chain.

DIRECTIONS TEMPLATE: Chinese directions follow a rigid template: 往/向[方向]走[距离]，然后[左/右]转，就到了. Knowing the four cardinal directions (东南西北) and terms 左转/右转/一直走 makes it possible to give or receive any navigation instruction in Chinese.

REGISTER NOTE — 您 VS. 你: Even with one's own manager, the textbook consistently uses 您 in formal/phone contexts. Chinese business culture maintains the 您 register in written messages, phone calls, and first-time meetings, even among colleagues with an established relationship. Reserve 你 for peer-level informal speech.

essays.title: 自我介绍——接管人事部（幸会·短文）
essays.text: 大家好，幸会！我想简单地介绍一下自己。我在市场部做经理，从2000年开始管理市场部，一直到今年，工作了十五年。从今天开始，将由我来负责人事部，我将和大家一起工作，这对我来说是一个新的开始。我对人事部的工作还不是很了解，也不太懂人事部的管理，希望各位能给我更多的帮助，谢谢大家！
essays.summary: A new department head delivers a brief self-introduction to their new team, establishing fifteen years of Marketing experience before announcing their move to lead HR, and closing with a humble request for support.
essays.why_study: A compact, authentic template for a Chinese workplace self-introduction when changing roles. Covers all required moves: background, tenure, new assignment framed as 新的开始, honest admission of knowledge gap, and collegial request for help. Every sentence is imitable.
key_devices: 从…开始…一直到…工作了X年 (establishing tenure arc)
从今天开始，将由我来负责… (formal delegation / role-assumption formula)
这对我来说是一个新的开始 (reframing a challenge as opportunity)
我对…还不是很了解，也不太懂… (humble dual-admission of unfamiliarity)
希望各位能给我更多的帮助 (collegial closing request)

essays.title: 销售经验访谈（为了健康·对话二）
essays.text:
王经理，你们公司的产品卖得很好。请问，你们的销售经验是什么？
在我看来，产品的质量一定要好。客户喜欢，才会选择我们的产品。
简单地说，产品的质量是最重要的，对吗？
对，让客户满意的质量是最重要的。
好的，我知道了，谢谢您！
essays.summary: A visitor asks a successful manager to share their sales experience. The manager explains that product quality that satisfies customers is the decisive factor, using 在我看来 to frame the opinion and 才 to express conditionality.
essays.why_study: Shows how to give a concise, confident business opinion: open with 在我看来, state the logic with 才, let your interlocutor paraphrase with 简单地说…，对吗？, then confirm with amplification. The closing formula 让客户满意的质量是最重要的 is a well-formed business maxim worth memorising.
key_devices: 在我看来 (personal-opinion opener)
才 conditional (X才会Y: only if X, then Y)
简单地说 (discourse marker for summarising the interlocutor's point)
让客户满意 (causative result phrase as nominalised subject)

essays.title: 竞争对手的销售经验（为了健康·短文）
essays.text: 我们公司和李经理公司的主要产品都是照相机。今年，他们公司的产品卖得比我们公司好。李经理向我们介绍了一些经验：第一，产品的质量和价格非常重要，产品质量好，价格便宜，客户才会喜欢。第二，一个好的产品介绍是做好销售的关键。第三，客户服务一定要做好。我认为，这些建议对我们的帮助很大。
essays.summary: A narrator reports on lessons learned from a competitor (Manager Li) whose camera products outsold theirs. Three enumerated takeaways cover quality-price balance, product presentation, and customer service, ending with a personal endorsement.
essays.why_study: A model for writing a brief competitive analysis or post-meeting summary in Chinese. The 第一…第二…第三 enumeration combined with an 我认为 closing assessment is a reusable skeleton for any business report or recommendations memo.
key_devices: 卖得比…好 (comparative selling performance)
向我们介绍了一些经验 (framing knowledge transfer from a senior party)
第一……第二……第三 enumeration (standard business rhetorical structure)
才会 conditional (quality + price → only then customers like it)
我认为，这些建议对我们的帮助很大 (closing personal endorsement)

essays.title: 春节公司聚会（节日聚会·短文）
essays.text: 对中国人来说，春节是最重要的节日。每年春节前，公司都会办一个很大的聚会，请所有部门的员工参加。下周二就是今年的春节，所以今天CTI公司有一个聚会。除了各部门的经理和员工，一些外国客户也来了。聚会上有很多中国有名的小吃，还有很多种茶和其他好喝的饮料。外国客户都很喜欢，公司的员工也都聊得很高兴。
essays.summary: A short description of how CTI Company organises a large Spring Festival gathering that includes all departments plus foreign clients, featuring famous Chinese snacks and teas, with everyone enjoying themselves.
essays.why_study: A reusable template for describing Chinese company cultural events. Opens with a cultural context sentence (对中国人来说), establishes a habitual annual practice (每年…都会…), uses 除了…也 to widen scope to include clients, and closes with a parallel satisfaction statement for both client and staff groups.
key_devices: 对中国人来说 (cultural framing opener)
每年…都会… (habitual annual event structure)
除了…也/还 (additive inclusion pattern)
聊得很高兴 (manner result complement expressing satisfactory outcome)

essays.title: 忙碌的一天——秘书的叙述（他出去了·短文）
essays.text: 我是CTI公司的秘书，我很喜欢这份工作，但是有时候工作有点儿多。上午九点，钱经理让我上去开会。开完会后，我又出去了一趟，给车加了油。刚回来不久，广告部的小王下来找我，让我打印几份报告。刚做完，又有一位姓周的先生过来找钱经理。钱经理正在做报告，我就进去告诉了他。过了一会儿，钱经理做完报告出来，和周先生谈了半个小时才谈完。终于可以下班了！
essays.summary: A secretary narrates a hectic day: attending a meeting upstairs, refuelling the car, printing reports for the Advertising Department, and managing a walk-in visitor who needed the manager — all before finally getting off work.
essays.why_study: The densest model text in this chunk for directional complements and sequential narrative. A chain of 上去/出去/下来/进去/出来 compounds creates vivid office movement. 刚…又 back-to-back events convey relentlessness. 才谈完 marks extended duration before completion. 终于 delivers the payoff.
key_devices: directional complement chain: 上去 → 出去 → 下来 → 进去 → 出来
刚…不久，又 (barely…then again — conveying a hectic pace)
V+完 as a temporal pivot (开完会后, 做完报告出来)
才谈完 (才 marking extended effort before completion)
终于可以下班了 (climactic resolution with 终于)

essays.title: 把字句叙事——周日的工作（请把门打开·短文）
essays.text: 今天是星期天，我没有去上班。上午，我在家先把门修理好了，然后去了一趟超市，把明天需要的水果买了。回来后，我把东西放在桌上，就去做饭了。吃完饭后，我把写给小李的信寄了出去。晚饭前，我把张经理需要的文件发到了他的电子邮箱，然后把明天开会要的报告读完了。今天虽然不上班，但是我还是觉得很累。
essays.summary: A first-person Sunday account where the narrator completes a chain of domestic and work tasks — repairing a door, shopping, cooking, mailing a letter, emailing documents, reading a report — all rendered with 把 constructions, closing with a 虽然…但是 concession.
essays.why_study: Five consecutive 把-sentences in a short passage make this the best imitation model in this chunk for task-completion narrative. Every 把 is followed by a result complement or direction complement, demonstrating the full range of the construction. The 虽然…但是…还是 close is a clean concessive wrap-up pattern.
key_devices: 把+Object+V+Result/Direction complement (×5 instances in sequence)
先…然后 (sequential steps)
吃完饭后 (V+完 temporal pivot)
虽然…但是…还是 (concessive close: 'even though…still…')

grammar.explanation: The 把 construction foregrounds the object and emphasises what happens to it — completion, placement, or directed action. In business writing it conveys that a task has been fully handled. The object must be specific and definite. Result complements include 好 (properly), 完 (finished), 清楚 (clearly), 出去 (out), etc.
examples: 请把门打开。(Please open the door.)
我们得把这份文件写好。(We have to write this document properly.)
今天一定要把问题的原因讲清楚。(Today we must clarify the reasons for the problem.)
我把写给小李的信寄了出去。(I sent out the letter I had written to Xiao Li.)
我把张经理需要的文件发到了他的电子邮箱。(I sent the documents Manager Zhang needed to his email.)

grammar.explanation: Introduces a perspective or standpoint: 'for / to [person or group]…'. A neutral and polite way to qualify statements from a specific angle — indispensable in business presentations, reports, and introductions.
examples: 对中国人来说，春节是最重要的节日。(For Chinese people, Spring Festival is the most important holiday.)
对我来说，汉语太难了。(For me, Chinese is too difficult.)
这对我来说是一个新的开始。(This is a new beginning for me.)
但是这份工作对我来说非常重要。(But this job is very important to me.)

grammar.explanation: Introduces a subjective opinion: 'in [person]'s view…'. More emphatic and personal than 对…来说 — it positions the speaker as offering their own interpretation of a situation rather than noting how something affects them.
examples: 在我看来，产品的质量一定要好。(In my view, product quality must be high.)
在我看来，最好的方法是和中国人聊聊天儿。(In my opinion, the best method is to chat with Chinese people.)

grammar.explanation: 'Speaking for / as for [topic or person]'. Used to narrow the frame of reference to a specific domain or individual, often to contrast personal experience against a general claim.
examples: 就我来说，学汉语很容易。(Speaking for myself, learning Chinese is easy.)

grammar.explanation: 才 between two clauses means 'only then / only if'. The second action is conditional on or delayed by the first. This is the key structure for expressing logical prerequisites in business argument: 'Customers will only choose us if quality is high.'
examples: 客户喜欢，才会选择我们的产品。(Only if customers like it will they choose our products.)
听医生的话，身体才能好得快。(Only by following the doctor's advice will you recover quickly.)
和周先生谈了半个小时才谈完。(It took half an hour of talking with Mr Zhou before they finished.)
产品质量好，价格便宜，客户才会喜欢。(Only when quality is good and price is low will customers like it.)

grammar.explanation: Marks the starting point of an ongoing action or state: 'starting from…'. Can be extended with 一直到 to close the arc. Used in introductions, timelines, and business handovers.
examples: 从2000年开始管理市场部，一直到今年，工作了十五年。(I managed the Marketing Department from 2000 until this year — fifteen years.)
从今天开始，将由我来负责人事部。(Starting today, I will be responsible for the HR Department.)

grammar.explanation: 'To be handled / managed by [person]'. A formal delegation structure — near-passive with an explicit agent. Used in announcements, contracts, and formal memos.
examples: 将由我来负责人事部。(The HR Department will be managed by me.)
这个部门由李经理负责。(This department is managed by Manager Li.)
高先生的日程由周秘书安排。(Mr Gao's schedule is arranged by Secretary Zhou.)

grammar.explanation: 'In addition to X, also Y'. Extends a list without discarding what has already been mentioned. Very common in business writing to broaden scope inclusively.
examples: 除了各部门的经理和员工，一些外国客户也来了。(In addition to managers and staff from every department, some foreign clients also came.)
除了和中国人聊天儿，你还有别的好方法吗？(Besides chatting with Chinese people, do you have any other good methods?)

grammar.explanation: Attached to a verb, 完 signals the action has been completed to its natural end. Frequently used as a temporal pivot in narratives: V+完+后/了 → next action.
examples: 开完会后，我又出去了一趟。(After finishing the meeting, I went out again.)
钱经理做完报告出来，和周先生谈了半个小时才谈完。(After Manager Qian finished the report, he and Mr Zhou talked for half an hour before finishing.)
把明天开会要的报告读完了。(I finished reading the report needed for tomorrow's meeting.)

grammar.pattern: Directional complements: V + 上/下/进/出/过 + 来/去 (+ 一趟)
grammar.explanation: Compound directional complements encode movement relative to the speaker or a reference point. 来 = toward speaker/reference; 去 = away. 一趟 ('one trip') quantifies a single errand. Essential for office and building-movement language.
examples: 钱经理让我上去开会。(Manager Qian told me to go upstairs for a meeting.)
广告部的小王下来找我。(Xiao Wang from Advertising came down to find me.)
我就进去告诉了他。(I went in and told him.)
你过来一趟取一下文件夹，好吗？(Could you come over once to pick up the folder?)
终于可以下班了。(Finally I could leave work / go downstairs.)

grammar.explanation: 'Just barely [did X], then [Y] again'. 刚 signals recency; 又 or 就 signals an immediate sequel. Creates a sense of relentless busyness or rapid-fire succession of events.
examples: 刚回来不久，广告部的小王下来找我。(I had barely returned when Xiao Wang from Advertising came down.)
刚做完，又有一位姓周的先生过来找钱经理。(I had just finished when another person came to see Manager Qian.)

grammar.explanation: Concessive structure: 'although X, (still) Y'. Adding 还是 in the result clause stresses the unexpected persistence of the consequence despite the concession. Common in narrative closings and self-assessments.
examples: 虽然今天很累，但是我认识了不少同事。(Although I am tired today, I have gotten to know many colleagues.)
今天虽然不上班，但是我还是觉得很累。(Although I didn't work today, I still feel very tired.)

grammar.explanation: 'For the sake of / in order to [purpose]'. Opens a clause stating a goal or motivation. Used in declarations, slogans, rationales for action, and business justifications.
examples: 为了健康，我明天就不吸了！(For my health, I'll quit starting tomorrow!)
为了学好汉语，我也试一下这个方法。(In order to learn Chinese well, I'll try this method too.)

grammar.pattern: 对 + N + 有帮助 / 不好 / 重要 / 满意
grammar.explanation: 'Good / bad / helpful / important / satisfying for [noun]'. Uses 对 as a preposition of relevance, benefit, or harm. Very common in health, product quality, and business-outcome contexts.
examples: 这个习惯对身体不好。(This habit is bad for your health.)
这些建议对我们的帮助很大。(These suggestions are very helpful to us.)
让客户满意的质量是最重要的。(The quality that satisfies customers is most important.)

expr.meaning: What an honour to meet you / delighted to make your acquaintance — more formal and literary than 认识你很高兴; used at first introductions in business or formal social settings
Keep in touch when you have time — a polite closing line after exchanging business cards or at the end of a first meeting
I've often heard [person] mention you — a warm, face-giving opener when meeting someone you've heard about indirectly
Sorry to have kept you waiting (formal) — the standard apology formula for lateness or delays in business settings; 您 signals formality
Excuse me / I'm sorry / how embarrassing — a versatile softener and apology for minor impositions or errors
To negotiate; negotiation — the standard business term for formal negotiations or talks
The schedule is full / packed — describes a booked-out calendar; 日程 alone means 'schedule / itinerary'
To share experience / insights with someone — formal way to frame knowledge transfer from one party to another
In my opinion / in my view — signals a personal assessment; slightly more deliberate and emphatic than 我觉得
For / to [person or group] — frames a statement from a specific perspective without asserting it as universally true
As for me / speaking for myself — narrows scope to personal experience; often used to offer a contrasting example
To satisfy customers / make customers satisfied — a core customer-service phrase, also usable as a nominalized subject: 让客户满意的质量…
To solve a problem / resolve an issue — the most common business action phrase for problem-handling
Something urgent came up — the standard explanation for a sudden departure or unexpected unavailability
To leave early; to depart ahead of schedule
Finally can… — expresses relief after sustained effort; a narrative climax / resolution marker
A problem arose / something went wrong — 出 here means 'to emerge / arise'; 出 alone can mean 'to happen' in this sense
To report to [someone]; to brief sb. — formal upward communication, both as verb and noun (报告 = a report / briefing)
Product quality — a fundamental business collocation appearing in sales, procurement, and contract contexts
Customer service — standard business term for the after-sales or support function
To remind; to give a heads-up — used in scheduling, deadline management, and task follow-up

expr.zh: 别担心，……怎么要求我们怎么做
expr.pinyin: bié dānxīn, ... zěnme yāoqiú wǒmen zěnme zuò
expr.meaning: Don't worry, we'll do whatever [the client] asks — a reassurance formula that echoes the client's own verb (要求 → 做) for emphasis
Requirements / demands (n.) or to demand / request (v.) — key in contract, client-management, and negotiation language
Step by step; gradual and orderly progress — a 四字成语 used in educational, strategic, and planning contexts
Each / every department — standard collective reference in company-wide communications and announcements
All employees / all staff — formal collective noun phrase used in notices, announcements, and HR communications
To go on a business trip — one of the highest-frequency business Chinese collocations
Comparatively / fairly / relatively (adv.) — a moderating adverb softer than 很; also means 'to compare'. 25箱比较好 = '25 cartons would be better / more appropriate'
Suddenly; unexpectedly — used to introduce unplanned events in narrative; 突然有急事 is a fixed phrase
Simply put / to put it simply — a discourse marker for summarising or reducing an argument to its core claim
All the way until; right up to — marks an unbroken duration from a starting point to an endpoint
Will be handled / managed by [person] — formal delegation formula: 将 (future) + 由 (by/agent) + 来负责 (take charge)
Product quality and price — the canonical pairing in BCT-level sales and marketing discourse
I think / I believe / in my assessment — slightly more formal and assertive than 我觉得; appropriate in written reports

takeaways: Role-handover speech formula: 从今天开始，将由我来负责[新部门]，我将和大家一起工作，这对我来说是一个新的开始 is a polished, reusable template when taking over a team or role. Pair it with a humble dual-admission (我对…还不是很了解，也不太懂…的管理) and a request for collegial help (希望各位能给我更多的帮助) to build goodwill without losing face.

Establish credibility through tenure: 从X年开始…一直到今年，工作了Y年 is a concise formula for anchoring your experience to a timeline. The progression from start point (从…开始) through unbroken duration (一直到) to total years (工作了Y年) accomplishes in one sentence what English often takes a paragraph for.

Enumerate business advice in threes: 第一……，第二……，第三…… signals organisational clarity to Chinese business audiences. Always open with the most concrete item and close with 我认为，这些建议对我们的帮助很大 or a similar endorsement. Never leave the list without a personal sign-off.

Use 在我看来 / 对…来说 / 就我来说 for perspective — rotate them strategically: 在我看来 is most assertive (your own reading of the situation); 对我来说 frames personal impact or relevance; 就我来说 speaks from personal experience in contrast to general claims. All three soften assertions without erasing them.

Use 才 for business logic conditionals: X，才会Y means 'only if X, then Y' — stronger and more logical than simply stating Y. In a competitive-analysis passage, 产品质量好，价格便宜，客户才会喜欢 makes the causal chain explicit and persuasive rather than implied.

把-sentence density signals task completion: when writing a task-completion narrative (what I accomplished today, a project summary), load 把 constructions to foreground the successful handling of each item. Five 把-sentences in six sentences, as in the Lesson 5 passage, is stylistically appropriate for a to-do-list diary or work log.

Chain directional complements for office movement: 上去/下来/进去/出来/过来 attached to verbs create vivid, idiomatic workplace narrative. 广告部的小王下来找我 is more natural than 小王从楼上来找我. These compounds are small but conspicuous markers of fluency in Chinese office writing.

刚……（不久），又…… creates a 'barely caught my breath' effect: this back-to-back structure is the Chinese equivalent of 'no sooner had I X than Y'. It efficiently conveys a hectic sequence without needing transition sentences — critical for professional narrative that must be both dense and readable.

终于 as a narrative payoff: place 终于可以……了 at the end of a completed task sequence to mark resolution or relief. It functions like 'at long last' or 'finally' — one adverb carries the emotional weight of everything that preceded it. Use it sparingly for maximum impact.

Cultural opener for company event writing: begin with 对中国人来说，[节日]是最重要的节日 when writing about a seasonal company event. This shows cultural awareness, immediately grounds the text in shared context, and makes the subsequent event description feel purposeful rather than incidental.

虽然……但是……还是 as a closing concessive: this structure is the standard pattern for acknowledging a trade-off at the end of a passage. Adding 还是 in the result clause stresses that the outcome persists despite the concession — 今天虽然不上班，但是我还是觉得很累 is more nuanced than simply saying 我很累.

grammar.pattern: 有一点 (yǒu yīdiǎn) vs. 一点 (yīdiǎn)
grammar.explanation: 有一点 precedes an adjective to express a slight — often mildly undesirable — degree: 有一点 + adj. 一点 follows an adjective or verb in comparisons or polite requests to indicate a small degree: adj + 一点 (+ 吗/好吗). Key note from this textbook: reduplicated adjectives (如 干干净净) cannot be modified by degree adverbs including 有一点.
examples: 这个房间有一点小。— This room is a little small. (mild complaint)
能不能便宜一点？— Could it be a little cheaper? (polite request)
这条路比那条近一点。— This road is a bit closer than that one. (comparison)

grammar.explanation: A + 比 + B + adj (+ differential): A is more adj than B. The degree of difference (e.g., 多了、一些、得多、100块) follows the adjective. Equality: A 跟 B 一样 + adj. Negative comparison: A + 没有 + B + adj.
examples: 北京的房租比上海高一些。— Beijing rents are somewhat higher than Shanghai's.
坐地铁比打车便宜得多。— Taking the subway is much cheaper than a taxi.
这家银行的利息跟那家一样高。— This bank's interest rate is as high as that one's.

grammar.explanation: Three key uses: (1) 如果/要是…就: if…then consequence; (2) immediate or early action (speaker evaluates as early or quick — opposite of 才); (3) 'just/only' limiting scope. Context disambiguates. Note: 就 = speaker judges timing as early; 才 = speaker judges timing as late.
examples: 如果你需要帮助，就告诉我。— If you need help, just tell me.
他八点就到了。— He arrived as early as eight o'clock.
我就想喝杯水。— I just want a glass of water.

grammar.pattern: 才 (cái) — not until / only then / merely
grammar.explanation: 才 indicates an action occurs later than expected or only under a specific condition. With quantities, 才 means 'only' (less than expected). Contrasts with 就 (early) in evaluative meaning. Cannot use 了 directly after 才 with timing sense.
examples: 他十点才回来。— He didn't come back until ten o'clock.
我才有五十块钱。— I only have fifty yuan (less than enough).
学好汉语才能在中国做生意。— Only by mastering Chinese can you do business in China.

grammar.pattern: 了 (le) — completion and change of state
grammar.explanation: Verb + 了: marks completed action. Sentence-final 了: marks a change of state or newly relevant situation. Negation of completed action: 没(有) + verb (no 了). 还没(有) + verb = not yet completed.
examples: 我已经办完手续了。— I have already completed the procedures.
他走了。— He's gone now. (change of state)
我还没存款，等一会儿再去。— I haven't deposited yet; I'll go later.

grammar.pattern: 着 (zhe) — ongoing state / manner of action
grammar.explanation: Verb + 着: indicates a continuing state or describes the manner in which another action is performed. V着V着 pattern: while continuously doing V, an unexpected result occurs. Contrasts with 在 + V (active ongoing process).
examples: 门开着，你进来吧。— The door is open; come on in.
他笑着跟客户握手。— He shook hands with the client while smiling.
他想着想着就想出了办法。— As he kept thinking, a solution occurred to him.

grammar.pattern: 因为…所以 / 由于…因此 (yīnwèi…suǒyǐ / yóuyú…yīncǐ) — cause and effect
grammar.explanation: 因为 introduces a cause; 所以 introduces the result. Either connector can stand alone. Formal/written register prefers 由于…因此 or 由于…所以. 因此 alone is very common in business reports and formal expositions.
examples: 因为银行今天关门，所以我明天再来。— Because the bank is closed today, I'll come back tomorrow.
由于经济形势不稳定，公司推迟了投资计划。— Due to economic instability, the company postponed its investment plan.
利率上升，因此很多企业减少了贷款。— Interest rates rose; therefore many companies reduced borrowing.

grammar.explanation: Two distinct meanings distinguished by the following conjunction: (1) Inclusive — 除了A以外，还/也 + B → besides A, also B (adds to A). (2) Exclusive — 除了A以外，只/都 + B → except for A, only/all B (excludes A). The presence of 还/也 signals inclusive; absence or 只 signals exclusive.
examples: 除了银行卡以外，他还带了现金。— Besides his bank card, he also brought cash. (inclusive)
除了他以外，大家都签了合同。— Except for him, everyone signed the contract. (exclusive)
除了价格以外，质量也是重要因素。— Besides price, quality is also an important factor.

grammar.explanation: 一 + V1, 就 + V2: the moment V1 happens, V2 immediately follows. Expresses tight temporal succession. Often used with two different subjects. Related: 只要…就 (as long as…then).
examples: 他一到公司就开始开会。— As soon as he arrived at the company, the meeting started.
超市一打折，顾客就特别多。— As soon as the supermarket has a sale, it's packed with customers.
只要质量好，价格稍高也没关系。— As long as the quality is good, a slightly higher price is fine.

grammar.explanation: 先 + V1, 再 + V2: complete V1 first, then proceed to V2 (V2 waits for V1). 先…然后 is slightly more narrative in tone. Standard structure for procedural descriptions, instructions, and step-by-step business communications.
examples: 请先填写申请表，再交给柜台人员。— Please fill in the application form first, then hand it to the counter staff.
先确认货物，然后再付款。— First confirm the goods, then make payment.
先存款，再查一下账户余额。— First make the deposit, then check the account balance.

grammar.explanation: 又 + adj1 + 又 + adj2: the subject has both qualities at the same time. The adjectives should be semantically compatible (both positive, both negative, or logically related). More colloquial than 既…又 (formal/written equivalent).
examples: 这款产品又实用又耐用。— This product is both practical and durable.
那家超市又近又便宜。— That supermarket is both close and cheap.
合同条款又详细又清楚。— The contract terms are both detailed and clear.

grammar.pattern: 越来越 (yuè lái yuè) — progressive change
grammar.explanation: 越来越 + adj/verb: the degree increases progressively over time. Cannot be modified by 更 or 很. Extended pattern: 越…越 (with two clauses) expresses 'the more…the more'.
examples: 网上购物越来越方便了。— Online shopping is becoming more and more convenient.
中国的电子商务越来越发达。— China's e-commerce is becoming increasingly developed.
价格越高，顾客越少买。— The higher the price, the fewer customers buy.

grammar.pattern: 快…了 / 就要…了 (kuài…le / jiù yào…le) — imminent future
grammar.explanation: 快/快要/就要 + verb/adj + 了: something is about to happen. The sentence-final 了 is essential. 就要 often pairs with a specific time reference; 快/快要 does not require one.
examples: 银行快关门了，我们快点！— The bank is about to close; let's hurry!
合同就要到期了，请及时续签。— The contract is about to expire; please renew in time.
他快回来了，再等一下吧。— He'll be back soon; wait a moment.

grammar.explanation: Subject + 被 + agent + verb + other elements. Agent can be omitted when unknown. In Chinese, 被 sentences typically describe undesirable or unintended events. For neutral processes, active voice is strongly preferred even where English would use passive.
examples: 我的信用卡被盗刷了。— My credit card was fraudulently charged.
货物在运输途中被损坏了。— The goods were damaged during transit.
原来的合同被取消了。— The original contract was cancelled.

grammar.pattern: 是…的 (shì…de) — emphasis on circumstances of past action
grammar.explanation: 是 + [time/place/manner/agent] + verb + 的: emphasizes when, where, how, or by whom a completed action was done. 是 can be omitted. The object can appear before 的 or after the verb. Used only for completed actions.
examples: 这份报告是昨天写的。— This report was written yesterday. (emphasis on time)
合同是在上海签的。— The contract was signed in Shanghai. (emphasis on place)
我们是坐高铁去的。— We went by high-speed rail. (emphasis on manner)

grammar.explanation: Subject + 把 + definite object + verb + complement/other element: the agent acts upon a known object with a resulting effect. Object must be definite (speaker and listener both know it). A result/complement after the verb is required. Cannot negate with 不 before 把 in most cases.
examples: 请把合同发给我们。— Please send us the contract.
他把钱存进银行了。— He deposited the money into the bank.
我把账单仔细检查了一遍。— I carefully checked through the bill once.

grammar.pattern: 把 + Object + V + 结果/趋向补语
grammar.explanation: The 把-construction moves the object before the verb to foreground what happens TO it. Required when the verb has a resultative or directional complement that specifies the object's fate or new location. The object must be specific/definite. Business contexts: 把合同签好 (sign the contract [to completion]), 把文件发过去 (send the documents [across]).
examples: 把合同签好
把资料整理好
把邮件发出去
把账算清楚

grammar.pattern: V + 得/不 + 结果补语 (可能补语 Potential Complement)
grammar.explanation: Insert 得 (possibility affirmed) or 不 (possibility denied) between a verb and its resultative complement to express whether an action can or cannot achieve its result. Extremely common in spoken and written business Chinese for hedging, promising, or refusing. 得 = can achieve result; 不 = cannot. Neither is negated with 不 externally.
examples: 这个项目来得及 (there's still time)
今天的任务完成不了 (today's task can't be finished)
合同签得好 (the contract can be signed well)
价格谈得下来 (the price can be negotiated down)

grammar.pattern: 结果补语: V + 好/完/到/住/开/懂/清楚
grammar.explanation: Resultative complements attach directly to verbs (no particle between them) and describe the result or completion of an action. 好 = well/done; 完 = finished; 到 = reached/achieved; 住 = fixed/retained; 开 = opened/separated; 懂 = understood; 清楚 = clearly. Essential for business writing precision.
examples: 写好报告
看完文件
达到目标
记住要点
想清楚再决定

grammar.pattern: 趋向补语: V + 来/去 (simple) or V + 上/下/进/出/回/过/起 + 来/去 (complex)
grammar.explanation: Directional complements indicate the direction of movement relative to the speaker. Complex forms express more nuanced meanings: 上来/上去 (upward), 下来/下去 (downward/continuing), 进来/进去 (inward), 出来/出去 (outward). 下来 can also signal a transition to a new state (e.g., 安静下来 = settle down).
examples: 把报告拿过来
走进会议室
想出来一个好办法
市场慢慢稳定下来

grammar.explanation: Frames an already-completed action to emphasize one of its circumstances: time, place, manner, or agent. The verb is bracketed by 是 and 的. In business contexts, often used to clarify the terms of a past agreement or event. 是 can be omitted in speech but not 的.
examples: 这份合同是昨天签的。
他是从上海来的。
产品是在本地生产的。
这个价格是我们特别优惠的。

grammar.pattern: 越...越... (The more X, the more Y)
grammar.explanation: Links two parallel changes: as one condition increases, so does another. Both clauses share the same subject or the second clause has its own. Very common in business analysis: 越卖越好 (the more it sells the better), 越早越好 (the sooner the better). Contrast with 越来越 + adj/V (increasingly...) which has only one clause.
examples: 价格越低，销量越好。
了解得越多，越有信心。
越来越多的客户选择网购。
这个问题越拖越难解决。

grammar.pattern: 才 vs 就: timing attitude particles
grammar.explanation: Both relate action B to a prior condition A, but encode opposite evaluations of timing. 才 = only then (speaker finds it late, slow, difficult, or conditional); 就 = right away / as early as (speaker finds it quick, easy, or surprising). Critical in business dialogue: 他才来 (he only just arrived — late!) vs 他就来了 (he'll be here right away).
examples: 等了两个小时他才来。(waited 2 hrs, only then came — late)
他一收到邮件就回复了。(the moment he got it, immediately replied)
价格谈了很久才决定。(took a long time to agree on price)
客户说明天就可以付款。(client says payment can happen as early as tomorrow)

grammar.explanation: Marks the subject as the receiver of an action. Agent is optional. In formal business Chinese, 被 passives frequently appear in reports and notices to describe outcomes (especially unwanted ones): 合同被取消 (the contract was cancelled). Also used neutrally in formal prose.
examples: 货物被损坏了。
合同被双方签署了。
项目被批准。
这个方案被否决了。

grammar.pattern: 只 vs 全 scope particles
grammar.explanation: 只 (zhǐ) restricts scope downward (only, merely); 全 (quán) expands scope upward (all, entirely). Both precede the verb. In formal registers, 仅 substitutes for 只. Contrast: 只有一个选择 (only one choice) vs 全部同意 (all agree).
examples: 我们只接受人民币付款。
全体员工参加培训。
仅供参考。
全年营业额增长了15%。

grammar.pattern: 量词短语 + 就/才 in quantitative judgments
grammar.explanation: Combining a specific quantity phrase with 就 (as little as / already) or 才 (only / no more than) encodes the speaker's evaluation of whether the quantity is large or small. 就 signals 'impressively small amount achieves result'; 才 signals 'disappointingly small amount or late'. Used in negotiations and reports to frame quantities rhetorically.
examples: 一千元就够了。(1000 yuan is sufficient — easy)
才卖出去五件。(only sold 5 — disappointing)
两天就完成了。(finished in just 2 days — fast)
花了一个月才搞定。(took a whole month — slow)

grammar.explanation: Causal connective pair. 因为 introduces the cause clause; 所以 or 因此 introduces the result. Either can be omitted when context supplies it. 因此 is slightly more formal/written than 所以 and is preferred in business reports and notices. The textbook explicitly describes this as 'HT…trtb…' (因为…所以) indicating a cause-effect relationship.
examples: 因为北京交通堵塞，所以越来越多的市民选择骑自行车上班。
由于旅游业快速发展，因此带动了周边餐饮和住宿行业的整体提升。

grammar.explanation: Concessive pair. The textbook describes 虽然 clause as confirming a preceding fact, while 但是/却 introduces an argument against that fact. 却 (adverb) is embedded mid-clause; 但是 (conjunction) starts the second clause. Both are formal; 却 is slightly more literary. Essential for nuanced business argumentation.
examples: 虽然农家乐条件简单，但是越来越受到城市游客的欢迎。
这种旅游方式虽然价格不高，却能给游客带来真实的乡村体验。

grammar.explanation: Conditional. The textbook labels 如果 as indicating a presumed relationship: 如果 introduces the condition, 就/那么 introduces the result. 如果 is neutral register; 要是 is colloquial. 那么 is formal/written and often appears alone without 如果 in business texts. In formal writing, 若 replaces 如果.
examples: 如果您有任何疑问，请随时与我们联系。
若贵公司有意向，我们可以安排进一步洽谈。

grammar.explanation: Coordinate 'both…and' pattern. The textbook specifies that both clauses must share the same subject. More formal than 'A和B'. Useful for balanced praise or description in business profiles and product descriptions. Implied positive valence — usually both qualities are desirable.
examples: 这种旅游方式既轻松又愉快，非常适合家庭出游。
该企业集团既注重产品质量，又重视售后服务，赢得了客户的高度信任。

grammar.explanation: The textbook explicitly describes this as used 'to strengthen a positive mood' and that a 'time adverbial modifier indicating past tense' is required. Deploy to emphasize the time, place, manner, or agent of a completed action: 是 [focal element] VP 的. The object may come before or after 的. Critical for professional precision when recounting events.
examples: 这份合同是上个月在上海签订的。
产品是在北京工厂生产的，不是在国外加工的。
他是坐高铁来的，不是坐飞机。

grammar.explanation: The textbook states: '过 is used after verb to indicate that certain action or transformation has ever happened.' Negated with 没(有). Distinct from 了: 过 asserts general life-experience or precedent; 了 pins a specific completed event. In business contexts, 过 frames qualifications and track record.
examples: 我们公司曾经与多家跨国企业合作过。
他参观过国内外许多企业，对现代化管理有深刻了解。
这种情况以前发生过，我们有应对经验。

grammar.explanation: The textbook notes: '了 is usually used at the end of a verb-predicate sentence to strengthen a positive mood that something happened. There's always time adverbial modifier indicating past tense in such sentence.' Post-verbal 了 marks bounded completion; sentence-final 了 marks a newly relevant situation (change of state). Do not confuse with 过.
examples: 参观结束了，大家都很满意。
企业的年产量提高了30%。
去年他来北京出差了三次。

grammar.explanation: The textbook lists this as: 'make, to cause: 让 + someone + verb/adjective phrase.' 让 implies permission or softer causation; 使 is more formal and signals abstract causation (suitable for formal documents, reports). The causee functions as object of 让/使, and the VP follows. In formal/business writing, prefer 使 for processes and policies.
examples: 清新的空气让游客感到心旷神怡。
新的管理制度使企业的整体效率大幅提升。
这次参观使我们对现代化工厂有了全新的认识。

grammar.explanation: The textbook describes the 被-sentence: 'The subject of such sentence pattern is the target of the action, and the object is the one who takes the action.' Agent follows 被 and may be omitted. Common in formal and business writing to describe results, evaluations, or processes where the agent is irrelevant or unknown.
examples: 这家企业被评为国家高新技术企业。
产品被广泛应用于工业生产和日常生活。
该计划已被上级主管部门批准。

grammar.explanation: Indicates progressive increase in degree: 'increasingly', 'more and more'. Also appears as 越…越… (the more X, the more Y) with two parallel clauses. Both forms are neutral register and very common in trend descriptions. In business/analytical writing, pair with quantitative data for credibility.
examples: 选择农家乐旅游的城市居民越来越多。
消费者对产品质量的要求越来越高。
越努力，成绩越好；越用心，服务越到位。

grammar.explanation: The textbook states: 'It indicates that some action or situation lasts invariably.' Asserts unbroken continuity of an action or state up to a reference point. Distinct from 总是 (habitual repetition) — 一直 stresses uninterrupted persistence. Common in company histories and progress narratives.
examples: 这家企业自成立以来一直坚持高质量生产标准。
他在这个岗位上一直默默奉献，从不抱怨。
销售额一直保持稳定增长态势。

grammar.explanation: The textbook glosses this as 'continuously, uninterruptedly.' Like 一直 but implies repeated recurrence rather than a single unbroken state. Often collocates with 发展、改善、提高、创新 in business writing. Stronger sense of ongoing effort than 一直.
examples: 企业不断引进先进技术，持续提升竞争力。
我们将不断改善服务质量，以满足客户需求。
产品品种不断丰富，已覆盖多个市场细分领域。

grammar.explanation: 自从 introduces a past starting point; the main clause describes what has continued or changed since. Naturally pairs with 一直, 不断, 越来越, or 已经. Very common in company introductions and business reports to frame a growth or change narrative. More formal than 从…开始.
examples: 自从实行新的管理制度以来，企业效益大幅提升。
自从加入该行业以后，公司一直处于快速发展阶段。
自从2000年成立以来，集团规模不断扩大。

grammar.explanation: Two conditionals with opposite logical force. 只要…就 = 'as long as X, then Y' (sufficient condition; optimistic/persuasive). 只有…才 = 'only if/when X, then Y' (necessary condition; stricter, exclusive). In business writing: use 只有…才 to assert that a specific prerequisite is non-negotiable; use 只要…就 to lower the perceived barrier.
examples: 只要坚持练习，汉语水平就会不断提高。
只有提高产品质量，企业才能在竞争激烈的市场上立于不败之地。
只有双方相互信任，合作才能长期持续。

grammar.explanation: Expresses that the result holds regardless of the condition. 不管 is colloquial; 无论 is formal/written (preferred in business documents). The subordinate clause typically uses interrogative words (什么、怎么、多少、如何). In formal writing, 无论…均… is an even more formal variant.
examples: 无论遇到什么困难，我们都会坚持履行合同义务。
不管价格如何变化，我们都会保证产品质量不变。
无论订单大小，均享受同等的售后服务保障。

grammar.pattern: 连动句 (Serial-Verb Sentence): V1 + O1 + V2 + O2
grammar.explanation: Two verb phrases appear in sequence with NO conjunction or pause between them. The relationship of V1 to V2 can be: (a) V1 expresses the intention or purpose of V2; (b) V1 expresses the manner or means by which V2 is accomplished; (c) V1 and V2 are consecutive actions. The subject of both VPs is shared. In a semi-clause (subject omitted), there should likewise be no stop or conjunction between the two continuous verb phrases.
examples: 他骑自行车去上班。(He rides a bicycle to go to work — V1 expresses manner of V2.)
我去图书馆借书。(I'm going to the library to borrow books — V1 expresses purpose of V2.)
她拿着地图走进来。(She walked in holding a map — V1 expresses manner accompanying V2.)
他关上门走了。(He closed the door and left — consecutive actions.)

grammar.pattern: 把字句否定 (Negation of the 把 Construction)
grammar.explanation: The negating adverb (不 or 没) must be placed BEFORE 把, never between 把 and its object. Some verb types can only be negated with 不 (volitional/habitual), others only with 没 (completed action). Verbs such as 放、留、带、告诉 etc. that involve displacement or transfer of an object are typical 把-sentence verbs. The object of 把 must be definite/specific.
examples: 你不能把这件事告诉他。(You must not tell him about this matter.)
他没把行李放好。(He did not put the luggage away properly.)
请不要把护照丢了。(Please do not lose the passport.)

grammar.explanation: A rhetorical interrogative expresses an emphatic assertion rather than requesting information. A positive rhetorical question conveys a negative meaning; a negative rhetorical question conveys a positive meaning. Common markers: 难道…吗, 怎么能…呢, 哪里…呢. The form is interrogative but the communicative intent is declarative and emphatic. Often used in formal writing and persuasive prose for strong effect.
examples: 这难道不是事实吗？（这就是事实。）(Isn't this a fact? — It IS a fact.)
你怎么能这样对待客户呢？（你不应该这样。）(How can you treat clients like this?)
这件事哪里有那么简单？（一点也不简单。）(Is this matter really that simple? — Not at all.)

grammar.pattern: 正反问句 (A-not-A Question / Affirmative-Negative Question)
grammar.explanation: Formed by juxtaposing the positive and negative form of a predicate: V + 不/没 + V, or Adj + 不 + Adj. It presents two alternatives, and the respondent chooses one. Unlike a 吗 question, it carries no presupposition. Answering by repeating the positive or negative form alone (without 是 or subject) is natural. This is a neutral, non-leading way to seek confirmation.
examples: 你去不去开会？(Are you going to the meeting or not?)
他来没来？(Did he come or not?)
价格合不合适？(Is the price suitable or not?)
这个方案可行不可行？(Is this plan feasible or not?)

grammar.pattern: 补语与宾语的语序 (Positioning of Complement vs. Object)
grammar.explanation: When a verb takes both a result/direction complement AND an object, the usual order is: V + Complement + Object. However, if the complement is a quantified expression (duration or quantity) OR if the object is a pronoun, the object is typically moved BEFORE the complement: V + Object + Complement. Mixing up this order is a common learner error in formal writing.
examples: 他写完了报告。(He finished writing the report — result complement before object.)
我等了他两个小时。(I waited for him for two hours — pronoun object before duration complement.)
她找到了新工作。(She found a new job — result complement after verb.)
他们讨论了这个问题三个小时。(They discussed this issue for three hours.)

grammar.explanation: A qualifying word, phrase, or clause inserted into a sentence so as to be grammatically independent of the surrounding structure. In written Chinese, parenthetical elements are set off by commas. They add the speaker's attitude, degree of certainty, transition cue, or meta-comment. Common parentheticals: 当然, 显然, 事实上, 换句话说, 总的来说, 总而言之, 一般来说. Mastering these raises the register of written business Chinese.
examples: 当然，这只是初步计划。(Of course, this is only a preliminary plan.)
显然，双方都需要让步。(Obviously, both parties need to compromise.)
总的来说，项目进展顺利。(Overall, the project is progressing smoothly.)
换句话说，我们需要调整策略。(In other words, we need to adjust our strategy.)

grammar.pattern: 连词 既然…就… (Conjunction: since/given that … then …)
grammar.explanation: 既然 introduces a premise that is already established or accepted as fact; 就 introduces the logical conclusion that follows. The premise (既然-clause) often restates something the listener concedes or should concede; the conclusion (就-clause) draws the implication. This is common in formal argument, business negotiation, and persuasive writing. 既然 clause typically comes first; inversion is unusual.
examples: 既然双方已经达成共识，就应该尽快签订合同。(Since both parties have reached a consensus, we should sign the contract as soon as possible.)
既然条件不成熟，就不要急于推进。(Since the conditions are not yet ripe, there is no need to rush ahead.)

grammar.pattern: 只要…就… / 只有…才… (Conditional Pairs)
grammar.explanation: 只要…就… = 'As long as … then …' (sufficient condition). 只有…才… = 'Only if/when … then …' (necessary condition). These are crucial for logical argumentation in business writing. Confusing the two changes the logical force of the claim significantly. 只有 sets a uniquely necessary condition; 只要 sets a broadly sufficient one.
examples: 只要双方诚意合作，就一定能取得成功。(As long as both sides cooperate sincerely, success is certain.)
只有提高产品质量，才能赢得市场。(Only by improving product quality can we win the market.)
