takeaways: Structure formal Chinese prose with 首先…其次…最后 (first / second / finally). This three-part sequence is the standard skeleton for business proposals, analytical reports, and formal emails in Chinese. Readers expect it; its absence feels unorganised. For longer arguments, add 另外 or 此外 to insert supplementary points without breaking the flow.

Upgrade cause-effect connectors by register: spoken/everyday uses 因为…所以; written/formal business uses 由于…因此, 鉴于…故, or 有鉴于此. Mixing registers — formal content with 因为/所以 — signals a learner rather than a native writer. Developing sensitivity to this register gradient is one of the highest-yield moves for professional Chinese writing.

Lean on disyllabic verbal compounds over single verbs in formal writing: 办理 not 做, 说明 not 说, 确认 not 知道, 提交 not 给, 协商 not 谈. Chinese business prose prefers these precise two-character verbs that encode procedural or relational meaning the single character lacks.

Master the 把 / 被 split: use 把 when an agent intentionally acts on a known object with a result (disposal); use 被 only for events the subject suffers or didn't choose (usually negative). For neutral processes, active voice is strongly preferred in Chinese even where English would use passive. Overusing 被 signals English interference.

Four-character expressions (四字格) signal fluency: business prose conventionally uses 物美价廉 (quality goods at fair price), 货真价实 (genuine goods at fair price), 互惠互利 (mutually beneficial), 按时交货 (deliver on time), 薄利多销 (small profit, high volume). These chunks are not ornamental — they are the expected register of Chinese commercial prose.

Sequence instructions explicitly with 先…再/然后, 之后, 接着, 最后. Chinese business communications (SOPs, process emails, step-by-step guides) depend on explicit temporal sequencing. Unlike English, which sometimes elides sequence markers ('Fill in the form and submit it'), Chinese makes each step an independent clause with a connector. Do not omit these markers.

Close business letters and emails with register-appropriate fixed formulas: 期待您的回复 (look forward to your reply), 请多关照 (please take care of us / look after us), 谢谢合作 (thank you for your cooperation), 如有问题，请随时联系我们 (if there are any issues, please contact us at any time). The degree of formality of the closing (敬 prefix signals high formality) should match the opener.

In product and service descriptions, always pair 质量 (quality) with either 价格 (price) or 性价比 (value for money). Chinese commercial writing frames value as a quality-price duality; mentioning one without the other feels incomplete to a native reader. The phrase 质量好，价格合理 is the minimum viable product-description sentence.

Use 比较, 相当, 较 for measured professional tone, not 很/非常. In business writing, 非常 can sound breathless or promotional. 比较 (comparatively), 相当 (quite/considerably), and 较 (written shorthand for 比较) convey evaluated judgment without hyperbole — the register of analysis rather than advertising.

Topic-comment structure: lead with the key issue, then comment on it. Chinese naturally frames sentences as 'as for X, (I think / the situation is)…'. In business writing this translates to: 关于付款方式，我方建议采用电汇 (Regarding payment method, we propose wire transfer). This front-loading of the topic matches how Chinese readers process dense information and contrasts with English S-V-O ordering.

Business Chinese at the beginner-intermediate level organizes around thematic, real-world scenarios (financial management, restaurant dining, bargaining, tech use, urban tourism) — when writing for a professional Chinese audience, ground your prose in a concrete situational frame before making abstract claims.

The 把-construction is not merely a grammar rule but a rhetorical choice: use it when you want to foreground what happens to a specific object, especially its transformation or displacement. In formal reports and instructions, this makes outcomes vivid and unambiguous.

Resultative and potential complements (V+好, V+完, V+得了/不了) are the backbone of precision writing in Chinese. Avoid bare verbs for completed actions; adding a resultative complement signals completion and often carries professional register weight.

Temporal attitude with 才 vs 就 is a high-leverage register tool. 才 frames an event as belated or difficult (useful for expressing urgency or frustration in business negotiation); 就 frames it as prompt or easy (useful for reassurance or selling). Choosing the wrong one inverts your intended tone.
The 越...越... pattern is ideal for business analysis: it links two variables in a dynamic relationship (越早决定，越有竞争优势) and reads as polished formal prose, unlike purely additive clause structures.

Old brand vocabulary (老字号, 信誉, 特色) reflects a distinctly Chinese commercial register where heritage and authenticity are key selling propositions — when writing about Chinese companies or products, incorporating this vocabulary signals cultural literacy.

Online commerce terminology (淘宝/当当/京东, 注册, 下载, 用户名) is now standard in business Chinese contexts; its register is neutral-to-informal but appearing in formal documents (company reports, e-commerce contracts) requires pairing it with formal syntactic structures.

Dialogue in Chinese business contexts — restaurant ordering, customer service, negotiation — routinely relies on 推荐 collocations: X推荐Y，（给）Z推荐 = recommend Y to Z. Mastering this pattern and its argument structure (what is recommended, to whom, by whom) is foundational for service-industry and hospitality writing.

Use numbered structure and parallel formatting (1. 2. 3.) for rules, fees, regulations, and registration notices. This textbook's administrative sections (fee schedules, registration rules) demonstrate that Chinese official documents overwhelmingly use enumerated items rather than flowing prose paragraphs. Mirroring this structure signals institutional competence.

Ground business narratives in concrete statistics. The Yanjing Beer enterprise passages integrate specific numbers (tonnage, percentages, years) directly into descriptive prose. Where English might say 'significant growth', professional Chinese says '产量增长了30%' or '年产量超过285万吨'. Vagueness reads as amateur; specificity reads as credible.

Use 自从…以来 + 一直/不断 to frame progress narratives. This temporal framing pattern — 'ever since [founding year], [company] has continuously [grown/improved]…' — is a standard opening move in enterprise introductions, company profiles, and annual reports. It establishes a trajectory and implies momentum.

The 是…的 emphasis frame is essential for professional precision. When stating how, when, where, or by whom something was done, use 是 [focal element] VP 的 rather than a bare verb. This pattern appears repeatedly in the textbook's grammar explanations and is especially important in contract discussions, meeting summaries, and official correspondence.

Concede before asserting. The 虽然…但是 structure signals rhetorical sophistication. Native-sounding business Chinese acknowledges constraints or counterpoints before pivoting to the main positive claim. This pattern appears in negotiation language, project proposals, and analytical reports — skipping the concession reads as overconfident.

Distinguish 让 (softer/permission-flavored causation) from 使 (formal, often abstract causation). In formal documents, reports, and notices, prefer 使: '新政策使销量大幅提升' reads as more authoritative than '新政策让销量大幅提升'. 使 is the default in analytical or institutional prose; 让 in narrative or conversational register.

For trend descriptions, 越来越 is your baseline tool; for intensification, use 越来越 anchored by a quantitative fact. Example: '选择农家乐的游客越来越多，2005年增幅达35%'. The juxtaposition of the trend phrase and the supporting statistic is a hallmark of professional analytical register.

Master the genre conventions of Chinese administrative notices (通知/启事): (1) title stating the subject clearly, e.g. 关于…的通知; (2) background/purpose statement; (3) enumerated details with deadlines and amounts; (4) requirements and conditions; (5) formal closing such as 特此通知 or 欢迎踊跃参与; (6) issuing body and date. Structural predictability is itself a register signal — deviating from it reads as informal or careless.

takeaways.lesson: Use serial-verb sentences to compress action sequences without conjunctions
takeaways.detail: In Chinese, two VPs can be chained directly with no 和/然后/并且 between them: Subject + V1 + O1 + V2 + O2. This is not only grammatical but preferred in formal writing. English learners over-use 然后 or 并且; dropping them produces crisper, more native-sounding prose. Reserve conjunctions for when the logical relationship needs marking (contrast, cause, concession).

takeaways.lesson: Rhetorical questions are a powerful device in Chinese persuasive and business writing
takeaways.detail: Chinese business and professional writing regularly employs rhetorical questions (难道不…吗？怎么能…呢？) to assert a point emphatically without stating it flatly. This raises argumentative force and engages the reader. In English this can sound combative; in Chinese it is a fully accepted formal register device. Practice crafting a point as a rhetorical question as an alternative to a blunt assertion.

takeaways.lesson: Distinguish 改进, 改善, 提高, and 增强 for precision in business writing
takeaways.detail: These four all relate to 'making something better' but differ: 改进 = improve a method/process/product (operational); 改善 = improve conditions/situations/environment; 提高 = raise a level or standard (quantifiable); 增强 = strengthen (capacity, ability, confidence). Confusing them reads as imprecise. Prefer 改进效率 (operational improvement), 改善环境 (environmental improvement), 提高质量 (quality levels), 增强竞争力 (strengthen competitiveness).

takeaways.lesson: Master the 只要…就…／只有…才… distinction for logical argumentation
takeaways.detail: Business proposals and reports often argue for conditions and outcomes. 只要 sets a sufficient condition ('as long as X, then Y follows'), while 只有 sets a necessary condition ('only by X can Y be achieved'). 只有 is the stronger, more exclusive claim and is often found in persuasive contexts where you want to argue that your solution is the ONLY path. Misusing them weakens the logical structure of an argument.

takeaways.lesson: Use formal vocabulary variants rather than everyday words to lift register
takeaways.detail: Business Chinese consistently prefers Sino-compound formal variants over colloquial equivalents: 询问 (not 问), 提供 (not 给), 保证 (not 说好), 处理 (not 弄), 安排 (not 弄好), 确认 (not 确定好). At HSK 5-6 level, the vocabulary is known; the challenge is deploying the formal variant as the default, not as a deliberate choice each time.

takeaways.lesson: Discourse connectors are the skeleton of a well-organized Chinese essay
takeaways.detail: The textbook drills connectors extensively because they signal logical structure. Key pairs to internalize: 首先…其次…最后 (firstly/secondly/finally), 一方面…另一方面 (on one hand/on the other), 不仅…而且 (not only…but also), 虽然…但是 (although…yet), 既然…就 (since…then), 只要…就／只有…才. Without these, even accurate sentences sound disconnected. In professional writing, a paragraph without a visible connector at the start is often perceived as loosely organized.

takeaways.lesson: In travel and hospitality business writing, master the collocations not just the words
takeaways.detail: Key collocations from this domain that appear in professional correspondence and reports: 安排行程 (arrange an itinerary), 提前预订 (book in advance), 核实信息 (verify information), 提供服务 (provide service), 满足需求 (meet requirements/demand), 确保质量 (ensure quality), 维护信誉 (maintain reputation), 促进合作 (promote cooperation). Memorizing these as chunks, not word-by-word, is more efficient and produces idiomatic output.

takeaways.lesson: The complement-object word order trap: always resolve ambiguity with structure, not guessing
takeaways.detail: When a verb has both a result complement and an object, the default is V + Complement + Object (写完报告). But with pronoun objects or duration/quantity complements, the object comes before the complement (等了他两个小时). This asymmetry is a consistent source of errors. In business writing, incorrect word order in complement-object structures reads as elementary-level mistakes — worth drilling explicitly even at HSK 5-6.

takeaways.lesson: 主动 is a high-value professional register word
takeaways.detail: 主动 (proactive, on one's own initiative) appears across Chinese professional contexts: 主动联系 (proactively reach out), 主动解决问题 (take initiative in solving problems), 主动承担责任 (proactively take responsibility). It signals a positive, can-do professional attitude and is a strong word to use in cover letters, self-evaluations, and business proposals. Its antonym 被动 (passive, reactive) is used to critique undesirable attitudes.

takeaways.lesson: Use parenthetical elements (插入语) to add authorial voice and hedging in formal writing
takeaways.detail: Parentheticals like 当然 (of course), 显然 (obviously), 事实上 (in fact), 总的来说 (generally speaking), 一般来说 (generally), 相对来说 (relatively speaking) allow the writer to modulate certainty and add meta-commentary without disrupting the sentence structure. They are characteristic of confident, sophisticated written Chinese. An advanced writer uses them naturally at clause boundaries; a beginner avoids them and produces flat, unvarying prose.

essays.title: 传统消费观与现代年轻人（对话节选）
essays.text: [NOTE: The underlying OCR text is too corrupted for faithful character-level reproduction. The chapter topic and dialogue content are reliably recoverable from comprehension questions (lines 841–880), vocabulary lists, and grammar notes. The dialogue contrasts a foreigner's questions with a Chinese person's explanations of traditional Chinese financial values: 勤俭节约 (diligence and frugality), 精打细算 (meticulous budgeting), and 理财观念 (financial philosophy). It then introduces modern youth phenomena including 月光族 (people who spend entire monthly salary), 啃老族 (adults who financially depend on parents), and 透支消费 (spending beyond one's means). The dialogue explores 代沟 (generation gap) in attitudes toward money, addresses 一知半解 (superficial understanding) of Chinese financial culture, and closes by questioning whether 勤俭 values remain relevant. Key structural feature: a foreigner asks probing questions; the Chinese respondent first acknowledges 传统观念 then complicates them with contemporary counter-examples.]
essays.summary: Multi-turn dialogue comparing traditional Chinese frugality (勤俭节约, 精打细算) with modern youth spending patterns (月光族, 啃老族, 透支). A foreigner's questions draw out nuanced explanation of how ancestral values clash with contemporary consumer culture, framed around 代沟 and changing 消费观.
essays.why_study: Rich lexical cluster for discussing personal finance, social trends, and intergenerational change — all high-frequency in contemporary business Chinese. The dialogue models how to present 传统 vs. 现代 contrast structures and hedge cultural generalizations. Key neologisms (月光族, 啃老族) appear constantly in Chinese media and op-ed writing.
key_devices: 先…然后… sequencing to build argument
Rhetorical questions with 难道 to challenge assumptions
族-compound neologisms to label social phenomena efficiently
Contrast structure: 传统上…但是现在的年轻人…

essays.title: 广交会参展经历（对话节选）
essays.text: [NOTE: OCR too corrupted for faithful verbatim reproduction. Chapter content recoverable from vocabulary lists (lines 1974–2113), comprehension questions (lines 2262–2295), and grammar notes. The dialogue covers: (1) an introduction to the 广交会 (China Import and Export Fair, 中国进出口商品交易会, Guangzhou) and the 中国国际服装博览会 (China International Clothing Fair); (2) practical trade-fair logistics: 展位 (booth) setup, 展区 (exhibition area) layout, meeting 参展商 (exhibitors) and 观众 (visitors/buyers); (3) business activities: 洽谈 (negotiations), 订购 (placing orders), 推销 (sales promotion), presenting 报价单 (price quotations); (4) outcomes: establishing 合作 (cooperation), improving 供需 (supply-demand) matching, raising 品牌 (brand) visibility. Speakers also mention Hong Kong (香港) and Macao (澳门) as nearby venues. One character reports that 广交会 achieves very high 排名 in global trade fairs and that exhibitors must 创造 unique selling points to stand out. The dialogue closes with discussion of whether the fair has helped 拓展 (expand) international market reach.]
essays.summary: Business dialogue about participating in the Canton Fair system: preparing booths, meeting buyers, negotiating prices (报价单), promoting Chinese brands, and extending 供需 connections internationally. Models the full arc from pre-fair preparation to post-fair cooperation follow-up.
essays.why_study: Core vocabulary and conversational moves for the trade-fair and B2B export context — essential business Chinese for anyone working in China's trade sector. The chapter also models how to describe organizational logistics and outcomes in formal dialogue.
key_devices: Topic-comment sentence structure for describing fair sections
Embedded enumerations listing fair activities
Formal measure words and classifiers for exhibitions (届, 家, 个)
Result complements to describe business outcomes (达到, 创造, 扩大)

essays.title: 燕京啤酒集团参观记（对话节选）
essays.text:
[NOTE: OCR partially legible; verbatim reproduction not possible for most turns. Chapter topic and content recoverable from questions (lines 3236–3255) and vocabulary list (lines 2944–3070). The dialogue describes a guided visit to Yanjing Beer Corporation (燕京啤酒集团 / 燕京啤酒公司 Yānjīng Píjiǔ). Key content: (1) Scale and history — the enterprise has grown from a small local brewery into a nationally recognized 一流品牌 (first-class brand) with enormous 产销量 (production and sales volume) and 市场占有率 (market share); (2) Modernization — the factory features fully 自动化 (automated) 生产线 (production lines) with 监控 (monitoring/quality control) at every stage; (3) Ownership structure — the guide explains China's enterprise ownership types: 私营 (private), 国家 (state-owned), 合资 (joint-venture), and 乡镇企业 (village and township enterprises), which have all 纷纷 (in succession) developed; (4) Competitive strategy — 优势 (competitive advantage) comes from 一体化 (vertical integration) and control of 劳动力 (labor); (5) Expansion — the company is actively 拓展 (expanding) market reach domestically and internationally. One speaker asks 'how does the factory handle quality under such 巨大 scale?' The guide explains that 规模 (scale) itself creates 优势 in the 成本 (cost) dimension.]

Partially legible lines from the dialogue (lines 2848–2870 area, OCR errors retained):
甲：这家工厂到底有多大规模？听说[产量超过350亿？]
乙：对，我们[...有三类农庄/合作农庄...]，国家给予了很大支持。
甲：生产线是全自动化的吗？
乙：[...自动化程度很高...每道工序都有监控...]，达到了国际一流水平。
甲：那么在市场竞争中，你们的优势是什么？
乙：主要是规模优势、品牌优势，加上[一体化的管理]，成本控制得很好。
essays.summary: Factory-tour dialogue at Yanjing Beer covering enterprise history, production automation (自动化, 生产线), ownership reform vocabulary (私营/国家/合资/乡镇), market position (市场占有率, 一流品牌), and expansion strategy (拓展, 一体化). A case study in Chinese enterprise discourse.
essays.why_study: Models the language of enterprise introduction and factory-tour discourse — extremely common in business Chinese contexts when visiting or presenting a company. Introduces the ownership-structure vocabulary that recurs in any discussion of Chinese business organization. The question-answer tour format is a genre worth imitating.
key_devices: Q&A tour-guide structure: 问…答… with factual enumeration
Scale-indicating expressions: 成千上万, 巨大规模, 一流
Comparison and contrast of enterprise ownership types
Achievement-highlighting pattern: 达到…水平, 实现了…化

grammar.explanation: Conjunction meaning 'so as not to / so as to avoid'. Placed after the proposed action to preemptively justify it by naming the bad outcome it prevents. More efficient than a separate 'otherwise' clause. Very common in business writing and professional emails.
examples: 我们提前确认好日期，免得临时有变动。
请尽早回复，免得误了截止日期。
走高速吧，免得堵车耽误时间。

grammar.explanation: Adverb meaning 'in vain / to no effect'. '白+V' means the action produced no result or was wasted effort. Colloquial/spoken register; formal writing prefers 徒劳 or 无济于事. Useful for understanding spoken complaints or light business banter.
examples: 等了两个小时，白等了。
没带证件，白跑一趟。
你说了也白说，他根本不听。

grammar.pattern: 据我所知 (jù wǒ suǒ zhī)，…
grammar.explanation: Sentence-initial phrase meaning 'as far as I know'. Signals the speaker is sharing known information while taking epistemic responsibility without overstating certainty. Highly productive in business reports, proposals, and professional emails. Equivalent to 'to my knowledge' or 'from what I understand'.
examples: 据我所知，那家公司的服务一流，口碑很好。
据我所知，合同还没有正式签署。
据我所知，他已经提交申请了。

grammar.explanation: Adverb meaning 'surprisingly / to one's surprise / actually (unexpectedly)'. Marks the action/fact as contrary to what was expected. Can express positive surprise or indignation. Stronger than '居然' in formal writing is debatable, but both are common. Very useful in persuasive writing to frame an opponent's behaviour as outrageous without explicit judgment.
examples: 他竟然没来参加会议，真让人意外。
这个地方竟然有这么多外国游客。
这么简单的错误，他竟然没发现。

grammar.explanation: Conjunction meaning 'otherwise / if not'. Introduces the negative consequence of not following the preceding advice or condition. '否则' is slightly more formal than '不然'. Common in warnings, instructions, and business communications.
examples: 请准时到达，否则会议无法开始。
你得早点预订，否则订不到房间。
必须按规定操作，否则后果自负。

grammar.explanation: Adverb meaning 'while you're at it / incidentally / without extra effort'. Signals that an additional action requires little extra effort since you are already doing something else. Very effective in spoken and written requests to soften the burden of an ask.
examples: 你去银行的时候，顺便帮我取一下快递。
我去书店，顺便买几本参考书。
您顺便把这份文件交给李经理好吗？

grammar.explanation: Adverb meaning 'even / going so far as to'. Used to introduce the most extreme member of a series, escalating the argument to its logical peak. Items before 甚至 should build progressively. Commonly paired with 也/都. Do not use 甚至 for the first or mildest case in a list.
examples: 他工作非常努力，甚至周末也不休息。
这家餐厅什么都有，甚至还有现场音乐。
价格很高，甚至超过了我们的预算。

grammar.explanation: Phrase meaning 'luckily / thanks to'. Expresses gratitude for someone/something that prevented a bad outcome. Typically followed by '否则/要不然' + the bad thing that was avoided. Powerful for formal thank-you letters, performance reviews, and business correspondence.
examples: 多亏你提醒，否则我真忘了带合同。
多亏了她的帮助，项目才顺利完成。
多亏经理及时介入，问题才得以解决。

grammar.explanation: Adverb meaning 'for X consecutive times / in a row'. Must be followed by a quantifier (number + measure word). Expresses that an action or state repeats without interruption. More vivid than simple repetition, implies notable persistence or frequency.
examples: 他一连打了三个电话，没人接。
这家店一连开了五天促销活动。
她一连工作了十个小时，没有休息。

grammar.explanation: Sufficient condition: 'As long as A, then B'. A is sufficient to guarantee B. The condition is easy to fulfil, and the result follows automatically. Contrast with 只有/才 which expresses necessity.
examples: 只要你努力，就一定能成功。
只要提前预约，就可以享受优惠。
只要按时提交，就不会有问题。

grammar.explanation: Necessary condition: 'Only if/when A, then B'. A is the one and only prerequisite for B. Without A, B cannot happen. Logically the opposite emphasis from 只要/就. Critical to distinguish these two in formal writing — wrong choice inverts your argument's logic.
examples: 只有签了合同，才能正式开始工作。
只有了解市场，才能制定好策略。
只有持有效证件，才能办理业务。

grammar.explanation: Additive pattern: 'Besides A, also B'. Indicates B is included in addition to A. Often followed by 还 (also, in addition) or 也 (also). Useful for listing comprehensive features, services, or capabilities without sounding exhaustive.
examples: 除了汉语，他还会说英语和法语。
除了价格优惠，服务也很周到。
除了办理开户，还可以申请网上银行。

grammar.explanation: 难怪 means 'no wonder / it's no surprise that'. Used to express that, having understood the cause, the result makes perfect sense. Often paired with 原来 (so that's why / as it turns out) which reveals the surprising underlying reason. This pair is useful for transitions in analytical writing: state observation → reveal cause with 原来 → validate with 难怪.
examples: 难怪他总是第一个到，原来他住得离公司很近。
难怪这家餐厅这么受欢迎，原来是名厨掌勺。
原来他去年就来过中国，难怪普通话说得这么好。

grammar.explanation: Adjective/adverb meaning 'rare / it is rare that / it is a privilege to'. Expresses that something does not happen often and should be cherished. Used to frame a welcome occasion, a special quality, or a rare chance. Slightly formal and appreciative in tone.
examples: 难得有这么好的机会，一定要好好把握。
他难得来一次，我们好好款待他。
能大饱口福一次，真是难得。

grammar.explanation: Spoken marker meaning 'right / that's right / oh, by the way'. Used either to confirm the other party's idea or to introduce a new topic that just occurred to the speaker. In business dialogue, 对了 signals a pivoting follow-up point. Register: conversational, suitable for meetings and calls but not formal written documents.
examples: 对了，你有没有带合同？
—我们下午两点开会。—对了，要通知李总吗？
对了，你说的方案我已经看过了，很不错。

grammar.explanation: Sentence-final particle meaning 'that's all / nothing more'. Used with 只是, 不过, 无非 to minimize or downplay a claim. Common in spoken and semi-formal written Chinese to express modesty, understatement, or mild dismissal. The textbook notes it is placed at the end of declarative sentences.
examples: 不过是兴趣罢了，谈不上什么专业。
这件事只是个误会罢了，别放在心上。

grammar.explanation: Auxiliary verb meaning 'easy to / convenient to / so as to'. Followed by a verb or verb phrase. Indicates that the main clause facilitates the subordinate action. The textbook identifies this as an auxiliary verb in the context of technology and service descriptions.
examples: 这款软件界面简洁，便于操作。
请留下您的联系方式，便于我们及时与您沟通。

grammar.explanation: Idiom: 'You get what you pay for.' Price reflects quality. Also appears reversed as 一分货一分钱. Used in commercial contexts to justify premium pricing or to counsel against choosing purely on cost. The textbook presents it as a commercial proverb about quality and value.
examples: 这个品牌贵有贵的道理，一分钱一分货嘛。
买东西不能只看价格，一分钱一分货。

grammar.explanation: The three consumer-protection guarantees standard in Chinese commerce: 退货 (tuìhuò, return), 换货 (huànhuò, exchange), and 维修 (wéixiū, repair). Knowing this term and its components is essential for consumer-rights discourse, retail negotiation, and business correspondence.
examples: 本产品享受三包服务，一年内可免费退换修。
商家必须提供三包保障，这是消费者的基本权利。

grammar.pattern: V + 不了 / V + 得了 (potential complement)
grammar.explanation: Potential complement indicating whether an action is feasible. V+不了 = 'cannot do (due to difficulty or impossibility)'; V+得了 = 'can manage to do'. Related forms include V+不完 (can't finish), V+不下 (can't hold/fit), V+不到 (can't obtain/reach). These cannot always be substituted with 能/不能. The textbook illustrates this with shopping and problem-solving contexts.
examples: 东西太多，一天买不完。
这个难题我一个人解决不了，需要大家一起想办法。

grammar.explanation: 'Let alone / much less / not to mention'. Introduces a rhetorically stronger case using an a fortiori argument: if the lesser case is challenging, the greater case is even more so. The textbook notes it is used with rhetorical tone and carries a sense of comparison. Often appears in transportation and logistics discussions.
examples: 大人都觉得难，何况孩子呢？
坐地铁已经很方便了，何况还有公共汽车和出租车可以选择。

grammar.explanation: Universal quantifier construction: 'All / every / any…will/must…'. 凡是 can be followed by 都, 总, or 就 in the result clause. Common in regulations, policies, and sweeping generalizations. Signals a formal or authoritative register and is used in consumer-rights and business-policy contexts.
examples: 凡是购买本产品的顾客，都可以享受售后服务。
凡是质量不合格的商品，一律不得上市销售。

grammar.explanation: Fixed potential complement: 'cannot bear / cannot stand / is unbearable'. Expresses that a situation exceeds the speaker's tolerance. Highly colloquial and emotionally expressive. The textbook uses it in transportation and daily-life contexts.
examples: 每天上下班堵车，真受不了！
高峰期坐地铁，人挤人，受不了。

grammar.explanation: Sufficient conditional: 'As long as / provided that…then…'. States the minimal condition guaranteeing a result. The textbook identifies this as a 'condition that leads to the result' and uses it in quality-of-service and consumer-behavior contexts.
examples: 只要质量好，顾客就愿意多花钱。
只要按照规定操作，就不会出现问题。

grammar.explanation: Concessive conditional: 'Even if / even though…still…'. Presents a hypothetical or contrary-to-fact condition and shows that the result holds regardless. The textbook notes this usually indicates an unrealized condition. Useful in argumentation and persuasive prose.
examples: 即使打了折扣，这个价格也不算便宜。
即使再忙，也要抽时间维权，保护自己的利益。

grammar.explanation: 'Up to / as many/much as'. Emphasizes a surprisingly large quantity, usually preceding a number. Used to highlight scale or magnitude. The textbook identifies it in the Tibet-travel chapter as meaning 'up to, as many as', common in journalistic and business-report writing.
examples: 参展的商品多达三千种。
本次活动吸引了多达一万名消费者参加。

grammar.explanation: 'To think / assume / consider' — often implying a mistaken or unverified belief that turns out to be wrong. The textbook treats it as 'consider / regard as / count as'. Contrast with 认为 (neutral belief). When followed by a narrative reversal, 以为 signals the speaker's expectation was incorrect.
examples: 我以为他不来了，没想到他准时到了。
别以为便宜的东西就一定质量差。

grammar.explanation: 'Not X, but rather Y'. Negates one element and affirms another in contrast. The textbook notes that the former element is denied and the latter confirmed. Common in explanatory, analytical, and persuasive writing for clarification and redirection.
examples: 这不是价格的问题，而是质量和服务的问题。
消费者维权不是无理取闹，而是依法保护自己的权益。

grammar.explanation: 'Must / have no choice but to / simply must'. Double negation expressing strong necessity or compulsion — more emphatic than 必须. The textbook presents it in the context of booking and business-travel necessity. Used when something is truly unavoidable.
examples: 订机票非提前预订不可，否则根本买不到票。
这件事非今天完成不可，不能再拖了。

grammar.explanation: Four-character idiom: 'Different from the common run / unique / distinctive'. The textbook defines it as having a peculiar style, special function, or quality that sets something apart from ordinary things. Positive connotation in commercial and aesthetic contexts.
examples: 这个品牌的设计风格与众不同，深受年轻消费者喜爱。
她的经营理念与众不同，所以公司发展得特别快。

grammar.pattern: 外行 vs 内行 / 行家 (wàiháng vs nèiháng / hángjiā)
grammar.explanation: 外行 = 'layman / outsider', someone lacking knowledge or experience in a field. 内行 / 行家 = 'expert / insider / professional'. Antonyms. The textbook explicitly notes these as antonyms and uses them to discuss expertise levels in technology and purchasing contexts.
examples: 我对电脑是外行，麻烦你帮我推荐一款。
他是这一行的行家，买家电可以向他请教。

grammar.pattern: 维权 / 诚信 as abbreviated forms
grammar.explanation: 维权 is a standard abbreviation of 维护消费者权益 ('safeguard consumer rights'). 诚信 is short for 诚实守信 ('honest and trustworthy'). Both are commonly used abbreviated forms in spoken Chinese and business/consumer-rights discourse. The textbook explicitly notes these abbreviated forms and their full originals.
examples: 消费者有权利也有必要维权，不能忍气吞声。
企业必须讲诚信，才能赢得顾客的长期信任。

grammar.pattern: 也…也…（无论/不管 + 也…也…）
grammar.explanation: Enumerative concessive pattern indicating that the listed items apply universally regardless of circumstances. The textbook notes: 'suggests that the enumerative items would not change in any circumstances.' Functions like English 'whether X or Y, the result stays the same.' Often paired with 无论/不管.
examples: 刮风也好，下雨也好，他每天准时到公司。
不管价格高也罢，低也罢，质量才是关键。
你去也行，不去也行，我们都支持你的决定。

grammar.explanation: Adverb meaning 'on the contrary / instead / conversely.' Marks a result contrary to expectation or logical norm. Similar in meaning to 相反 but syntactically integrated into the predicate phrase. Often follows a concessive or conditional clause. The textbook glosses it as 'be on the contrary to … similar meaning as 相反.'
examples: 他越解释，大家反而越不相信他。
吃了药，病反而更严重了。
市场竞争加剧，一些企业的利润反而提高了。

grammar.explanation: Fixed expression meaning 'be indifferent / it doesn't matter / I don't care either way.' Used when the speaker has no preference or considers the outcome unimportant. The textbook gloss: 'be indifferent, no matter.' Can stand alone or precede a noun/clause with 什么.
examples: 去不去无所谓，你自己看着办吧。
对他来说，薪水多少无所谓，关键是工作内容有意思。
我无所谓，随便都行。

grammar.explanation: Adverb meaning 'after all / when all is said and done.' Introduces a conclusion drawn from objective, acknowledged facts. Often signals a concession that still supports the speaker's main point. Placed before the predicate. The textbook notes it 'suggests getting a conclusion according to the fact.'
examples: 他毕竟是第一次参加面试，紧张一点是正常的。
毕竟是大品牌，质量有保障。
他工作了二十年，毕竟经验丰富，你应该听他的建议。

grammar.explanation: Directional complement indicating the inception or ongoing continuation of a state or action. V+起来 = 'start doing V / begin to V (and continue)'; adj.+起来 = 'become / turn adj.' Extremely productive in both spoken and written Chinese. The textbook notes: 'indicates that something happens and continues.'
examples: 随着经济的发展，人们的生活富裕起来了。
看到合同上的数字，他笑了起来。
生产线实现自动化后，效率提高起来了，成本也降低了。

grammar.explanation: Sentence-initial or clause-initial adverb meaning 'it is said / reportedly / allegedly / I hear that.' Marks information as hearsay or unverified. Slightly more formal than 听说. Common in written expository and journalistic Chinese. The textbook also lists parallel forms: 有说法是, 有人说.
examples: 据说广交会每届参展商超过两万家。
据说燕京啤酒已经跻身全国品牌前三名了。
据说明年广州还要举办更大规模的国际贸易展。

grammar.explanation: Inclusive/emphatic concessive pattern meaning 'even X (also/all)….' The item after 连 is the extreme or least-expected case — if even that case applies, all others certainly do. Often pairs with 何况 or 更别说 ('let alone') to form an escalating chain. The textbook notes 何况 has similar meaning to 连…也不能/更别说.
examples: 连中国年轻人都不太理解这个习俗，更别说外国人了。
他连简历都没有准备好，怎么可能通过面试？
这道题连专家都做不出来，我当然更不行了。

grammar.explanation: Colloquial adverb meaning 'always doing sth. / do nothing but / constantly.' Carries a mildly critical or exasperated tone. Common in spoken Chinese and informal writing. The textbook specifies it 'is normally followed by verbs.'
examples: 他净说废话，一点有用的信息都没有。
这家公司净招没经验的人，难怪效率这么低。
你净挑别人的毛病，自己有没有问题？

grammar.explanation: Productive suffix forming verbs or adjectives meaning 'to make X / to become X / -ize / -ify.' Very common in formal, business, and policy Chinese. The textbook gives: 商业化 (commercialize), 现代化 (modernize), 自动化 (automate), 绿化 (beautify/green). This suffix is essential for discussing reform, development, and institutional change.
examples: 工厂正在积极推进生产自动化，目标是五年内全面实现。
政府计划在该地区大力推进绿化工程。
随着市场竞争加剧，企业管理也需要科学化、规范化。

grammar.explanation: Strong negation pattern meaning 'not even a little bit / not at all.' Strengthens a negative predicate for emphasis. The textbook notes this 'is used to strengthen negative tone.' Can be used in both spoken and written registers.
examples: 这件事和他一点关系都没有，不要误会他。
产品质量一点都不差，完全符合国际标准。
我对这份工作一点兴趣也没有。

grammar.explanation: Concessive pattern meaning 'no matter how X, still Y.' The first clause presents an extreme condition; the second states an unchanged result. Frequently used in persuasive writing to anticipate objections. The textbook includes examples of this pattern in several chapters.
examples: 路再难走，我们也要坚持走下去。
价格再高，客户有需求就会接受。
问题再复杂，也要找到切实可行的解决方案。

grammar.explanation: Progressive additive structure meaning 'not only X but also Y.' The second clause escalates or extends the first. Very common in formal written Chinese for introducing additional favorable (or unfavorable) attributes. Equivalent to English 'not only…but also.'
examples: 广交会不但吸引了国内参展商，而且吸引了大批海外买家。
他不但有丰富的工作经验，而且英语水平也很高。
这款产品不但质量上乘，而且售后服务也非常完善。

takeaways.lesson: Use 免得 for preemptive justification in professional writing
takeaways.detail: In business emails and memos, state the recommended action and immediately follow with 免得 + the bad outcome it prevents. This is more economical than a separate 'otherwise' sentence and sounds more decisive. Pattern: [do X]，免得[bad Y]。 Example: '请提前发送日程，免得与会人员时间冲突。'

takeaways.lesson: Distinguish 只要/就 from 只有/才 for precise conditional logic
takeaways.detail: 只要A就B expresses a sufficient condition (A alone is enough to guarantee B). 只有A才B expresses a necessary condition (B is impossible without A). Using the wrong one inverts your argument: '只要签合同就开工' (signing is sufficient to start) ≠ '只有签合同才能开工' (signing is the only way to start). In formal proposals and reports, choose carefully.

takeaways.lesson: Escalate arguments with 甚至 before the climactic case
takeaways.detail: 甚至 is not a general intensifier — it introduces the most extreme, surprising, or notable item in a progression. Build up a series from mild to strong, then introduce the peak with 甚至: '他工作积极，周末也不休息，甚至放弃了年假。' Do not use 甚至 for the first or mildest example in a list.

takeaways.lesson: Hedge claims professionally with 据我所知
takeaways.detail: Place 据我所知 at the start of a sentence when reporting what you understand but cannot fully verify. This protects credibility in business reports and professional emails: it signals you are sharing genuine knowledge while inviting correction. More professional than either stating as undisputed fact or over-hedging with 也许/可能.

takeaways.lesson: Frame gratitude with 多亏…，否则… for maximum impact
takeaways.detail: The pattern 多亏了[person/thing]，否则[bad outcome that was avoided] is far more powerful than a simple 谢谢. It makes the value delivered concrete and explicit — essential in formal thank-you messages, performance reviews, and client communications. Example: '多亏了您的及时反馈，否则我们可能错过了这个机会。'

takeaways.lesson: Use 顺便 to soften additional requests
takeaways.detail: When making a secondary request of a colleague or client, 顺便 signals the ask is low-effort because the person is already doing something related. This reduces perceived imposition. '你去财务部的时候，顺便把这份报告交过去好吗？' sounds much lighter than a standalone request. Use freely in verbal communication and informal written messages.

takeaways.lesson: Cluster 招待、周到、照顾 when writing about service quality
takeaways.detail: These three words form a natural collocational cluster in business Chinese when describing hospitality or customer service: 招待周到 (attentive hospitality), 照顾入微 (attentive care), 服务周到 (thoughtful service). Using them together raises register and demonstrates command of service-industry lexicon in business correspondence and reviews.

takeaways.lesson: Know the difference between 发票 and 收据 for expense contexts
takeaways.detail: In Chinese business practice, 发票 (fāpiào) is the official tax-stamped invoice issued by a registered entity, required for reimbursement (报销). 收据 is a generic payment receipt that is often not acceptable for reimbursement. Always ask for 发票 when a transaction is business-related. This distinction frequently appears in professional dialogue and correspondence.

takeaways.lesson: Use 难怪…原来… to structure cause-effect transitions in analytical writing
takeaways.detail: The pair 原来 (reveals the underlying reason) + 难怪 (validates the observable result) creates a satisfying analytical transition: first reveal the cause with 原来, then confirm it explains the effect with 难怪. Works in reverse order too: 难怪→原来. Example structure: '难怪她的汉语说得这么流利，原来她在北京住了五年。' This is more elegant than 因为A所以B in descriptive and reflective writing.

takeaways.lesson: 竟然 signals controlled indignation or surprise without explicit judgment
takeaways.detail: In persuasive or analytical writing, 竟然 before an action or fact frames it as outrageous or surprising without needing a separate evaluative sentence. '他竟然没有通知任何人就做了这个决定' carries the judgment implicitly. This is a more sophisticated rhetorical move than stating 他这样做是不对的. Use when you want the reader to form the negative judgment themselves.

takeaways.lesson: Business dining vocabulary is essential for professional social contexts
takeaways.detail: Master the distinction between 请客 (one person treats, pays for all) and AA制 (each pays their own share). Know 包间 (private room), 点菜 (order dishes), 结账 (settle the bill), 招牌菜 (signature dish). In professional dining contexts, choosing whether to 请客 or go AA制 carries social significance and knowing how to negotiate this linguistically is a key soft skill.

takeaways.lesson: 应有尽有 is a high-yield chengyu for describing comprehensive offerings
takeaways.detail: 应有尽有 (yīngyǒu jìnyǒu, 'everything one would expect is there') is extremely productive in business descriptions: product catalogues, store recommendations, service descriptions. It saves multiple sentences of listing: '这个平台应有尽有，从办公用品到电子产品都能找到。' Memorise and reuse — it appears frequently in business promotional language and evaluations.

takeaways: Register command is a primary skill in business Chinese: The textbook systematically contrasts formal vocabulary (权益、义务、协商、征求) with colloquial terms (讨价还价、砍价、受不了、别提了). Advanced writers should deploy these registers deliberately—formal in reports, proposals, and letters; colloquial in dialogue, narrative scene-setting, and character voice.

Master the consumer-rights vocabulary cluster as a coherent semantic field: 消费者、权益、义务、侵害、投诉、假冒伪劣、维权、诚信 all appear together in civic and business-ethics contexts. Internalize both full forms and standard abbreviations (维权 = 维护消费者权益; 诚信 = 诚实守信; 三包 = 退货/换货/维修) to operate fluently in complaints, editorials, and formal letters.

Four-character idioms and fixed expressions create commercial texture: 一分钱一分货, 与众不同, 各取所需, 一举两得, 络绎不绝, 物美价廉, 无微不至, 薄利多销 are high-frequency collocations in business Chinese. Using them signals idiomatic fluency and is expected in polished commercial writing, advertising copy, and business arguments.

Conditional and concessive patterns are high-yield for persuasive and explanatory prose: 只要…就…, 即使…也…, 凡是…都…, and 何况 are workhorses of Chinese exposition and argumentation. Embedding them correctly—especially 凡是 with 都/总/就, and 何况 in a fortiori reasoning—signals syntactic sophistication and is especially effective in business proposals, consumer-rights arguments, and policy writing.

Potential complements (V+得/不+了/完/下/到) are non-substitutable and texturally richer than 能/不能: Forms like 买不完, 解决不了, 受不了, 放不下 carry idiomatic weight that bare modals lack. They are emotionally expressive, context-bound, and native-sounding. Build a personal repertoire of the most productive combinations.

Non-必须 patterns for necessity: 非…不可 is more emphatic and idiomatic than 必须 for expressing truly unavoidable obligation, especially in spoken and semi-formal Chinese. 不得不 covers reluctant necessity ('have no choice but to'). Distinguishing these three necessity markers allows precise gradation of compulsion in business and negotiation contexts.

Abbreviation and compression are hallmarks of Chinese business register: Standard abbreviated forms (三包, 维权, 诚信, 精通) signal in-group fluency in spoken contexts. Using the full form in formal writing signals clarity and precision. The learner should know both and switch appropriately. The textbook explicitly glosses several abbreviations, signaling their practical importance.

Street-market and formal-retail Chinese represent two ends of a commercial register spectrum: Xiushui/Dongjiaco market material (亏本、赚钱、薄利、讨价还价、老板、砍价) versus Xidan shopping-center material (促销、三包、信誉、做工、品牌、积分、返券) illustrate this clearly. Controlling the full spectrum—from haggling register to corporate retail register—is essential for authentic and versatile business Chinese writing.

毕竟 signals reasoning from acknowledged fact, not mere assertion. Placing it before the predicate — 他毕竟是新人，犯错误很正常 — lends credibility by conceding the opponent's premise while redirecting to your point. Use it in the middle of an argument, not as an opener.

The 连…也/都… + 何况/更别说 chain creates a two-stage emphasis move that amplifies any claim. First cite the extreme case with 连, then use 何况 or 更别说 to extend: 连专家都不确定，更别说普通消费者了. This is a standard move in persuasive and analytical Chinese writing.

The -化 suffix is essential for formal writing about reform, modernization, or institutional change: 商业化, 现代化, 自动化, 规范化, 国际化. Chinese policy and business documents use these compound verbs constantly. Knowing them passively is insufficient — produce them actively in discussions of industry trends.

四字成语 like 精打细算, 取长补短, 美中不足, 名副其实, 百闻不如一见, 一言为定 are not decorative: they function as compressed predicates or arguments. Learn each in a clause context (e.g., 两家公司在技术上可以取长补短) so you can deploy them as functional units, not isolated chunks.

族-compound neologisms (月光族, 啃老族, 志愿者…族) are a productive pattern in contemporary Chinese media and op-ed writing. They efficiently label social phenomena and signal cultural literacy. When writing about social or economic trends, one well-placed 族 compound is worth several explanatory sentences.

For trade-fair and B2B writing, master the supply-chain cluster as a set: 供需, 品牌, 洽谈, 展位, 报价单, 订购, 推销, 合作, 供应商, 参展商. These terms co-occur in Chinese business correspondence and should be command vocabulary — not just recognition vocabulary — so you can generate sentences without pausing to search.

In enterprise introductions, Chinese writers reliably address five dimensions: scale (规模, 产销量), ownership type (私营/国家/合资/乡镇), competitive position (市场占有率, 一流品牌), modernization level (自动化, 现代化), and growth trajectory (拓展, 一体化). Using this five-point framework when writing or speaking about a company produces output that sounds native and authoritative.

Hedge hearsay vs. data-backed claims explicitly: 据说 (hearsay/unverified) vs. 据统计/根据数据显示 (statistics-backed). Chinese business and academic writing distinguishes these clearly. Using 据说 for facts you could cite a statistic for reads as weak; using 据统计 for anecdote reads as overclaiming. Match the hedge to the evidence type.

The 再…也… concessive structure ('no matter how X, still Y') is the standard move for anticipating objections in formal writing: 价格再高，质量也要保证；路再难走，也要坚持到底. It maps onto English 'even if…still' and is required in any persuasive or argumentative register.

反而 is the Chinese writer's tool for counter-intuitive analysis. After stating an expected outcome, use 反而 to introduce the surprising or ironic reality: 竞争加剧，利润率反而提高了. This signals sophisticated analytical reasoning rather than simple narration — use it whenever you want to show that reality is more complex than the surface suggests.
This chunk is a pure vocabulary appendix — no prose model exists. The takeaways are about the vocabulary patterns it reveals, which inform written business Chinese style.

FOUR-CHARACTER IDIOMS (成语 chéngyǔ) ARE A PRESTIGE MARKER IN BUSINESS WRITING. The glossary includes at least a dozen: 供不应求, 精打细算, 货真价实, 物美价廉, 取长补短, 争先恐后, 应有尽有, 一举两得, 一言为定, 一知半解, 络绎不绝, 更新换代, 大同小异, 成千上万, 无精打采, 慷慨解囊. In business Chinese at HSK 5-6, inserting one or two apt chéngyǔ into a report, letter, or proposal signals command of the register. Overuse sounds affected — aim for one per paragraph at most.

COMPOUND BUSINESS NOUNS ARE HIGHLY PRODUCTIVE. Many core terms are 2- or 3-syllable compounds: 产销量 (production-sales-volume), 参展商 (exhibitor), 生产线 (production line), 劳动力 (labour force), 供需 (supply-and-demand). Fluent business writing strings these compounds efficiently rather than using periphrastic descriptions.

VERB-OBJECT COLLOCATIONS MATTER MORE THAN INDIVIDUAL WORDS. Note: 采取措施 (take measures), 开发票 (make out an invoice), 发挥优势 (bring advantages into play), 签合同 (sign a contract), 投入资金 (put in capital), 促进增长 (promote growth), 扩大市场 (expand the market). When studying this vocabulary, always learn the standard verb that pairs with each noun — the wrong verb is a register error.

诚信 (INTEGRITY) AND 信誉 (CREDIT STANDING) ARE CORE VALUE TERMS IN BUSINESS CHINESE. 诚信 appears frequently in mission statements, business letters, and negotiation contexts. Using it signals alignment with Confucian business ethics. Contrast: 诚信经营 (run a business with integrity) vs. 信誉良好 (have a good reputation). Both are appropriate in formal proposals and cover letters.

REGISTER CONTRAST: SPOKEN VS. WRITTEN BUSINESS CHINESE. The glossary covers both registers. Colloquial business terms: 老板 (boss), 电邮 (email — vs. formal 电子邮件), 生意 (business — vs. formal 业务 or 商务). Written/formal terms: 策略 (strategy), 举办 (hold an event), 洽谈 (negotiate — more formal than 谈). In your writing, default to the formal column.

SET PHRASES FOR CLOSING BUSINESS NEGOTIATIONS. 一言为定 ('it's a deal, a promise is a promise') and 洽谈 (hold talks) are both from this glossary. For formal closings, 签订合同 (sign a contract) or 达成协议 (reach an agreement) are the standard collocations.

THE VOCABULARY COVERS AT LEAST 6 BUSINESS DOMAINS: (1) Finance & banking: 股票, 贷款, 国际金融, 税款, 透支, 资金; (2) Trade & commerce: 合同, 合作, 销售, 折扣, 促销, 采购; (3) HR & employment: 简历, 招聘, 就业, 人才, 应聘; (4) Marketing: 品牌, 名牌, 广告, 营销, 宣传; (5) Operations: 操作, 程序, 生产线, 措施; (6) Digital: 互联网, 网络, 宽带, 下载, 电邮. When writing for a specific domain, load your text with the appropriate cluster — this is how professional register is created in Chinese.

ECONOMY OF EXPRESSION: Many English multi-word phrases compress to 2-character Chinese compounds. 'Supply and demand' = 供需; 'production and sales volume' = 产销量; 'domestic demand' = 内需; 'labour force' = 劳动力. Prefer these compact forms in reports and memos over paraphrase.

OCR NOTE FOR DOWNSTREAM PROCESSING: The CJK character layer in the original PDF was almost entirely illegible in OCR — producing garbled ASCII strings. Only English translations and (partially corrupted) pinyin romanizations were readable. All Chinese characters in this output are reconstructed from those two signals using standard vocabulary knowledge. The reconstructions are reliable for standard dictionary words but should be verified against a live dictionary before adding to Anki cards.

essays.title: 在中国做生意
essays.text:
中国人见面时常常用握表示欢迎。很多喜欢在第一次见面时交换名。交换时，应该双把的名给别；别给你名时，你应该双接，表示礼貌。
有些的名上有很多头衔，一般来说，第个头衔最重要。另外，和很多人同时见面时，一定要先把你的名给地位最的。
在正式的商务活动中，如果你要介绍别，应该先把职位的介绍给职位低的。
如果你要称呼别，最好称呼他的头衔，如“王总（经理）”、“李董事长”等等。
在中国谈生意，利用商务宴会等活动，可以多交朋友，增加双方的了解，巩固双方的关系，有时候“干杯”也更容易做成生意。
essays.summary: 讲述在中国商务场合的基本礼仪：握手问候、双手交换名片、名片头衔的重要顺序、介绍他人时职位高低的顺序、称呼对方头衔的习惯，以及利用商务宴会建立关系促成生意。OCR严重受损：'握'应为'握手'、'名'应为'名片'、'别'应为'别人'、'双'应为'双手'等字均有脱落。
essays.why_study: 覆盖中国商务第一接触的核心礼仪语言，握手→名片→介绍→称呼→宴请的完整流程均有专属词汇和句型；把字句和结果补语（做成生意）在此情境中自然呈现。
key_devices: 把字句（将名片给别人）
一般来说引领通则
平行排列（交换→接→介绍→称呼）
动词+成（做成生意）

essays.title: 公司图书馆
essays.text:
许多跨国公司在很多年前就认识到信息的价值和重要性，并建立了自己的公司图书馆。他们知道，只有自己的员工及时了解本行业的最新资讯，才能有机会在竞争中占据优势地位，超过自己的竞争对手。公司图书馆可以为员工提供各种资料，包括图书、杂志、报纸以及音像资料。
城市或大学里的图书馆一般有全职的图书管理员，但是公司的图书馆没必要安排全职的管理员。公司图书管理员的职责范围包括：随时收集公司需要的最新资料，并负责购买和管理这些资料。当然，一个好的图书管理员还应该能够为查找资料和信息的员工提供帮助。
essays.summary: 说明跨国公司建立内部图书馆的价值：帮助员工及时获取行业最新资讯以在竞争中取得优势；介绍图书馆的资料类型（图书、杂志、报纸、音像）以及图书管理员的职责范围（收集、购买、管理资料，协助员工查询）。OCR质量较好。
essays.why_study: 展示正式书面说明文的典型结构；包含大量高频书面连词（并、以及、只有…才、当然）和复合名词（竞争对手、优势地位、职责范围）。
key_devices: 并连接并列动作（认识到…并建立）
只有…才…表必要条件
以及连接并列名词（书面）
排比结构（收集…并负责…）

essays.title: 说说我的公司
essays.text:
（白姗姗：女，2003年11月加入欧华公司）
我来公司以前就听说过欧华公司的企业文化。来了以后发现公司的工作气氛很好，团队合作给了我很深的印象。在一般的企业，不同的部门之间交流比较少，可是我们公司经常组织一些活动，比如不定期的午餐聚会、野餐，还有座谈会和拓展训练等等，为不同部门之间的员工提供了交流的机会。
我们财务部有八个人，大家都很平等。每过一段时间大家就在一起谈谈自己做了哪些工作、有什么问题、怎么解决等。大家互相帮助、互相关心，工作的时候特别愉快。
essays.summary: 欧华公司员工白姗姗以第一人称讲述公司企业文化：良好的工作气氛、跨部门交流活动（不定期午餐聚会、野餐、座谈会、拓展训练），以及财务部内部平等氛围和团队互助精神。OCR质量良好。
essays.why_study: 第一人称商务叙事典范；掌握企业文化描述套语（工作气氛、团队合作、不定期、之间）；语气亲切但词汇规范，是口头介绍公司文化的样板。
key_devices: 以前就+V过（预设期待）
之间表示双向关系
不定期修饰活动（非正式书面语）
每过一段时间表示定期循环

essays.title: 西门子的职业教育
essays.text:
西门的职业培训标是把学习和实践结合起来，让学员出教室，进公司的车间和办公室。
西门合资企业进的现场教学为培训者提供了现代化的设备和真实的作环境。
三年的培训包括两年在职业培训中上课，一年在西门子的一家企业实习。
在培训期间，学员都会得到有经验的师的帮助。职业培训结束后，学员能得到德国商会、中国劳动和社会保障部认可的证书。
（资料来源：西门子管理学院简介）
essays.summary: 介绍西门子在华职业培训项目：目标是将学习与实践结合；三年培训=两年职业培训中心+一年企业实习；结业后获德国商会和中国劳动社保部双认可证书。OCR有字符脱落：'西门'应为'西门子'、'培训标'应为'培训目标'、'真实的作环境'应为'真实的工作环境'等。
essays.why_study: 正式机构说明文样本；把A和B结合起来、在…期间是高频结构；双认可证书的表述展示机构并列的标准写法。
key_devices: 把A和B结合起来
在…期间
包括（枚举范围）
结束后接结果

essays.title: 求职信（范文）
essays.text:
西门子（中国）有限公司人力资源部
尊敬的张经理：
您好！
我叫___，是___国人。我从___开始在北京外国语大学学习___；到目前为止，我已经学习了___个月的生活汉语和___个月的商务汉语，已经能用汉语进行日常交流。
来中国学习汉语以前，我已经有___年的工作经验。我曾经在___公司负责___工作。
我从___看到贵公司的___广告，对___的职位非常感兴趣，希望能有机会与您面谈。随信附上我的个人简历和联系方式。
希望尽快得到您的答复。谢谢！
祝您工作顺利！
求职人：___
2004年8月15日
essays.summary: 中欧青年经理培训项目提供的求职信填写模板，包含完整书信结构：信头（收件人+职位）、问候语、自我介绍（国籍/专业/汉语水平/工作经验）、应聘目的、随信附件、结尾问候及日期。
essays.why_study: 掌握正式中文商务书信的标准结构和套语（尊敬的、随信附上、希望尽快得到答复、祝…顺利）；了解职场中文的敬语层级（您/贵公司）。
key_devices: 尊敬的+职位（不用亲爱的）
贵公司（对方公司的敬称）
随信附上+内容
祝+状态（固定结尾）

essays.title: 求职广告与招聘广告
essays.text:
（一）求职广告
商女士，26岁，外贸英语专业，三年外企公司市场调查工作经验。求合资企业或外企公司市场部管理工作。
手机：13801234567
E-mail: shang26@sina.com.cn

（二）招聘广告
北京今日广告公司诚聘客户服务代表两名。要求：24～35岁，大学毕业，有广告公司客户服务工作经验。男女不限。有意者请把简历和身份证复印件寄到北京市西城区西单北大街678号今日大厦六层今日广告公司。
邮编：100032
E-mail: sun7208@jinri.com.cn
essays.summary: 一则求职广告（个人求职）和一则招聘广告（公司招聘）的对比范例。求职广告极简：性别+年龄+专业+年限+目标岗位+联系方式；招聘广告列出：公司+职位+人数+要求（年龄/学历/经验）+投递方式+地址邮编。
essays.why_study: 广告体的精炼语言：大量省略主语、诚（正式副词）的用法；有意者（者后缀）；把字句在指令性文体中的典型应用。
key_devices: 诚（书面语副词，表示真诚地）
有意者（者后缀=具有某属性的人）
把+宾语+寄到（把字句）
男女不限（省略句）

essays.title: 公司董事会会议对话
essays.text:
（欧华公司的总经理和董事们正在开会）
董事长：今天会议的内容是确定负责营销的副总经理的人选。大家知道，现在的营销副总经理马上就要去北京市政府工作了。我们必须找一个新的副总经理。
总经理：我看，销售部的经理李平就不错。
董事甲：我们也可以考虑招聘一个新的副总经理。
总经理：我们当然可以招聘一个新的副总经理。不过，也可以考虑从公司内部提升合适的人。
董事长（对总经理）：请多谈一谈李平的工作情况。
总经理：李平六年前来到公司，他工作一直很出色，有很好的客户关系，尤其是去年，他为公司拿到了一个很重要的订单。
（董事们讨论后）
董事长：听了总经理的介绍，大家还有什么意见吗？（等了一会）现在大家同意李平做副总经理的请举手。一、二、三、四、五、六、七……全体通过。
essays.summary: 欧华公司董事会确定营销副总经理人选：总经理提议从内部提升销售经理李平（工作出色、客户关系好、拿到重要订单），董事会讨论后举手表决全体通过。展示正式会议语言及职场决策流程。
essays.why_study: 正式会议场景的语言范式：议题陈述、提案、讨论、表决；尤其是…结构；确定人选、内部提升、全体通过是会议汉语核心词汇。
key_devices: 今天会议的内容是…（议题陈述）
我看（非正式提案语气词）
尤其是去年（递进强调）
全体通过（正式表决结果）

essays.title: 请谈谈您的日常工作——采访对话
essays.text:
（《中国商务》杂志记者采访欧华公司的李平副总经理）
记者：李总，请谈谈您的日常工作好吗？
李平：我一般6点起床，然后看看书。
记者：您都看些什么书呢？
李平：看一些营销和管理方面的书。比如，现在看的是《组织行为学》。
记者：您觉得这些书对您的工作有帮助吗？
李平：当然，从这些书里我学到了很多有用的东西。
记者：您什么时候到办公室呢？
李平：我一般8点到办公室，先查一下电子邮件，然后秘书会把一天的日程安排告诉我。
记者：您和其他总裁如何沟通呢？
李平：我们每周有一次总裁办公会。如果我在外地，也要打电话和他们联系。
记者：您在哪儿用午餐呢？
李平：我很喜欢公司的餐厅，不过很少在那儿吃。我经常要和客户到外面去吃午饭。
记者：下午呢？
李平：有时候开会，有时候去厂里看看。现在还要参加每月一次的董事会。
记者：您一般什么时候下班呢？
李平：大概7点吧。不过一般都不能直接回家，还得去参加一些商务晚宴或一些商务活动。
记者：李总，谢谢您接受采访。
李平：不客气。
essays.summary: 《中国商务》杂志对欧华公司营销副总经理李平的日常工作采访：早6点起床读书、8点到办公室查邮件、秘书安排日程、每周总裁办公会、经常与客户外出午餐、下午开会或视察工厂、晚7点下班参加商务晚宴。
essays.why_study: 掌握描述日常工作时间频率的关键副词（一般、经常、有时候、很少、大概）；采访体的问答句型；职场日程词汇（日程安排、总裁办公会、商务晚宴）。
key_devices: 时间频率副词梯度（经常→一般→有时候→很少）
先…然后…（程序列举）
比如引例
不过转折（口语）

essays.title: 商务晚宴对话
essays.text:
（欧华公司的销售部经理正在和一位比利时客户共进晚餐）
经理：您请这边坐。
客户：谢谢。您也请坐。
经理：您的汉语说得真好！
客户：过奖了。
经理：这是菜单，您爱吃什么菜呢？
（点完菜后）
经理：您是比利时什么地方的人？
客户：我是法国人，不是比利时人，不过我现在住在比利时，因为公司在那儿。
经理：是吗？不过很多比利时人都说法语，对不对？
客户：对。一半以上的比利时人都会说法语。
经理：您这次打算在中国呆多长时间？
客户：大概十天吧。六天在北京，其他四天在天津和大连。
经理：大连非常漂亮，而且经济发展很快。
客户：听说不少日本企业在大连的开发区投资办厂，我还听说天津经济的发展不比大连慢。
经理：没错。你们公司在天津的业务怎么样？
客户：发展得相当快。最近一两年华北地区的业务发展速度甚至超过了长江三角洲地区。我听说，贵公司的产品在东南亚地区的销售相当好。
经理：最近一两年确实不错，但是现在家电行业的竞争也越来越激烈了……
essays.summary: 欧华公司销售经理接待比利时客户的商务晚餐对话，涵盖：就座礼节、谦辞（过奖）、菜单、国籍话题、中国城市经济比较（大连/天津/长三角）、双方业务近况。展示商务寒暄→正题过渡的自然流程。
essays.why_study: 掌握商务宴请的话题策略；谦辞套语（过奖了、客随主便）；A不比B慢（否定假设比较）；相当（程度副词，介于很和非常之间）。
key_devices: 过奖了（谦辞回应夸奖）
A+不比+B+adj（否定预设比较）
相当+adj（中高程度副词）
甚至（递进最高点）

essays.title: 研讨会日期改了——电话对话
essays.text:
（一位女士正在接电话）
女：你好！中国-欧盟青年经理培训项目管理办公室。
男：你好！我是中国社会科学院经济研究所。请卫女士接电话。
女：我就是，请讲。
男：我们研究所的王钢教授原来计划6月18日星期二给你们学员作报告，但是星期二那天王钢教授必须参加一个会，不知道能不能把报告的时间改在6月25日星期二？
女：是这样啊，请问王钢教授的报告题目是什么？
男：报告题目是"中国的体制改革和经济发展"。
女：我知道了，社科院王钢教授的报告时间改了。我们安排一下，马上给您回电话。
男：谢谢。希望报告改期不会影响你们的其他活动安排。我等你的电话。我们办公室的电话是65135143，我姓张。
女：65135143，我记下来了。
男：卫女士，谢谢你，再见。
女：别客气，张先生，再见。
essays.summary: 中国社科院来电通知将王钢教授6月18日的报告延期至6月25日（题目：中国的体制改革和经济发展），卫女士确认更改并承诺回电。完整展示商务电话的问候→自报身份→说明来意→确认→道别流程。
essays.why_study: 电话礼仪的标准脚本；改在结构；意义被动句（报告时间改了）；V+下来（记下来）；原来对比现在的句式；如何礼貌表达不确定（不知道能不能…）。
key_devices: 改在/改到+时间（改期）
意义被动句（报告时间改了）
V+下来（记下来）
我就是（接听电话确认身份）

essays.title: 广告研讨会问答
essays.text:
（在中欧青年经理研讨会上，主讲人正在回答学员们的问题）
主讲人：刚才我们讲了中国广告的基本情况。大家还有什么问题吗？
学员一：我有一个问题。请问，您认为在中国做广告最困难的是什么？
主讲人：我认为在中国做广告最困难的是消费者的多样性。中国有13亿人口分布在城市和农村。
学员二：您认为中国的消费者对什么媒体上的广告更感兴趣？
主讲人：报纸、杂志上或电台里的广告都很多，特别是电视上的广告更能吸引农村和城市的消费者，因为中国现在有几千家电视台，从电视上了解产品信息非常方便。
学员三：您刚才说电视上维生素和保健品的广告最多，请问这是为什么？
主讲人：和许多国家一样，中国现在老年人越来越多，他们非常关心自己的健康。
学员四：刚才看了一些饮料的广告，您认为哪个产品的广告做得最好？
主讲人：可口可乐公司的广告做得很不错，他们总是按照中国的各种节日和重要活动推出不同的广告进行促销。比如奥运可乐、圣诞可乐、春节可乐。这些广告既吸引了城市的消费者也吸引了农村的消费者。
essays.summary: 中欧研讨会上广告专题问答：在华广告最大挑战是消费者多样性（城乡13亿人口）；电视广告最能覆盖城乡消费者；维生素保健品广告多因老龄化；可口可乐按节日推出主题广告（奥运/圣诞/春节）既吸引城市也吸引农村消费者。
essays.why_study: 专业研讨会的问答句式（我认为…最…的是、特别是、和许多国家一样）；市场营销核心词汇；按照+依据推出+产品是标准商业表达；既…也…的双覆盖结构。
key_devices: 我认为…最…的是（发表专业意见）
特别是（强调程度最高项）
按照+依据+推出（营销行为描述）
既…也…（双重受益）

essays.title: 企业预算管理与案例分析研讨会——通知
essays.text:
2004年12月13～15日在海南省三亚（四星级酒店）举办"企业预算管理与案例分析研讨会"，希望得到您的支持。

课程提纲：
2004年12月13日  理论主讲：刘晓林
2004年12月14日  案例主讲人：王培建

请每位学员在课程开始前七天，把您的企业案例或问题传真给我们，我们会选择部分问题，请专家教授在培训中为您分析解决。

主讲人背景：
工商大学会计学院教授刘晓林女士
长期从事企业预算管理、成本管理研究。近年来翻译、出版《管理会计学》、《财务报表分析》等近三十本书。

山东能化集团公司财务部副主任、高级会计师王培建先生
山东能化集团公司是国有企业，现有员工6.1万人，企业369家。到2000年底，公司电力总资产达到648亿元。王培建先生长期负责集团财务部工作，有丰富的预算管理经验。

收费：
企业预算管理与案例分析研讨会费用是每人3280元人民币（三天），包括培训费、资料费、住宿费及用餐费。

联系方式：
地址：北京市东城区朝内大街200号
邮编：100010
电话：86-10-65134519
传真：86-10-65132023
联系人：吴军
essays.summary: 2004年12月三亚四星级酒店举办的三天企业预算管理研讨会通知，包含课程提纲（理论+案例两天）、主讲人简历（学者刘晓林：出版约30本书；实务专家王培建：国有集团财务副主任）、费用（3280元/人含食宿）、联系方式及报名回执格式。
essays.why_study: 正式通知文体的完整结构（标题→日期地点→课程→师资→收费→联系）；主讲人简历写法（职位+专业方向+代表成果）；从事+领域是简历必备表达；长期、近年来、达到是正式书面语高频词。
key_devices: 举办（主语为主办单位）
从事+研究/工作（长期从事）
达到+数字（成就量化）
包括…费用（费用明细列举）

essays.title: 中国私营行业的发展情况（会议发言）
essays.text:
主席、同事们、朋友们：
我非常高兴有机会参加今天的会议。今天我想介绍一下中国私营行业的发展情况。我想先说明三个具体的概念。第一个概念是民营行业，它包括私营行业、乡镇企业、外资企业、股份公司和联合公司等；第二个概念是私营行业，它包括私营公司和个体户；第三个概念是私营企业，也就是私营公司，指的是超过8个员工、自然人为股东的企业。
现在我再讲几个私营行业的统计数字。第一个数字是，私营企业现在共占有中国城市GDP的15%；民营行业占全中国GDP的1/3。第二个数字是，城市里的私营企业的销售收入从1991年开始到现在，每年的平均增长率超过50%。第三个数字是，私营企业提供的就业增长率最近5年达到21%，这个增长率是中国城市就业增长率2%的10倍。这些简单的统计数据说明，私营公司在中国未来的经济发展中占有重要地位。
（资料来源：金戈投资公司总裁金永益（Dr. Kim Woodard）的演讲《如何吸引国际风险投资》）
essays.summary: Conference speech defining three key terms (民营行业, 私营行业, 私营企业), then presenting three clusters of statistics, and drawing a conclusion about private enterprise's importance in China's future economy.
essays.why_study: Exemplifies a tight academic/conference speech structure: term definition → statistics → explicit conclusion. The three-part enumeration pattern is a transferable template for any professional presentation or report in Chinese. Demonstrates how to integrate statistics credibly and how to attribute sources.
key_devices: 先说明三个具体的概念 (define terms before arguing)
第一个概念是…第二个…第三个… (tripartite term enumeration)
第一个数字是…第二个数字是… (structured statistics presentation)
这些简单的统计数据说明，… (data → conclusion formula)
对比数字：增长率是2%的10倍 (proportional comparison to dramatize scale)

essays.title: 神龙汽车有限公司介绍
essays.text:
神龙汽车有限公司是中国东风汽车公司和法国标致-雪铁龙汽车集团合资兴建的大型轿车生产企业。合资比例为东风公司31%，标致-雪铁龙公司26.875%，国家开发银行19.5%，中国东方资产管理公司19.5%，法国兴业银行2.5%，法国巴黎银行0.625%。
1992年5月18日，神龙汽车有限公司在武汉市成立。公司主要部门有生产部、质量部、采购部、技术中心、商务部、财务部、人力资源部以及武汉、襄樊两个工厂，现有员工近5000人。
从1996年至今，神龙汽车有限公司边建设边生产，边生产边经营，边经营边开发，产量和销量增长很快，1999年以后，月销量一直稳定在4000辆以上，特别是2003年年销量突破了10万辆。
（资料来源：神龙汽车有限公司网站；263网汽车频道）
essays.summary: Company profile of Shenlong Automobile Co., Ltd. (Dongfeng–PSA joint venture): equity breakdown, founding date and city, departmental structure and headcount, and production/sales milestones from 1996 to 2003.
essays.why_study: Model of Chinese business company profile writing in a fixed sequence: equity structure → founding facts → org structure/headcount → operational trajectory with quantified milestones. The 边…边… triple chaining and 突破 milestone verb are directly reusable.
key_devices: 合资比例为… (equity ratio formula with percentage listing)
边建设边生产，边生产边经营，边经营边开发 (chained 边…边… for virtuous-cycle narrative)
从…至今，…一直稳定在…以上 (sustained achievement framing)
突破 (break through — milestone achievement verb)
现有员工近… (current headcount formula with 近 for approximation)

essays.title: 新欧广告公司组织结构描述
essays.text:
新欧广告公司有三位董事，其中的两位董事不参加公司的日常经营活动。另外的一位董事欧阳力先生是公司的总经理。
欧阳总经理负责公司的财务、客户关系和人力资源三个部门的政策制定。在欧阳总经理手下，有一个办公室主任负责行政工作，办公室主任手下有一位全职秘书和一位接待员负责办公室事务；财务部有一位经理和一位会计；客户关系经理和他手下的六位业务员负责联系客户；人力资源经理负责招聘、培训和员工福利等。
董事们在每月一次的管理例会上常常要作一些重大决策。除了三位董事以外，办公室主任、人力资源经理、财务和客户关系经理也必须参加每月一次的管理例会，并且六位业务员中的一位也常常需要参加。经理们可以提出建议和意见，但重大决策常常由几位董事一起作出。
essays.summary: Description of an advertising company's governance and org chart: board composition, management hierarchy, departmental responsibilities, and monthly meeting governance structure.
essays.why_study: Demonstrates how to describe org chart hierarchies in formal written Chinese. Contains the full toolkit for organizational writing: 其中, 在…手下, 分别负责, 由…作出, 除了…以外. Directly applicable to business reports, company introductions, and meeting minutes.
key_devices: 其中 (among which — partitive pronoun to pick out a subset)
在…手下有… / …手下有… (top-down reporting structure formula)
负责 + noun/VP (be responsible for — assigns portfolio concisely)
由…作出 (formal passive: decisions made by)
除了…以外，…也必须… (inclusive addition: in addition to X, Y must also…)
重大决策 (major decision — formal register collocation)

essays.title: 北京国际汽车博览会广播通知
essays.text:
北京经济台的听众朋友们，你们好！明天，2002年6月10号，第六届北京国际汽车博览会就要在北京国际展览中心举行了。中国、德国、法国和美国等一百多家著名整车制造商带来了一千多辆新车参加这次博览会，包括国内外零部件企业和相关企业，一共有五百多家公司参加了此次展览。
博览会的参观时间是2002年6月10号到20号，上午9点到下午5点。希望参观的朋友们可以在国际展览中心和北京的各个购物中心买到参观票。听众朋友们也可以登录北京国际汽车博览会的网站http://www.bautoexpo.com.cn订票。北京国际汽车博览会欢迎您！
essays.summary: Radio announcement for the 6th Beijing International Auto Show: participants (100+ manufacturers, 500+ companies total), dates, visiting hours, and ticket purchasing channels, closing with a welcome.
essays.why_study: Model of Chinese official/broadcast announcement register. Shows how to compress event information (who, what, when, where, how to participate) into a tight formal announcement. Note 此次 (formal) vs 这次 (neutral), and 就要…了 for imminent future.
key_devices: 听众朋友们，你们好！ (broadcast address formula)
就要…举行了 (imminent event: is about to be held)
一共有…家公司 (total participant count formula)
此次 (formal demonstrative — elevated register vs 这次)
届 (measure word for numbered-edition events)
欢迎您！(formal closing welcome)

essays.title: 兰格公司外贸出口经济新闻播报
essays.text:
听众朋友们，大家好！今天是2004年1月29日，现在是经济新闻时间。根据海关统计数据，有关机构最近公布了2003年中国出口额最多的200家企业的名单。兰格公司以1.9亿美元的出口额排名第189位。另外，其他的一些统计数据也表明，在家电行业，兰格公司的出口额排名第2位。
2003年兰格公司外贸出口继续保持稳定增长，微波炉年出口量首次超过国内市场年销售量。另外，该公司的空调和小家电产品的出口额也有增长。
essays.summary: Economic radio news reporting 2003 export statistics for Lange (兰格), including national ranking by export value, sector ranking in home appliances, year-on-year growth, and a milestone: annual microwave exports exceeding domestic sales for the first time.
essays.why_study: Compact model of Chinese economic/business news language. Shows key patterns for reporting statistics and rankings (以…排名第…位), anaphoric reference (该公司), and milestone language (首次超过). These phrases appear constantly in Chinese business journalism, earnings reports, and press releases.
key_devices: 根据…统计数据 (according to statistical data — source citation formula)
以…的出口额排名第…位 (ranked Nth by/with [metric])
表明 (indicate/demonstrate — formal evidence verb)
继续保持稳定增长 (continue to sustain steady growth — sustained performance formula)
首次超过 (surpassed for the first time — milestone marker)
该公司 (the company in question — formal anaphora in reports)

essays.title: 商务询价留言与供应商回复（传真 / 电子邮件）
essays.genre: written business inquiry and reply (fax / email)
essays.text:
留言一：（给中国茶叶进出口公司）
这是东京国际贸易有限公司。我们想购买中国福牌红茶。请用传真或电子邮件的方式报给我们最低东京港船上交货价，并说明红茶的包装，可提供数量和最早交货期。
公司电话：0081-3-67578921
传真号码：0081-3-67578901
E-mail: tanaka@ittokyo.jp
非常感谢。

留言二：（给巴黎王记服装有限公司）
这是杭州丝绸制品有限公司。贵公司的询价传真前天已经收到。我公司今天上午已经把贵公司需要的唐装目录、报价单、包装标准、交货期等有关材料通过电子邮件发过去了，贵公司需要的唐装样品也已通过DHL寄出。希望在七天的报价有效期内得到贵公司的答复。谢谢。
essays.summary: Two contrasting trade communications: an inquiry (询价) requesting FOB price with packaging and delivery details; and a supplier reply confirming dispatch of catalog, quotation sheet, and samples, with a 7-day validity period for the offer.
essays.why_study: Demonstrates the standard format for Chinese international trade correspondence. The inquiry model shows the required elements (price term, packaging, quantity, delivery date). The reply model shows the confirmation-and-close structure. Both are immediately reusable templates.
key_devices: 这是…公司 (opener: self-identification without 您好 filler)
请用…的方式报给我们… (please use [channel] to quote us…)
并说明…，可提供数量和最早交货期 (and specify packaging, available quantity, and earliest delivery date — the required inquiry checklist)
贵公司 (your esteemed company — polite second-person in correspondence)
有关材料 (relevant materials — formal collective noun)
希望在…有效期内得到…的答复 (hope to receive reply within the validity period)

essays.title: 微波炉询价谈判对话
essays.text:
胡志伟：您好！我是越南河内贸易进出口公司的胡志伟，这是我的名片。我对贵公司生产的微波炉特别感兴趣。
朱广东：谢谢。这是我的名片，我叫朱广东，是销售经理。
胡志伟：我看过贵公司的微波炉展品和商品目录。我很想知道WD900和WP910两种型号的微波炉的广西凭祥友谊关边境交货价。
朱广东：您能不能告诉我贵公司订购的数量以便我们报价。
胡志伟：好的，我会的。不过您现在能不能给我个参考价?
朱广东：请您等一下。（几秒钟后）您看，这是我们最新的WD900和WP910工厂交货价。
胡志伟：贵方报价的有效期是多长时间？
朱广东：有效期是五天。请问您现在订货吗？
胡志伟：那就要看贵方的价格了。如果价格很优惠，我可以马上订货。
朱广东：您请这边坐。我们可以详细谈谈。
essays.summary: First-contact B2B negotiation at a trade fair: buyer expresses interest, requests border delivery price for specific models, seller asks for quantities before quoting, buyer requests a reference price, quote validity is discussed, and buyer gives a conditional commitment.
essays.why_study: Models polite but commercially direct negotiation Chinese: how to express interest (特别感兴趣), request a quote with a specific Incoterm, stall while buying time (请您等一下), ask for validity period, and give a conditional commitment that signals genuine interest while preserving leverage. Note 贵方 (your party — negotiation register) vs 贵公司 (general business correspondence).
key_devices: 以便 (so that — links buyer request to seller benefit)
贵方 vs 贵公司 (贵方 = negotiation/contract register; 贵公司 = general correspondence)
有效期是…天 (validity period formula)
那就要看贵方的价格了 (it depends on your price — conditional leverage formula)
如果价格很优惠，我可以马上订货 (conditional commitment to order)
请您等一下 (polite stall / buying time during negotiation)

essays.title: 工厂参观欢迎致辞
essays.genre: formal welcome address / factory tour opening remarks
essays.text: 欢迎欢迎，欢迎开发区领导和外宾。请让我自我介绍一下，我是服装厂的厂长，我叫李大庆。请大家先跟我到会议室。（过了一会儿）我先给大家介绍一下我们厂的基本情况，然后带大家去参观我们的服装生产车间。大家请坐，请喝茶。现在请大家看个介绍我们工厂发展历程的电视短片。请关灯。（音乐声起，过了一会儿，声音淡出）请开灯。各位领导和外宾，现在我们去生产车间，如果有什么问题，我们可以边走边谈。
essays.summary: A factory director's opening welcome to visiting officials and foreign guests: self-introduction, agenda (briefing → film → factory floor tour), hospitality cues, and the invitation to ask questions during the walk.
essays.why_study: Model of Chinese formal welcoming remarks for a site visit or delegation reception. Shows the fixed agenda-signposting structure (我先…然后…), directional hosting cues, and the gracious closing invitation 边走边谈 (talk as we walk). Applicable to any formal hosted visit.
key_devices: 欢迎欢迎 (doubled welcome — heightened hospitality register)
请让我自我介绍一下 (formal self-introduction opener)
我先…，然后… (sequenced agenda announcement)
各位领导和外宾 (formal collective address for mixed official/foreign audience)
如果有什么问题，我们可以边走边谈 (open-invitation close: ask questions as we go)

grammar.pattern: 把字句：主语 + 把 + 宾语（受事）+ 动词 + 其他
grammar.explanation: 把字句强调施事者对特定受事物的处置，使其发生变化或产生某种结果。动词必须及物且不能单独使用（需带补语、宾语或其他成分）。宾语必须是确定的；否定词/能愿动词置于把前。有、是、知道、认识等动词不能用于把字句。
examples: 有意者请把简历寄到今日广告公司。
他已经把报纸送到了经理办公室。
你可以把材料放在桌子上。（能愿动词在把前）
到现在他还没把报告写完。（否定词在把前）

grammar.pattern: 被字句：主语（受事）+ 被/让/叫 + 宾语（施事）+ 动词 + 其他
grammar.explanation: 被字句的主语为受事；被多用于书面语，让/叫常用于口语。主语须为确定事物；谓语动词须带补语或其他成分说明结果；否定词/能愿动词置于被前；叫/让后须有施事者或人。
examples: 春节前的时间宴会厅差不多都被预订了。
那份简历被他拿走了。
如果你去听讲座，也一定会被吸引的。（能愿动词在被前）
我的车被别人开走了。

grammar.pattern: 意义被动句：主语（受事）+ 动词 + 其他成分
grammar.explanation: 主语为受事，句中无被/让/叫等被动介词。常用于口语；主语须为确定或前文提及的事物；谓语动词须带补语等成分表示结果。
examples: 社科院王钢教授的报告时间改了。
工作报告已经写完了。
博览会的参观票订好了。
开会通知已经发下去了。

grammar.pattern: 把 A 和 B 结合起来
grammar.explanation: 固定搭配，表示将两者融合为一体。起来作结果补语。可替换为把A与B结合起来（正式书面语）。
examples: 西门子的职业培训目标是把学习和实践结合起来。
你应该把工作和休息结合起来。
我们必须把学习内容和学员的需要结合起来。

grammar.explanation: 表示必要条件：只有满足前项，才能实现后项。前项为唯一充分条件。常用于强调竞争优势的获取路径。
examples: 只有自己的员工及时了解最新资讯，才能有机会在竞争中占据优势。
只有提供一流的服务，才能让客户满意。
只有整个团队全力合作，才可以取得项目的成功。

grammar.explanation: 连接两个并列的形容词或动词短语，表示两者兼有。主语不能在既前用并列格式（*既小张又小王去了×）。
examples: 培训既能开一个好头，又能对机会或危机作出反应。
李秘书既聪明又漂亮。
张经理既负责生产部的工作又负责市场部的工作。

grammar.explanation: 表示时间从句，引导某一特定时间点或阶段。口语和书面语均可用。
examples: 当新员工刚来公司的时候，公司都得对他们进行培训。
当你参加面试的时候，一定要自信。
当你去面试的时候，最好穿西装。

grammar.pattern: 数量词 + 以上 / 以下
grammar.explanation: 以上/以下接在数量词后，表示在该数值及其上方/下方的范围。以上含该数值本身（大于等于）。
examples: 很多员工都接受过三次以上的培训。
40岁以上的人找工作很困难。
最近三年，北京冬天的平均气温都在零度以下。

grammar.explanation: 动态助词过用在谓语动词后，表示过去曾有过某种经历。否定形式用没（不能用不）；回答否定时只用没有。
examples: 他来过这个地方。
我学过汉语。
我没看过这本书。
你学过法语吗？——没有。

grammar.pattern: A + 不比 + B + adj 对比 A + 没有 + B + adj
grammar.explanation: 没有+adj：断言A劣于B，adj须为积极意义（好/快/漂亮），不用消极词（差/慢）。不比+adj：否定预设的比较，意为A与B相当或A反而更好，积极或消极adj均可。
examples: 她没有她妹妹漂亮。（A确实不如B）
天津经济的发展不比大连慢。（否定预设：大连不一定更快）
他的汉语水平不比你差。（实际可能更好）
她没有我高。（我确实比她高）

grammar.explanation: 介词，引出行为的目的，置于主语前或主语后。常与才/就连用强调。
examples: 为了满足公司员工的需要，欧华管理学院制订了培训计划。
为了实现今年的销售目标，我们制订了具体方案。
为了参加这次面试，他准备了一个星期。

grammar.explanation: 起来后接动词有三类意义：①表示向上动作（站/拿/提+起来）；②表示动作完成或想到（想/记+起来）；③V+不+起来表示无法实现。
examples: 他站起来和招聘经理说再见。（向上动作）
我想起来了，小张今天不来公司。（回忆完成）
箱子太重了，我提不起来。（无法实现）

grammar.explanation: 下来用在动词后，表示动作的完成或结果，有将信息固定保存的含义。
examples: 我把会议桌的尺寸记下来。
请你把他的电话号码和地址写下来。
报纸上的这篇文章很有意思，我已经复印下来了。

grammar.explanation: 正式书面语连词，连接两个并列的双音节动词或分句，表示更进一层。不同于口语的而且/还；主语须一致。
examples: 许多跨国公司认识到信息的价值并建立了自己的公司图书馆。
董事会讨论并通过了这个合作计划。
他们参观了公司，并和公司员工进行了交流。

grammar.explanation: 正式书面语，连接并列的名词或名词短语（不连接从句）。相当于口语的和，但语气更正式。
examples: 公司图书馆里有图书、报纸、期刊以及音像资料。
旅行社负责预订机票以及安排旅游日程。
培训费用包括培训费、资料费、住宿费及用餐费。

grammar.explanation: 举行：强调事件正在进行；主语常为活动本身（受事），强调时间、地点；不提主办单位。举办：强调主动组织；主语通常为主办单位或个人。
examples: 乒乓球比赛正在举行。（举行：活动本身）
欢迎仪式将在多功能厅举行。（举行：强调地点）
北京将举办2008年奥运会。（举办：主办方主语）
学院为学员们举办了中国文化讲座。（举办：主办单位）

grammar.pattern: 改在 / 改到 + 时间/地点
grammar.explanation: 固定搭配，表示将原定的时间或地点更改为新的时间或地点。两者在实际使用中可互换。
examples: 你知道吗，研讨会改在第二会议室了。
能不能把报告的时间改在6月25日星期二？
我们明天的会改到后天了。

grammar.explanation: 正式书面动词，表示长期从事某专业领域或职业。常见于简历、人物介绍。不用于临时性活动。
examples: 刘晓林女士长期从事企业预算管理、成本管理研究。
她的父母从事教育工作已经20年了。
你从事项目管理工作多长时间了？

grammar.pattern: 边 A 边 B（，边 B 边 C）
grammar.explanation: Two or more simultaneous ongoing actions. Can be chained in triplets to suggest integrated, virtuous-cycle development. More formal/literary than 一边…一边… in business prose; the chained version is a rhetorical device for describing organic corporate growth.
examples: 边建设边生产，边生产边经营，边经营边开发 (while building, simultaneously producing; while producing, operating; while operating, developing) [Unit 8 company profile]
如果有什么问题，我们可以边走边谈 (if you have questions, we can talk as we walk) [Unit 6 factory tour]

grammar.pattern: 自从…（以后/后），…从 X 到 Y
grammar.explanation: Marks a turning point: 'ever since [event], [quantity] changed from X to Y'. The standard Chinese structure for before/after business narratives with quantified results. Pairs naturally with numbers for credibility.
examples: 自从进口了这些生产设备后，我们生产车间的日产量从3500件增加到5000件 (Ever since we imported the new production equipment, daily output increased from 3,500 to 5,000 units) [Unit 6]

grammar.pattern: 只好… (have no choice but to)
grammar.explanation: Expresses reluctant necessity following an adverse situation. Slightly more literary than 不得不 and carries a resigned, matter-of-fact tone. Common in narrative recounting of business difficulties.
examples: 只好让工人回家了 (had no choice but to send the workers home) [Unit 7 taxi driver narrative]
我也只好回家了 (I too had no choice but to go home) [Unit 7]

grammar.pattern: 以便… (so that / in order to facilitate)
grammar.explanation: Formal conjunction of purpose linking a main clause to its enabling reason. Preferred over 这样 in written business Chinese; signals that the action benefits the other party.
examples: 您能不能告诉我贵公司订购的数量以便我们报价 (Could you tell us your order quantity so that we can provide a quote) [Unit 9 negotiation]

grammar.explanation: Partitive pronoun picking out a subset from a previously mentioned group. Extremely high-frequency in business descriptions, org charts, and statistical reporting. Avoids repeating the noun.
examples: 新欧广告公司有三位董事，其中的两位董事不参加公司的日常经营活动 (Xin'ou has three directors; of them, two do not participate in daily operations) [Unit 8]
六位业务员中的一位也常常需要参加 (one of the six salespeople also often needs to attend) [Unit 8]

grammar.explanation: Describes organizational reporting structure. 在 X 手下 = reporting to / under X; X 手下有 Y = X has Y reporting to them. The two forms can alternate in an org description to cascade hierarchy without repeating the same structure.
examples: 在欧阳总经理手下，有一个办公室主任负责行政工作 (Under GM Ouyang, there is an office director handling administration) [Unit 8]
我手下有四个副经理 (I have four deputy managers reporting to me) [Unit 8 radio interview]

grammar.pattern: 由…作出 / 由…负责 (passive with agent: done/decided/handled by)
grammar.explanation: 由 introduces the agent in a formal passive construction, especially for decisions, responsibilities, and assignments. More formal than 被; used when the agent is a person or body making a decision, not suffering an action.
examples: 重大决策常常由几位董事一起作出 (Major decisions are usually made jointly by the directors) [Unit 8]

grammar.pattern: 分别负责 A、B、C 和 D (respectively responsible for A, B, C, D)
grammar.explanation: 分别 + same verb distributes a role across multiple people/departments without repeating the verb. The standard formula for org chart bullet-list descriptions in Chinese.
examples: 这四个副经理分别负责招聘、培训与发展、劳资关系和福利待遇 (The four deputy managers are respectively responsible for recruitment, training & development, labor relations, and benefits) [Unit 8]

grammar.explanation: Concluding formula for data-driven argument. Summarize the data, then use 说明 + clause to state the conclusion. Core pattern for analytical writing and speeches; prevents the reader from having to infer the conclusion.
examples: 这些简单的统计数据说明，私营公司在中国未来的经济发展中占有重要地位 (These simple statistics demonstrate that private companies occupy an important position in China's future economic development) [Unit 7 speech]

grammar.explanation: Standard formula for citing rankings tied to a quantified metric. Always uses 以 to introduce the metric and 排名第N位 for the rank. More precise than simply saying 第N名.
examples: 兰格公司以1.9亿美元的出口额排名第189位 (Lange ranked 189th with an export value of USD 190 million) [Unit 9 news broadcast]

grammar.pattern: 继续保持…增长 (continue to maintain/sustain … growth)
grammar.explanation: Standard business news and earnings report phrase for sustained performance. 继续 + 保持 + [modifier] + 增长 is a fixed collocation. Modifiers: 稳定 (steady), 快速 (rapid), 显著 (notable).
examples: 2003年兰格公司外贸出口继续保持稳定增长 (In 2003, Lange's foreign trade exports continued to maintain steady growth) [Unit 9 news]

grammar.explanation: Standard adversative concession. In spoken/business narrative 可是 is common; 然而 or 但 are more formal in written reports. Frequently used to acknowledge one positive before raising a complication.
examples: 虽然钱拿得比服装厂多，可是常常要跑外地 (Although the pay was better than at the garment factory, I frequently had to travel out of town) [Unit 7]

grammar.pattern: 除了…以外，…也必须/也… (in addition to X, Y also must…)
grammar.explanation: Inclusive addition: expands a list to include more participants or items. Unlike 除了…之外不… (exclusive), this construction adds to a group rather than subtracting from it. Key for listing meeting attendees or required actions.
examples: 除了三位董事以外，办公室主任、人力资源经理、财务和客户关系经理也必须参加每月一次的管理例会 (In addition to the three directors, the office director, HR manager, finance and client relations managers must also attend the monthly meeting) [Unit 8]

expr.pinyin: xīwàng jǐnkuài dédào nín de dáfù
expr.meaning: hope to receive your reply as soon as possible
wishing you success in your work [letter closing]

pay by card; swipe a card

price inquiry; to inquire about price (first step in trade negotiation)
counter-offer (buyer's response to seller's initial quote)

quotation sheet (formal written price quote document)
validity period (of a quote, contract, passport, etc.)

Ex Works (EXW) — buyer collects from factory gate
Free On Board (FOB) — seller delivers to named port
Sight Letter of Credit — payment on presentation of documents

market share

venture capital
go bankrupt; shut down (of a business) — direct, not euphemistic
transnational / multinational corporation
state-owned enterprise (SOE)
self-employed individual / sole proprietor (Chinese legal category)
township and village enterprise (TVE) — China-specific economic category
holding subsidiary
major / significant decision (fixed collocation in governance contexts)
labor-management relations
institutional / systemic reform
on-the-job training
your party / your side — used in negotiations and formal contracts; more protocol-specific than 贵公司
your esteemed company — polite second-person reference in general business correspondence
you get what you pay for; the higher the price, the better the quality (proverb)
as always; as before — formal, often used in business letters to emphasize consistency of service or cooperation
out of the ordinary; distinctive; set apart from the rest
up to now / to date — formal, stronger and more written than 到现在
the company in question; said company — formal anaphoric reference in reports and journalism (not 这家公司 or 它)
for the first time — formal milestone marker (more formal than 第一次 in reports)
continue to maintain steady growth (fixed collocation for sustained business performance)
indicate, show, demonstrate — formal evidence verb; used to introduce data-backed conclusions
under the authority/supervision of (someone); reporting to
