他在《梅兰芳的舞台艺术》一书中总结了一整套舞台艺术理论，提出戏剧反映生活要在"似与不似"之间；应重神似而不重形似；演员在表演时，必须深入角色，忘掉自我，把握人物情感的发展层次。他还提出戏剧要顺从时代发展的要求，在不断创新中求得发展。

梅兰芳最擅演旦角，在长达半个世纪的舞台生活中，他塑造了众多艺术形象，如《白蛇传》中为追求爱情而同邪恶势力搏斗的白娘子；《打渔杀家》中随父英勇报仇的渔家姑娘萧桂英；《黛玉葬花》中的林黛玉；《贵妃醉酒》中的杨玉环，还有巾帼英雄穆桂英、梁红玉、虞姬等。这些个性鲜明的艺术形象体现了中国妇女各种良好的品德和温柔、含蓄、潇洒、高雅的特征。在精心塑造这些优美的艺术形象的过程中，他运用唱、念白、身段、舞蹈等技巧，把人物的心理状态刻画入微，表演自然、和谐、质朴、俏丽，极富节奏感和内在魅力，达到了炉火纯青的境界。

梅兰芳在艺术上的卓越成就引起了国外戏剧界的广泛关注和重视，并享有很高的声誉。俄国著名的戏剧理论家斯坦尼斯拉夫斯基观看了梅兰芳的京剧表演之后指出："中国京剧的表演，是一种有规律的自由动作。"既"有规律"，又能运用"自由"，这恐怕是戏剧表演中的最高境界了。德国戏剧家布莱希特也承认他的一些广泛被各国戏剧界所接受的理论是受了梅兰芳表演艺术的启示。梅兰芳先后带团多次应邀到日本、美国、前苏联等国家演出，增进了各国人民对中国古典戏剧艺术的了解，也为中外艺术史上作出了巨大的贡献。
essays.summary: Biographical portrait of Mei Lanfang (1894–1961), covering his origins, training, artistic achievements, theoretical contributions, the roles he created, and his international influence on world theatre.
essays.why_study: A model biographical essay that moves from chronological facts to thematic assessment. It shows how to build a portrait in layers: birth/training → art theory → specific roles → international reception. The phrasing for praising achievement (起了承前启后的作用, 炉火纯青的境界, 享有国际盛誉) is highly reusable in professional and academic Chinese. The technique of citing foreign authorities (Stanislavski, Brecht) to validate Chinese art is a recurring device in formal Chinese writing.
key_devices: Opening summary sentence states the thesis: life span + career achievement + historical role + title
Chronological micro-biography: 出身 → 师从 → 8岁 → 10岁 → 戏路
Aphorism quotation from his own theory: "似与不似"之间 — abstract principle made vivid
Exemplification through role list: five specific characters from famous operas cited with context
Four-element climactic list: 自然、和谐、质朴、俏丽 — registers precision
Foreign-authority endorsement: Stanislavski and Brecht used to establish global legitimacy
Chengyu as culminating judgement: 炉火纯青的境界

essays.title: 政府上网
essays.text:
当今社会，网络与人们的联系越来越密切。"个人网"、"公司网"、"政府网"等层出不穷。可以说，人们的生活已越来越离不开网络。

中华人民共和国中央人民政府门户网站，简称"中国政府网"，于2006年1月1日正式开通。它成为继"中国科技网"、"中国计算机网"、"中国教育网"、"中国信息网"之后的中国第五大网络。中国政府网是中国国务院和国务院各部门，以及各省、自治区、直辖市人民政府在国际互联网上发布政府信息和提供在线服务的综合平台。中国各级政府及其工作部门在各自的网站上以图文并茂的形式，介绍其组织结构设置、职能配置，发布有关公共事务管理的法律、法规以及政策文件，开办公共事务的成就展览。有些政府机关甚至还在网站上办公，如税务总局的网站接受单位及个人通过互联网络申报纳税，教育部授权北京市高校通过网络招生，等等。

中国政府网是政府面向社会的窗口，是公众与政府互动的渠道，对于促进政务公开、推进依法行政、接受公众监督、改进行政管理、全面履行政府职能具有重要意义。这其中最重要的有两点：

首先，它为改善政府与公民关系提供了基础。政府上网，可以使公民能够通过网络随时了解政府，了解它的组织结构和职能配置，了解有关公共事务管理的相应法律、法规和政策文件。更多的熟悉、更多的了解，意味着更多的信任，这无疑是政府争取公民信任、提高自己合法性水平的重要途径。

其次，它为公民监督各级政府遵守法律，实施法治政府、法治行政提供了充分的机会。公民要监督政府是否守法，首先需要知道政府应该遵守什么法律。过去，中国各级政府作出了很大的努力，进行广泛的法制宣传，但是，这种宣传具有临时性、局部性特点，没有长期的效应。而公民由于没有实际的需要，也没有积极性去关心。一旦需要了解特定的公共事务方面有哪些法律时，往往难以找到适当的途径。政府全方位上网，公民极其容易在网上找到有关法律依据，这样当公民认为自己的权益受到非法侵害时，就可以轻易地以法律为武器，保护自己的合法权益。

政府上网，政务公开，为政府改善与公民的关系、加强法治政府建设提供了充分的机会。然而，要使政府网充分发挥其作用，还有大量的工作要做。

首先是要转变观念。政府上网的重要目的之一是把政府的大部分有价值的信息资源放到网上，使其最大限度地得到共享，并实现政务公开，接受民主监督。这样做恐怕对许多政府机关工作人员来说，一时会难以适应，因为这是在向旧观念挑战。

第二，制度创新。政府部门工作制度化和规范化以及如何为群众提供服务性办事程序是一个很重要的问题。我们政府现在很多工作过程人治因素较重，随机性较强。为推进政府上网的顺利进行，有必要对现行的政府部门内部管理制度进行创新，加强政府部门工作的制度化、规范化、程序化。

第三，提高信息化能力。政府工作人员都应具有一定的信息化能力，即相应的信息操作能力与处理能力。由于我国信息化工程起步较晚，因此政府工作人员信息化意识弱，信息化能力普遍较低。

第四，资金、技术需加大投入。政府上网是一个复杂的、长期的系统工程。要使政府网站能长期良好地运行，必须有资金保障。另外，相关的技术问题也不容忽视。

政府上网是政府办公模式与观念上的一次革命，将为国家信息产业的健康发展创造一个良好的环境，同时对促进国家政治、经济和文化的发展会产生深远的影响。
essays.summary: Argues that China's e-government portal (launched 2006) improves citizen-government relations and rule of law, then identifies four challenges: changing mind-sets, institutional innovation, digital capacity, and funding/technology investment.
essays.why_study: A model of Chinese policy argumentation: phenomenon → significance (two key points) → problems → numbered solutions → grand conclusion. The two-part significance section (首先…其次) is a reusable template. The essay shows how to concede past efforts before showing their inadequacy (作出了很大的努力…但是…没有长期的效应). Excellent for professional and academic register: 推进依法行政, 最大限度地得到共享, 具有重要意义.
key_devices: Numerical scaffolding: 首先/其次 for arguments; 第一/第二/第三/第四 for problem list
Concession-rebuttal: 过去政府作出了很大努力…但是…没有长期的效应
Definitional anchor: 中国政府网是政府面向社会的窗口，是公众与政府互动的渠道
Legal-rights rhetoric: 以法律为武器，保护自己的合法权益
Escalating conclusion: from specific platform to 产生深远的影响 on national politics/economy/culture
Bureaucratic register vocabulary: 政务公开, 依法行政, 履行职能, 制度化/规范化/程序化

essays.title: 减肥的方法
essays.text:
肥胖，如今已成为困扰许多人的一种疾病。专家认为，肥胖症是越来越严重的流行病，如果不及早控制，它将成为全球性的重要健康问题。

怎样判断一个人是否肥胖呢？一个简易的计算方法是：标准体重（公斤）=身高（厘米）-100。通常把超过正常体重10%称为超重，超过20%就认为是肥胖。病态肥胖是指超过理想体重100%或更多。现在很多临床医生开始采用体重指数（BMI）诊断肥胖。BMI的计算方法为：BMI=体重（公斤）÷身高（米）的平方。BMI等于或低于25为正常；25-26.9为超重；27-29.9为肥胖；30-34.9为中度肥胖；35-39.9为重度肥胖；大于40为病态肥胖。

常见的治疗肥胖的方法有节食、锻炼、药物、手术。这些方法可以单独使用，也可以配合使用，但不管怎样，都必须长期坚持才会有效，才能取得最后的成功。

饮食调节主要是通过食用低脂、低热量食物，如蔬菜、豆类等，以降低体重。如果确实需要饮食治疗，应请临床医生或营养师帮助，让他们来指导制订饮食疗法方案。医疗人员首先应该了解病人是否想改变自己的饮食情况，并且对现有食谱进行评价，然后医生提出一个缓慢、循序渐进的方案，并针对每个人的具体情况制订不同的营养食谱。大多数人都知道他们应该吃哪些食物，问题是他们要么没有注意到自己正在吃哪些食物，要么是没有找到合适的食谱。从长远来看，饮食治疗可能比药物治疗更经济。但对许多肥胖患者来说，想单独通过饮食控制达到长期减肥的目的往往会失败。

许多医学专家研究发现，短期锻炼的减肥效果并不理想。中等强度的体育锻炼也并不能在短期内（几天到几周）使体重明显下降。此外，肌肉组织在脂肪组织减少的同时可能保持不变，甚至增加，这些因素都可影响减肥效果，从而使减肥者信心受到伤害。实际上，只有坚持长期的减肥运动，加上饮食控制，才能提高减肥的成功率。专家认为，运动疗法的标准是每天运动时间不少于30分钟，每周不少于4天，每次运动强度约为最大运动量的40%-60%。

由于人们已经认识到体重的增加与患病率和死亡率的上升有关，而许多人通过标准饮食和锻炼又达不到减肥目的，对这类患者就有必要进行药物减肥。但目前还没有足够的研究资料表明药物治疗对肥胖患者的效果，且长期服用减肥药的安全性问题也还在研究之中，因此使用减肥药时一定要注意。在中国有人采用中药减肥、针灸减肥和气功减肥，有一定的疗效。

目前市场上可以见到很多减肥茶、减肥药，但多数并没有确实可靠的疗效，有的还具有明显的副作用，因此在选择这类产品时最好先找专科医生咨询一下。

肥胖患者通常情况下不需要手术治疗，可采取脂吸术减肥，但脂肪组织有时会在同一位置重新聚集，或者聚集在其他地方，而且脂吸术对严重病态肥胖患者并不适用，所以在必要情况下才可以考虑手术治疗，手术治疗可以产生长期的减肥效果。目前胃减容手术的成功率在60%-70%之间，平均减肥达30%。许多患者手术后糖尿病、高血压和高血脂病症得到改善。但手术治疗有一定副作用，所以医生和患者在选择手术减肥这一方法时要仔细考虑。一般来说，不到非手术不可的程度，不要采用手术方法。
essays.summary: Systematic review of obesity: definition criteria (BMI scale), four treatment approaches (diet/exercise/medication/surgery), the limitations of each, and a clear hierarchy of intervention from least to most invasive.
essays.why_study: A model of pragmatic Chinese science writing. Shows how to present technical content accessibly: define → criteria → enumerate methods → evaluate each in turn → conclude with caveats. The structure (常见的…方法有…这些方法可以…但不管怎样…都…) is reusable for any enumeration of options. Also shows careful hedge language: 并不理想, 还没有足够的研究资料表明, 有一定的疗效 — essential for credible professional and academic writing.
key_devices: Question-as-section-opener: 怎样判断一个人是否肥胖呢？ — signals a new sub-topic
Formula presentation: BMI expressed as equation, then tiered numeric scale
Four-item enumeration as essay spine: 节食/锻炼/药物/手术 — each gets its own section
要么…要么… (either…or…) for exhaustive alternative listing of patient behaviors
只有…才… to assert the necessary condition for success
Epistemic hedges for academic tone: 并不理想, 有一定的疗效, 一定要注意
Closing caution principle: 不到非手术不可的程度，不要采用 — puts the most invasive option last

essays.title: 人民币
essays.text:
1999年10月1日，在中华人民共和国成立50周年之际，中国人民银行决定发行第五套人民币，首先发行100元券。第五套人民币共有100元、50元、20元、10元、5元、1元、5角及1角八种面额，其中100元、50元、20元、10元、5元为纸币，5角及1角为硬币，1元有纸币和硬币两种。

《中华人民共和国中国人民银行法》规定："中华人民共和国的法定货币是人民币。"

1948年12月1日，随着第一套人民币的发行，中国人民开始有了自己统一的货币。半个多世纪以来，中国的货币制度不断发展和完善。从1952年第二套人民币开始，发行了人民币硬币。从此新中国货币进入纸币、硬币同时流通的时代。1962年发行的第三套人民币是中国首次完全独立设计与印制的一套货币。1987年，为适应经济发展和人民生活的需要，中国人民银行在发行第四套人民币时调整了货币结构，增发了50元、100元大面额的人民币，进一步丰富了货币的品种。中国努力抑制通货膨胀，防止通货紧缩，保持了人民币币值的稳定，并且努力保持国际收支平衡，保持了人民币汇率的稳定。从1993年开始，人民币由禁止出入境改为限额管理。人民币在中国社会主义经济建设和人民生活中发挥了重要作用。

货币是一个国家的"名片"。它既是价值尺度、流通手段，又是反映民族文化和精神风貌的艺术品，是一个国家综合实力的体现，是一国政治、经济、文化、艺术、科技等的综合反映。发行第五套人民币就是中国社会稳定、经济发展、文化艺术繁荣、科技进步等的有力证明。

改革开放以来，中国国民经济持续、快速、健康发展。经济发展速度在世界上名列前茅，社会对现金的需求量也日益增大。经济发展的客观形势对人民币的数量与质量、总量与结构都提出了新要求。货币制度需要随着经济发展变化的实际情况进行适时调整。中国第四套人民币的设计、印制始于改革开放之初，由于当时条件的限制，第四套人民币本身存在一些不足之处，如防伪措施简单，不利于人民币的反假，缺少机读性能，不利于自动化处理等等。正是由于存在这些问题，才迫切需要发行第五套人民币。

与第四套人民币相比，第五套人民币具有鲜明的特点：首先，它是由中国人民银行首次完全独立设计与印制的货币，经专家论证，其印制技术已经达到了国际先进水平。第二，第五套人民币各种面额纸币正面均采用毛泽东同志建国初期的头像，底衬采用中国著名花卉图案，背面主景图案分别选用了人民大会堂（100元券）、布达拉宫（50元券）、桂林山水（20元券）、长江三峡（10元券）、泰山（5元券）、杭州西湖（1元券）。这些有代表性的图案充分表现了中国悠久的历史和壮丽的山河，具有鲜明的民族特色。第三，第五套人民币的主景人像、水印、面额数字都比以前大，便于识别。第四，第五套人民币应用了先进的科学技术，在防伪性能和适应货币处理现代化方面都有了较大提高。

总之，第五套人民币是一套科技含量高、具有较鲜明民族特色的货币。

第五套人民币采用一次公布，分次发行的办法。这样做，一方面继承了前四套的发行传统；另一方面也是借鉴了其他国家的成功经验，有利于货币的替换和衔接。
essays.summary: History and significance of the RMB from its founding in 1948 through the five series, explanation of why the fifth series was needed, and detailed account of its distinguishing features, design philosophy, and issuance strategy.
essays.why_study: A model of official Chinese institutional writing. Shows the historical-narrative-to-features structure used in policy documents and corporate communications: context/background → historical development → problems with current state → new solution → features of new solution → conclusion. The 货币是一个国家的"名片" metaphor is a reusable pattern for elevating any concrete object to symbolic status. 一方面…另一方面… for justification of decisions appears in boardroom and government register alike.
key_devices: Legal citation as authoritative ground: 《…法》规定…
Chronological spine: 1948/1952/1962/1987/1999 — each date triggers a development
Metaphor for elevation: 货币是一个国家的"名片"
既…又…/是…也是… to add multiple attributes to one subject
Problem-solution pivot: 正是由于存在这些问题，才迫切需要…
Numbered feature list: 首先/第二/第三/第四 with varying types of evidence per point
总之 + 一方面…另一方面… as two-part conclusion

essays.title: 南锣鼓巷
essays.genre: cultural geography essay / travel writing (文化地理散文)
essays.text:
南锣鼓巷位于北京什刹海右边，南北走向，长约800米，靠西各有8条胡同整齐排列着。清代南锣鼓巷曾叫罗锅巷，乾隆十五年（1750年）改称为南锣鼓巷。

现在的南锣鼓巷街区，北边是鼓楼东大街，南边是地安门东大街，西边是地安门外大街，东边是交道口南大街。由这4条街围合的这块长方形地块，是元大都的两个坊。以南锣鼓巷为界，东面是"昭回坊"，西边是"靖恭坊"，南锣鼓巷就是两坊间的分界巷。

南锣鼓巷地区是北京城内的一片居民区，与皇城只有一街之隔，已有700多年的历史。胡同、四合院、绿树，相对于红墙金瓦的主流文化，这里是砖灰瓦，呈现出另一种独特的民间色彩。

南锣鼓巷东西各有8条胡同，从南向北，西面的8条胡同是福祥胡同、裱衣胡同、雨儿胡同、帽儿胡同、景阳胡同、沙井胡同、黑芝麻胡同、前鼓楼苑胡同；东边的8条胡同是炒豆胡同、板厂胡同、东棉花胡同、北兵马司胡同、秦老胡同、前圆恩寺胡同、后圆恩寺胡同、菊儿胡同。每一条胡同都有着丰厚的文化积淀，每一所宅院里都诉说着老故事。

[Notable residences: 齐老胡同35号 was a Qing official's flower garden called绮园; 帽儿胡同35、37号 was the pre-wedding residence of the last Qing empress 婉容; 后圆恩寺胡同7号, originally a Qing prince's residence, later sold to a French buyer, features a blend of Chinese and Western architecture; 后圆恩寺胡同13号 is the former residence of the novelist 茅盾; 雨儿胡同13号 is where the master ink-wash painter 齐白石 once lived.]

如今，南锣鼓巷既是让老北京人能够回忆起往事的地方，也是年轻人休闲逛街常选之地，近几年它又成为许多时尚杂志报道的热点，许多国外游客也把它列为北京的必游景点。

这里汇聚了众多特色小店，吃、穿、玩、用全有。如充满老北京记忆的喜鹊咖啡馆；可以一边品味咖啡一边欣赏，甚至可以动手制作陶制品的玩天陶社；能吃到北京最好吃的奶酪的大宇奶酪店；女孩子进去就出不来的百分百杂货店；还有能找到与摄影相关的道具和黑白照片、各式相机的"这里"；放映上个世纪80年代经典老片的老港客电影馆……

南锣鼓巷，北京古都风貌中一块保存完整的"碧玉"，也是新北京一条有着非常特色的街巷，是至今保护最完整的四合院区。它融会了传统与现代流行时尚元素，成为越来越多国外友人和时尚人士的休闲场所，为北京又增添了一道亮丽的风景。
essays.summary: Introduction to Nanluo Drum Lane (南锣鼓巷) in Beijing: location/history, its 16 side alleys, notable historical residences (Empress Wanrong, Mao Dun, Qi Baishi), and its current role as a trendy destination blending old Beijing with modern boutiques.
essays.why_study: Excellent model of place-based writing moving from geography → history → human stories → present vitality. The technique of personifying alleys (每一所宅院里都诉说着老故事) and building emotional resonance through famous residents is directly imitable for any cultural/travel writing. The closing 碧玉 metaphor + 融会了传统与现代…成为…为北京又增添了一道亮丽的风景 is a recurring pattern in Chinese writing about revitalized historic districts.
key_devices: Orientation before description: cardinal directions + bordering streets establish spatial frame
Historical naming: tracing the name change from 罗锅巷 → 南锣鼓巷 adds depth
Contrast of materials as cultural contrast: 红墙金瓦 vs. 砖灰瓦 encodes class/identity
Anaphora: 每一条胡同…每一所宅院… — parallel structure builds rhythm
Personification: 宅院里都诉说着老故事
Three-audience enumeration: 老北京人/年轻人/国外游客 — shows broad appeal
Closing metaphor + synthesis: 碧玉 image + 融会了传统与现代

grammar.explanation: Corrective contrast: 'not X but (rather) Y'. Rejects a surface reading and supplies a deeper reality. Creates emphasis through negation before assertion. Common in both literary and expository prose.
examples: 他不是为了写作去接近那些普通人，从他们的生活中去寻找素材，而是认为自己应该为他们尽心尽力，自己就是他们的亲人。（老舍先生二三事）
我不再只注意增加新的汉语知识，而是把汉语当做工具去了解中国社会、文化、历史和文学。（学习汉语的乐趣）

grammar.pattern: 与其…不如… / 与其说…倒不如说…
grammar.explanation: 'Rather than X, it is better to Y' or 'Rather than saying X, it is more accurate to say Y'. Introduces a reframing or correction. Formal register, effective for nuanced argumentation and self-reflection.
examples: 与其说我回家乡工作是为了更好地照顾爸妈，倒不如说是他们更好地照顾了我！（打进领带里的女儿心）

grammar.explanation: 'Not only X, but also Y'. Standard additive escalation. 而且 adds an equal or greater fact; 还 adds something supplementary. Both are standard written register.
examples: 老舍先生不仅亲自讲课，还亲自解决戏曲艺人、国画家、盲人的生活困难。（老舍先生二三事）
学汉语带给我的收获还不仅如此。（学习汉语的乐趣）

grammar.explanation: 'As X increases/changes, Y…'. Expresses parallel or consequential change. Very common in expository and business prose when describing trends.
examples: 随着汉语水平的提高，我将能用所掌握的汉语知识去阅读和欣赏中国的文学作品了。（学习汉语的乐趣）
随着中国与世界各国友好交往的不断增强，汉语的使用范围会越来越广。（你了解汉语吗）

grammar.pattern: 何况 (let alone; how much more so)
grammar.explanation: Used after establishing a milder case to argue that a stronger case must follow logically. Formal register. Creates rhetorical escalation without stating the stronger conclusion explicitly.
examples: 鸟是如此，何况人类？相伴相守到白头，这是生命的一种幸福。（十姐妹）

grammar.pattern: 据…（研究/统计/资料/观测）显示/介绍/报道
grammar.explanation: Evidence-citing formula: 'According to [source], …'. Essential for formal expository, journalistic, and academic writing.
examples: 据观测，一公顷的阔叶林，每天可以吸收1000公斤左右的二氧化碳。（地球之肺）
据测算，中国因水资源缺乏给经济发展造成的损失每年都在2300亿元以上。（水！水！水！）

grammar.pattern: 使得 / 造成…的现象/后果
grammar.explanation: 'Causing / resulting in'. 使得 is slightly more formal than 使; 造成 carries negative connotation (causing damage). Both link cause to consequence.
examples: 大规模的砍伐和森林大火，使得世界上的森林正以每年1800—2000万公顷的速度消失。（地球之肺）
城市用水需求增长太快，不得不大量使用农业用水，造成工农业互相争水。（水！水！水！）

grammar.pattern: 心中没有…，怎么能… (rhetorical question for emphasis)
grammar.explanation: Rhetorical question implying 'if X is absent, Y is impossible'. Creates emotional force while making a logical point. Typical of passionate speech or elevated prose.
examples: 心中没有祖国，怎么能写出好作品来！（老舍先生二三事）

grammar.explanation: 'Must X and also Y' / 'both X and Y'. Expresses dual obligation or compound attribute. Common in instructive or prescriptive writing.
examples: 他认为：既要写大作品，也要写通俗文艺作品。（老舍先生二三事）
在科学上，你既要从整体上考虑问题，也要从局部考虑问题。（怎样解决难题）

grammar.explanation: 'Only if/by doing X can Y happen'. Expresses a necessary condition. Very common in policy and argument essays asserting that a particular path is the only viable one.
examples: 只有开源与节流并举，中国才能摆脱贫水的困扰，经济才能得到可持续发展。（水！水！水！）

grammar.explanation: 'X has become the only path to Y'. Fixed formal expression for asserting that a course of action is both necessary and inevitable. Strong rhetorical close for argumentative essays.
examples: 保护森林、挽救森林、扩大森林，已成为人类保护自己生存的必由之路。（地球之肺）

grammar.explanation: Ordinal enumeration for presenting multiple solutions or aspects: 'First, … Second, …'. Clean, formal, and unmistakably structured. Preferred in policy, report, and business writing over 第一/第二 in body paragraphs.
examples: 其一，开源——修建大规模的引水、供水、蓄水工程。……其二，节流。（水！水！水！）

grammar.explanation: 'Although X, nevertheless Y'. Standard concessive. 却 is slightly more literary and emphatic than 但是. Both are used in written and spoken registers.
examples: 我俩语言交流虽然暂时有困难，但彼此却心心相印。（在中国安家）
中国森林覆盖率虽然从建国初期的8.6%增加到20.36%，但由于人口多，人均占有森林面积仅为0.145公顷。（地球之肺）

grammar.explanation: Emphasizes that a specific cause is precisely what produces the result. Stronger than 因为…所以…; conveys 'it is exactly X and nothing else that accounts for Y'. High register; common in formal editorials and arguments.
examples: 正是人们对舒适生活和金钱无休止的追求，才造成了对藏羚羊的大量捕杀。
正是这种特殊气候条件，才使茅台酒有了独一无二的香气。

grammar.explanation: Pivot phrase meaning 'from this we can see / therefore we conclude'. Places a logical inference after a block of evidence. Formal; essential in expository and argumentative writing.
examples: 由此可见，汉族是因汉朝而得名。
由此可见，这些不出门的人，周末多与电视、书报和音乐为伴。

grammar.pattern: 随着…的（不断）发展/进步/提高，…
grammar.explanation: Standard expository opening or transition: 'as X has progressed/developed, Y has resulted'. Nearly obligatory in formal Chinese writing about social or scientific trends.
examples: 随着科学技术的不断进步和人们生活条件的日益改善，中国的人口死亡率大大降低。
随着人们保护野生动物意识的提高，藏羚羊一定会得到有效的保护。

grammar.explanation: Progressive addition: 'not only X but also Y (and even Z)'. 而且 adds equal weight; 甚至 escalates to surprising or extreme degree.
examples: 臭氧层的破坏不仅对人类，而且对动植物也会产生严重的危害。
茅台酒不仅五次蝉联国家金奖，而且连续四次荣获国际金奖。

grammar.explanation: Simultaneous double attribute: 'both X and Y at the same time'. More literary than 也…也…. Common in descriptions of products, lifestyles, or policies that balance two qualities.
examples: 他们要求享受的是一个既经济、轻松，又多少能增长些知识的假日。
绿色洗衣机既可清洁衣服，又可消毒灭菌。

grammar.pattern: 由于…，导致/造成/使得…（进而…）
grammar.explanation: Formal cause-effect chain. 由于 is more formal than 因为; 导致/造成 imply negative consequences; 进而 extends the chain to a secondary effect. Essential in analytical writing.
examples: 由于日益严重的全球荒漠化问题不断威胁着人类的生存，联合国通过决议将6月17日定为「世界防治荒漠化和干旱日」。
由于工业废弃物对水、土壤的污染，造成了水产品、农作物被污染，进而影响了人们的食品安全。

grammar.explanation: Standard conditional. Adding 那么 makes the consequence more formal and deliberate. Common in analytical scenarios and policy writing.
examples: 如果臭氧层减少25%，海洋中初级生物产量将减少10%。
如果我们现在还不对藏羚羊的捕杀采取措施，那么在不远的将来，我们的后代只能在博物馆看到藏羚羊了。

grammar.pattern: 据（调查/报道/统计/研究）表明/显示，…
grammar.explanation: Citation formula for data or reported facts. 据 + source + 表明/显示 + clause. Indispensable in journalistic and academic Chinese; signals evidence-based argumentation.
examples: 据调查，中国700余条河流中水质良好的仅剩32.2%。
据报道，到2009年，西藏境内藏羚羊数量已经达到15万只左右。

grammar.pattern: 以…为主（/核心/基础/依托/补充）
grammar.explanation: Identifies the dominant or foundational element in a composition or policy structure. 以A为主 = 'with A as the main component'. Used in classification, policy, and system descriptions.
examples: 绿茶采取茶树新叶，其成品以绿色主调为主。
要建立以居家养老为基础、以社区服务为依托、以社会机构养老为补充的养老机制。

grammar.explanation: Exclusive condition: 'only if X, then Y'. The condition is necessary, not merely sufficient. Creates emphasis on the one true path. Formal register.
examples: 只有在茅台镇这块地方去做，才能酿造出这精美绝伦的好酒。
学会过上积极而有意义的休闲日，才能使人们的生活质量得到明显改善。

grammar.explanation: Immediate sequence or trigger-response: 'as soon as X, immediately Y'. Expresses temporal immediacy and logical causation. Very common and versatile.
examples: 只要有灯光照射，藏羚羊就绝对跑不出灯光照射的范围。
只要它有特色，一吃就让你忘了自己还有高血压、糖尿病和肥胖症。

grammar.explanation: Explicit comparison structure. Elevate the comparison to a formal analytical move. Prefer 与……相比 over 比……在 in formal writing.
examples: 与汽油车相比，天然气汽车一氧化碳减少97%，烃氢化合物减少72%。
中国愿意消费绿色食品的人比例与西方发达国家相比差距明显。

grammar.explanation: Transition phrase meaning 'regarding this / in response to this'. Introduces the author's or society's response to a described situation. Formal and direct.
examples: 对此，社会和家庭成员应该充分理解和关怀老年人。
对此，专家建议，解决养老问题，要发展经济，增强经济承受能力。

grammar.pattern: 一方面…，另一方面…；与此同时…
grammar.explanation: Two-aspect analysis structure. Signals balanced treatment of a complex issue. Common in social analysis and persuasive writing to show nuance.
examples: 一方面绿色产品受到越来越多消费者的欢迎，另一方面国内绿色产品的质量和技术水平还比较低。
中国政府加大了对藏羚羊的保护力度，与此同时也加大了对青藏高原环保工作的投入。

grammar.pattern: 使…发生（了）变化 / 产生了… / 受到了…
grammar.explanation: Causative construction with 使 ('cause X to Y'). 使 is more formal than 让 in expository writing. The embedded result clause can be a state change, production, or reception.
examples: 强烈的紫外线会使某些农作物的化学组成发生变化，从而使作物在生长过程中发生变异。
1995年的双休日制度改变了中国城镇职工的生活方式，使休闲产业获得了新的发展机遇。

grammar.explanation: Indicates the primary component or focus. Used to say something mainly consists of X, or centers on X. Very common in formal exposition and technical description.
examples: 京剧的唱腔以"西皮"和"二黄"为主。（The vocal styles of Peking Opera center primarily on xīpí and èrhuáng.）
食品消费结构由主食型向副食型转变。（Dietary consumption structure shifted from staple-food-centered to side-dish-centered.）
整个景区以世界罕见的石英砂岩峰林峡谷地貌为主体。（The scenic area is defined primarily by its world-rare quartzite peak-forest canyon landform.）

grammar.explanation: Not only… but also. A fundamental parallel intensifier. In formal writing it often appears at clause or sentence level, adding a stronger or more important point to a first point.
examples: 黄山不仅风光奇秀，而且还出产名贵动植物。（Huangshan not only has extraordinary scenery, it also produces rare and precious flora and fauna.）
整座府邸不仅宽大，而且建筑规制也相当高。（The entire mansion is not only spacious but also built to a very high architectural standard.）
肥胖不仅仅影响外观，更是一个危害健康的杀手。（Obesity does not merely affect appearance; it is a more fundamental threat to health.）

grammar.explanation: As X increases/changes, Y also (increasingly) changes. Expresses co-variation or proportional change. Very common in economic, social, and scientific discourse.
examples: 随着生活费和食品费的增加，北京居民食品支出所占比重在逐年降低。（As living expenses and food costs rise, Beijing residents' food expenditure as a share of total spending falls year by year.）
随着都市生活节奏的加快，饮食社会化程度也越来越高。（As urban life speeds up, the degree to which eating is outsourced to social settings increases.）
随着经济社会的不断发展，城镇居民家庭收入水平大幅度提高。（As economic and social development continues, urban household income levels have risen sharply.）

grammar.explanation: Only if / only when X, then Y. Expresses necessary condition. Used to argue that a single approach is indispensable for achieving a result.
examples: 只有坚持长期的减肥运动，加上饮食控制，才能提高减肥的成功率。（Only by maintaining long-term exercise combined with dietary control can the weight-loss success rate be improved.）
只要把政府的信息资源放到网上，才能使其最大限度地得到共享。（Only by putting government information resources online can they be shared to the fullest extent.）

grammar.explanation: Due to X, therefore Y / it is because of X that Y. Causal connector heavily used in policy, science, and economic writing. 由于 can open the sentence or clause; 才 adds emphasis that Y is a direct and exclusive consequence.
examples: 由于山上地势不平，悬崖峭壁纵横交错，黄山松无法垂直生长。（Because the mountain terrain is uneven and cliffs crisscross, Huangshan pines cannot grow vertically.）
由于我国信息化工程起步较晚，因此政府工作人员信息化能力普遍较低。（Because China's information technology programs started relatively late, government staff have generally low digital capability.）
正是由于存在这些问题，才迫切需要发行第五套人民币。（It is precisely because of these problems that the fifth series of the RMB was urgently needed.）

grammar.explanation: On one hand…on the other hand. Used to present a two-pronged justification, explanation, or contrast. Very common in policy documents, reports, and analytical essays.
examples: 这样做，一方面继承了前四套的发行传统；另一方面也是借鉴了其他国家的成功经验。（Doing it this way, on one hand, upholds the issuance tradition of the previous four series; on the other hand, it draws on the successful experience of other countries.）
短期锻炼一方面效果有限，另一方面肌肉组织可能增加，从而影响减肥效果。（Short-term exercise, on one hand, has limited effect; on the other, muscle mass may increase, affecting the weight-loss result.）

grammar.explanation: No matter / regardless of X, always Y. Used to assert that a rule or principle applies universally, without exception. Common in explaining cultural norms or universal conditions.
examples: 不管是哪个朝代的人物，基本上都是明朝的打扮。（Regardless of which dynasty a character belongs to, costumes are basically in Ming Dynasty style.）
不管怎样，都必须长期坚持才会有效。（No matter which method is used, long-term persistence is required for it to be effective.）

grammar.explanation: Both X and Y / is at once X and Y. Enumerates two co-existing qualities or functions. Adds elegance compared with 也…也… by suggesting more intrinsic coherence between the two attributes.
examples: 货币既是价值尺度、流通手段，又是反映民族文化和精神风貌的艺术品。（Currency is both a measure of value and a medium of exchange, and an artwork reflecting national culture and spirit.）
南锣鼓巷既是让老北京人能够回忆起往事的地方，也是年轻人休闲逛街常选之地。（Nanluo Drum Lane is at once a place for old Beijingers to recall the past and a favorite hangout for young people.）

grammar.explanation: According to X, data show / evidence indicates. Introduces cited evidence in a neutral, authoritative register. Essential in economic, policy, and scientific writing.
examples: 据中国有关部门统计，中国成人识字率已达90%以上。（According to Chinese government statistics, the adult literacy rate has exceeded 90%.）
据国家统计局的一项调查显示，只有38.6%的消费者相信防伪标志。（A survey by the National Bureau of Statistics shows that only 38.6% of consumers trust anti-counterfeiting labels.）

grammar.explanation: Cannot do X, can only do Y. Expresses forced adaptation or constraint. Shows how a subject responds to an obstacle by taking an alternative path. Useful for describing resilience or limitation in analytical writing.
examples: 黄山松无法垂直生长，只能弯弯曲曲地甚至向下生长，显示出顽强的生命力。（Huangshan pines cannot grow vertically; they can only grow in twists and turns or even downward, displaying indomitable vitality.）
政府上网，公民极其容易找到有关法律依据，以法律为武器，保护自己的合法权益。

grammar.explanation: Firstly…secondly…thirdly. Numerical scaffolding for organizing multi-point arguments. In Chinese formal writing, these markers carry structural weight and signal the writer's control of the material.
examples: 首先，它为改善政府与公民关系提供了基础。其次，它为公民监督各级政府遵守法律…提供了充分的机会。（First, it provides a foundation for improving government-citizen relations. Second, it provides citizens with full opportunity to supervise government compliance with law.）
与第四套人民币相比，第五套人民币具有鲜明的特点：首先…第二…第三…第四…（Compared with the fourth series, the fifth series RMB has distinctive features: first…second…third…fourth…）

grammar.explanation: From the perspective of X / as regards X. Frames the discussion within a specific angle of analysis. Used heavily in academic, statistical, and policy discourse.
examples: 从方位分布上看，全山有东海、南海、西海、北海和天海。（From the perspective of spatial distribution, the mountain has five cloud seas: East, South, West, North, and Sky.）
就目前情况来看，动物性食品和细加工食品仍偏低。（As regards the current situation, animal-based foods and finely processed foods are still insufficient.）

grammar.explanation: This is undoubtedly / unquestionably… Used to assert a conclusion with confidence after presenting supporting reasoning. More emphatic than 这是. Common in argumentative and analytical writing.
examples: 这无疑是政府争取公民信任、提高自己合法性水平的重要途径。（This is undoubtedly an important avenue for the government to win citizen trust and enhance its own legitimacy.）

grammar.explanation: Cannot be ignored / must not be overlooked. A formulaic expression in policy and analytical Chinese, usually placed at the end of a clause about a risk, problem, or consideration.
examples: 相关的技术问题也不容忽视。（Related technical issues must not be overlooked.）
长期服用减肥药的安全性问题也还在研究之中，因此使用减肥药时一定要注意。（The safety of long-term use of weight-loss drugs is still under investigation and must not be ignored.）

takeaways: Open with a received wisdom, then immediately problematize it. This creates intellectual tension from the first sentence and gives the essay a thesis to build toward (e.g., 常听人说…对于这句话，我一直体会不深).

Use concrete sensory details as emotional triggers rather than stating feelings directly. Lao She's muffled toothless voice (瓮声瓮气), birds' feathers dyed red by the setting sun — the image does the emotional work, not the narrator's commentary.

The 不是…而是… pattern is essential for nuanced self-correction. Use it whenever you need to replace a simple or incorrect reading with a more honest, more precise one. It signals intellectual honesty and depth.

与其…倒不如… is one of the most elegant Chinese patterns for reframing: 'Rather than claiming X, it is truer to say Y.' Deploy it when you want to undercut a flattering interpretation and offer a more humble or accurate one.
For argumentative or policy essays, structure dual solutions as 其一…其二… and evaluate each. End by asserting both are necessary (…并举 / 只有…才能…). This is the standard Chinese policy essay structure.
The rhetorical question (心中没有祖国，怎么能写出好作品来？) is far stronger than a declarative assertion in Chinese formal prose. Use it at emotional or argumentative peaks, not throughout.
In professional and expository writing, deploy statistics in comparison pairs: not just 'X is large' but 'X was Y in period 1, only Z in period 2.' The contrast makes the number meaningful.
Learn to close on a three-part parallel verb phrase for rhetorical punch: 保护森林、挽救森林、扩大森林. Escalation of meaning within parallelism is a signature of high-register Chinese expository prose.

Use anecdote as character proof rather than characterization by assertion. Lao She's choice of durable shoes for a poor person reveals his values more vividly than any adjective. Show then tell, or show without telling.

For lyrical prose, anchor abstract reflections in a specific physical scene before articulating the universal point. Birds huddled together → 相伴相守到白头 is the model: earn the abstraction with the image first.
Classical allusions deployed casually as dialogue are extremely effective: 父母在，不远行 uttered as a spoken line both characterizes the speaker and enriches the register without feeling pedantic.
The 从此以后 close formula is a powerful formal marker that elevates a small incident into a turning point. Reserve it for moments that genuinely change a situation (not as filler).
In personal narrative, indirect emotional expression is preferred to direct statement. Pack the feeling inside an object — 打进领带里的是做女儿的一片心 — rather than writing 'I was grateful and moved.'

When writing about identity or values, consider the Chinese tradition of finding meaning in one's own name or role. Lao She's etymology of 舍予 is a model: personal, specific, and intellectually substantive. This works well in professional self-introductions too.
Open with historical contrast or a surprising statistic before stating your thesis. 那时候有一百多万只，而一百年后不超过75000只 creates urgency immediately, without abstract editorializing.
Use enumerated parallel structures to make analytical points feel exhaustive: 一是……二是……三是…… or policy 要……要……要…… The six-point 要 list in 养老 is a standard pattern for formal Chinese argumentation.
Quantify with emotional weight, not just data: 一条女士披肩是用3头藏羚羊的生命和无数人的辛勤劳动换来的 turns a number into an ethical claim.
Deploy cause-chains for analytical depth: 由于A → 导致B → 进而C. This three-link chain appears throughout the corpus and is more persuasive than a flat single cause-effect statement.
Use rhetorical questions as structural pivots between sections: 养老到底应该走向何方呢？ or 是什么原因使藏羚羊数量减少得这么快呢？ These reset reader attention and signal a new analytical move.
Anchor abstract arguments in concrete everyday scenes: 买菜、拣菜、洗菜、切菜 is far more vivid than 做饭过程很复杂. Use verb enumeration chains for procedural vividness.
Close comparative essays with a logical twist rather than a summary: the North American food culture essay's 短处摇身一变成了特长 is memorable precisely because it subverts expectations.
Use formal citation phrases (据调查/据报道/研究表明) to signal evidence-based reasoning even in cultural essays — this distinguishes formal from informal Chinese prose and builds authority.
Master the 随着……中国/人们/社会…… opening: it is the single most common opener in formal Chinese expository writing and immediately sets a socio-temporal frame that professional readers expect.
End advocacy essays with communal first-person calls to action (让我们……, 大家行动起来吧！) or anchor the close with 义不容辞 + 总之 — these signal social responsibility and are standard in Chinese public discourse.

Open with scope, then drill down. Chinese expository essays consistently move from the general (location, total area, international fame) to the specific (individual peaks, species counts, exact temperatures). Avoid jumping straight into detail without orienting the reader first.

Use a named organizing framework. The best texts anchor their structure in a named set: 四绝 (four wonders), 四功 (four skills), 生旦净丑 (four role types). This technique signals analytical control and makes the essay memorable. When writing about any complex domain, find or create a named framework.

Statistical specificity elevates descriptive prose. Numbers (海拔1864米, 70度, 300多种脊椎动物) anchor impressionistic language and establish authority. Mix data with sensory description — this combination is more persuasive than either alone.

Classical citations and sayings serve as the highest register of endorsement. Quoting a classical couplet (五岳归来不看山，黄山归来不看岳) or a UNESCO official signals that the claim is beyond personal opinion. Build a repertoire of quotable sayings for topics you write about frequently.

The concession-rebuttal pattern is essential for argumentative essays. Chinese policy writing does not ignore counterevidence; it acknowledges past efforts, identifies their specific inadequacies (临时性、局部性特点，没有长期的效应), and then presents the new solution as a direct answer. This pattern (过去…努力…但是…没有…效应) is more credible than ignoring objections.

Numbered first/second/third scaffolding (首先…其次…/第一…第二…) is the default organizational device in Chinese analytical writing. Use it for arguments, problems, and solutions. It signals a logical mind and makes your writing easy to follow in professional contexts.

Metaphors in formal Chinese writing are usually brief and grounded in concrete objects. 货币是一个国家的'名片' (currency is a country's business card) works because it unpacks into multiple attributes (identity, compact, representative). Strong Chinese metaphors are reversible: the tenor and vehicle each illuminate the other.
要么…要么… and 一方面…另一方面… are the two standard binary framing devices. Use 要么…要么… for exhaustive alternatives (the reader either does A or does B); use 一方面…另一方面… for justifications or co-existing factors.

Hedge with specific phrases, not vague qualifiers. Formal Chinese academic and medical writing uses 并不理想, 有一定的疗效, 还没有足够的研究资料表明 rather than vague adverbs. Specific hedges signal scientific honesty; vague hedges signal evasion.

Closing sentences in Chinese essays often zoom out to maximum significance. After all the specific details, the conclusion asserts the broadest impact: 对促进国家政治、经济和文化的发展会产生深远的影响. Train yourself to write a 'significance escalator' from the specific claim to the largest relevant domain.

The pattern '不到非X不可的程度，不要采用X方法' (do not resort to X unless absolutely necessary) is a reusable template for any graduated advice — medical, legal, business. It shows the writer has a sense of proportionality.

Place writing follows a reliable sequence: location → historical name/origin → boundaries/layout → notable sub-elements → famous associated people → present-day significance. This sequence works for describing a district, a building, or an institution.

essays.title: 第一课课文：开学（张天明到宿舍）
essays.text:
张天明是大学一年级的新生。快开学了，他家离大学很远，得坐飞机去学校。他坐飞机坐了两个多小时，下飞机以后，马上叫了一辆出租汽车，很快就到了学校宿舍。

人真多！
你是新生吧？
是，我是新生。你呢？
我是研究生。在这儿帮新生搬东西。请问，你叫什么名字？
我叫张天明。
张天明？是中文名字吗？
对，我爸爸妈妈是从中国来的。可是我是在美国出生，在美国长大的。请问你的名字是……
我正在学中文，我的中文名字是柯林。你的名字是哪三个字？
张是弓长张，就是一张纸的张，天是天气的天，明是明天的明。
你是怎么来学校的？
我先坐飞机，从机场到学校坐出租汽车。
柯林，你也住在这儿吗？
不，这是新生宿舍，我住在校外。
是吗？你为什么住校外？你觉得住在校内好，还是住在校外好？
有的人喜欢住在学校宿舍，觉得又方便又安全，有的人喜欢住在校外，因为校外的房子比较便宜。我住在校外，除了想省点儿钱以外，还因为住在校外自由。再说，住在校内也不见得很方便。
真的吗？那我以后也搬到校外去。
你刚来，在学校住对你有好处，可以适应一下学校的生活。要是你以后想搬家，我可以帮你找房子。
好吧，我以后要是搬家，一定请你帮忙。
天明，前边没人了，我帮你把行李搬进去吧。
好，谢谢。哎，我的电脑呢？……糟糕，电脑可能拉在出租车上了！
essays.summary: Zhang Tianming, a Chinese-American freshman, arrives at his college dormitory by plane and taxi. He meets Ke Lin, a graduate student helping newcomers move in. They discuss how to write and explain Zhang's Chinese name using mnemonic decomposition, how Zhang traveled to campus, and the trade-offs of living on-campus vs. off-campus. Ke Lin argues for off-campus living (cheaper, more freedom) while also counseling the newcomer to stay on campus first to adjust. The dialogue ends with Zhang realizing he may have left his laptop in the taxi.
essays.why_study: This dialogue models the natural rhythm of educated spoken Mandarin: sequential question-answer turns, character-name disambiguation by decomposition, balanced-view presentation (有的人…有的人…), reason-stacking with 除了…以外还…, and polite counter-argument with 不见得. Though the register is informal, the structural patterns transfer directly into professional discussion and written argumentation.
key_devices: Character disambiguation via decomposition and association: 张是弓长张，就是一张纸的张 — standard native-speaker technique to clarify homophones
Balanced-view framing: 有的人…，有的人… — presents two sides before expressing personal stance
Reason-stacking with 除了…以外，还… — layers a primary and secondary reason without repetition
再说 as discourse connector — adds a further point conversationally, more native-sounding than 而且
不见得 for soft pushback — hedges without confrontation (not necessarily / it's not certain that)
Conditional offer template: 要是你以后想…，我可以… — natural spoken form of a polite offer
Consecutive action sequencing: V了 + V₂ — 下飞机以后，马上叫了一辆出租汽车 tightens narrative
糟糕 as spontaneous alarm exclamation — high-frequency colloquial interjection

essays.title: 张天明上大学的第一天
essays.text: 张天明是大学一年级的新生，他家离大学很远。大学快开学了，他坐飞机去学校。他坐飞机坐了两个多小时，下飞机以后，马上叫了一辆出租汽车，很快就到了学校宿舍。宿舍前边的人很多，在那儿，他认识了柯林。
essays.summary: A short narrative recounting Zhang Tianming's first day at college: a far-from-home freshman who flies in, takes a taxi, and meets Ke Lin at the busy dorm.
essays.why_study: A compact model of how to make a multi-sentence narrative cohere: it shows pronoun substitution (他 for 张天明), omission of repeated subjects when they stay the same, and the use of time and place words to chain events.
key_devices: Pronoun 他 replaces the named subject once it is established
Repeated identical subject pronouns are dropped (the textbook bracketed [他] before 下飞机/马上/很快)
Time/sequence connectors: …以后, 马上, 快…了, 就
Place word 在那儿 as a connective hinge that introduces the meeting with Ke Lin
Verb-duration complement 坐了两个多小时 to express elapsed time

essays.title: 张天明的宿舍很方便
essays.text: 张天明的宿舍很小，因为没有空调，很热，但是很方便。学生餐厅就在楼下，旁边有一个小商店，卖日用品和文具。这里洗衣服也方便得很，每层楼有三台洗衣机和三台干衣机。离教室也不远，走路差不多五、六分钟。这里离大马路很远，很安静。附近还有一家中国餐馆儿，菜很地道。
essays.summary: A paragraph arguing that despite being small, hot, and air-conditioner-less, the dorm is very convenient, listing the cafeteria, shop, laundry, classrooms, quiet, and a nearby authentic restaurant.
essays.why_study: A clean example of stating a thesis (很方便) and then supporting it with concrete, parallel evidence. It models how place words, conjunctions, and adverbs knit a description into a flowing whole.
key_devices: Concession then claim: 很小…很热，但是很方便
Place words as connectives: 楼下, 旁边, 这里, 离…
Conjunctions 因为/但是 to join clauses; adverbs 就/还/也 as cohesion markers
Degree complement 方便得很 for emphasis
Approximation 差不多五、六分钟 to make detail vivid without false precision

essays.title: 宿舍房间描写（张天明的房间）
essays.text: 张天明宿舍的房间不太大，住两个人。他的同屋已经来了。房间里家具不多，靠窗户摆着两张书桌，每张桌子的前边有一把椅子。书桌的旁边是床，床上有被子和毯子。床旁边有两个衣柜，柜子里挂着一些衣服。门右边放着两个书架，书架还是空的。
essays.summary: A systematic description of a two-person dorm room, moving from desks by the window to the bed, wardrobes, and bookshelves by the door.
essays.why_study: The canonical model for describing a space using existential sentences. It shows how to lay out a room object by object in a logical spatial path so a reader could draw it.
key_devices: Existential pattern: Place + verb + 着/了 + numeral + measure word + noun (靠窗户摆着两张书桌 / 柜子里挂着一些衣服 / 门右边放着两个书架)
Spatial anchors chained one to the next: 靠窗户→旁边→床上→床旁边→门右边
Measure-word precision: 张 (desk), 把 (chair), 个 (wardrobe/bookshelf)
是 vs 有 to signal a single item vs. multiple items at a location

essays.title: 在饭馆儿（点菜叙事开头）
essays.text: 今天是周末，功课也做完了，张天明就给柯林发微信，说他和他的女朋友丽莎想吃中国饭，问他想不想去。柯林说正好他也想吃中国饭，就让张天明和丽莎在宿舍门口等着，他开车来接他们。半个钟头以后，柯林的汽车到了。张天明和丽莎上车一看，车里还有一个女孩儿，柯林介绍说，她是从中国来的留学生，叫林雪梅。几分钟以后，他们四个人到了那家中国饭馆儿。
essays.summary: A narrative setup: on a free weekend Zhang Tianming WeChats Ke Lin about getting Chinese food; Ke Lin drives over, and they discover a fourth person, exchange student Lin Xuemei, before arriving at the restaurant.
essays.why_study: A model for the narrative 'frame' that sets up a scene before dialogue. It shows topic-fronting of known information and tight action sequencing with time phrases.
key_devices: Topic-comment opening with given information fronted: 今天是周末，功课也做完了
就 to mark the natural next action (发微信 / 让…等着)
Momentary 一 + verb: 上车一看 ('took one look and saw…')
Time-stamped sequencing: 半个钟头以后…几分钟以后…
Reported speech with 说/问/介绍说 to compress dialogue into narration

essays.title: 住校外的三个理由（柯林的回答）
essays.text: 有的人喜欢住在学校宿舍，觉得又方便又安全，有的人喜欢住在校外，因为校外的房子比较便宜。我住在校外，除了想省点儿钱以外，还因为住在校外自由。再说，住在校内也不见得很方便。
essays.summary: Ke Lin explains why he lives off campus: some prefer dorms for convenience and safety, others off campus for cheaper rent; he himself lives off campus to save money and for freedom, adding that on-campus living isn't necessarily convenient.
essays.why_study: A textbook demonstration of stacking connectives to deliver an organized, persuasive answer rather than a flat list. The same content is also shown reorganized with 第一/第二/第三, illustrating two equivalent structuring strategies.
key_devices: Contrast framing: 有的人…，有的人…
Reason chain: 因为 + 除了…以外，还 + 再说
Polite hedge 不见得 to soften a counter-claim
Paired-adjective 又方便又安全
Alternative enumeration via 第一…，第二…，第三… (shown as a variant)

essays.title: 在中国饭馆儿吃饭 (Eating at a Chinese Restaurant)
essays.text: 周末，张天明、丽莎、柯林和柯林的女朋友林雪梅一起在一家中国饭馆儿吃饭。那个饭馆儿鸡做得不错，鱼也做得很好，特别是清蒸鱼，味道好极了。那儿的芥蓝牛肉也挺好，又嫩又香，菠菜豆腐汤做得也很好，那儿还有有机的小白菜。所以他们点了一个清蒸鱼，一个芥蓝牛肉，一个菠菜豆腐汤和一个小白菜。柯林告诉服务员，菜要清淡一点儿，别太咸，少放油，别放味精。他们的菜很快就来了。
essays.summary: A short third-person account of four friends ordering at a Chinese restaurant: which dishes the place does well, what they ordered, and the special requests Ke Lin made to the waiter (lighter seasoning, less salt, less oil, no MSG).
essays.why_study: The textbook explicitly presents this as a 'Keep It Flowing' model for cohesion. It shows how to turn a list of facts (good dishes -> dishes ordered -> special requests) into a smoothly connected paragraph rather than disjointed sentences. Excellent template for any 'describe a dining experience' or recommendation paragraph.
key_devices: Definite reference 那个/那儿 replaces the indefinite first mention 一家饭馆儿 (introduce-then-refer-back)
Causal connective 所以 links the dishes' merits to the ordering decision
Emphasis marker 特别是 singles out the standout dish (清蒸鱼)
Additive adverbs 也 / 还 stack parallel positives (鱼也…，那儿还有…)
Descriptive 又…又… (又嫩又香) compresses two qualities into one phrase
Time/place words (周末, 那儿) act as connective anchors

essays.title: 张天明和柯林买衣服的标准 (Zhang Tianming's and Ke Lin's Clothes-Buying Criteria)
essays.text: 张天明买衣服一定要买名牌的，要不然就不买，因为他觉得名牌的衣服质量好。柯林觉得买衣服只考虑便宜当然不好，但是不一定非买名牌的不可。他买衣服的标准，第一看穿着舒服不舒服，第二看质量好不好，第三看价钱合适不合适。他不在乎是什么牌子的，也不在乎样子时髦不时髦。他认为穿衣服是为了自己，不是为了给别人看。张天明看见柯林穿了一件名牌衣服，就问柯林为什么穿名牌的衣服？柯林说，他说过不一定非买名牌的不可，但是没说过不穿名牌呀。
essays.summary: Contrasts two characters' shopping philosophies: Zhang Tianming insists only on brand names (believing they are higher quality), while Ke Lin ranks comfort, quality, and price over brand and fashion, arguing one dresses for oneself, not for others. Ends with Zhang catching Ke Lin wearing a brand-name item and Ke Lin's distinction (he never said he wouldn't wear brands, only that he wouldn't insist on buying them).
essays.why_study: A clean model for structured comparison of two viewpoints and for listing prioritized criteria with 第一…第二…第三…. Shows how to state a position, give a reason, then handle a counterpoint/apparent contradiction logically.
key_devices: Ordinal enumeration 第一…第二…第三… to rank criteria
Pronoun 他 keeps the subject unchanged across sentences without repeating the name
Conjunctions 要不然 (otherwise), 因为 (cause), 但是 (contrast) chain the logic
Adverb 就 ties consequence to condition (要不然就不买 / 就问柯林)
Parallel negation 不在乎…，也不在乎… for balanced emphasis
是为了…不是为了… frames purpose vs. non-purpose neatly
Resolving an apparent contradiction by drawing a fine distinction (买 vs. 穿)

essays.title: 下个学期选什么课？ (Which Classes to Take Next Semester?)
essays.text: 张天明这个学期选了四门课：世界历史、电脑、政治学和中文。他觉得这几门课都很有意思，也学到了不少东西。现在得选下学期的课了，他希望下个学期能轻松点儿。这一天下午，他跟李哲聊选课的事。听说李哲要选经济和电脑课，他很高兴，[他]也决定选这两门课。然后他们又聊选专业的事。张天明说他喜欢文学，可是他妈妈非叫他学他不喜欢的金融不可。李哲说他们班很多同学选专业很自由，因为他们的父母不太管孩子选什么专业。
essays.summary: A condensed retelling of a longer dialogue: Zhang Tianming's current four courses, his wish for a lighter next semester, his chat with Li Zhe leading him to also pick economics and computing, and the tension over majors (he likes literature but his mother insists on finance; Li Zhe notes many classmates have more freedom).
essays.why_study: Demonstrates how to compress a multi-turn dialogue into a tight third-person summary—a key academic writing skill. Models sequencing with time words and reporting both hearsay and speech without quoting verbatim.
key_devices: Pronouns 他/他们 replace names once the subject is established; redundant subject omitted (shown as [他])
Time/sequence words 现在, 这一天下午, 然后 advance the narrative
Reporting verbs 听说 (hearsay) and …说 (reported speech) fold dialogue into narration
Contrast/cause conjunctions 可是, 因为 connect clauses
非…不可 dramatizes the mother's insistence inside reported speech

essays.title: 买东西 (Shopping) — Lesson 4 dialogue
essays.text:
张天明从家里来的时候，妈妈给他买了一些衣服，像T恤衫、毛衣、牛仔裤什么的，可是他觉得无论是样子还是颜色都不太好。今天是星期日，正好林雪梅和丽莎也需要买卫生纸、牙膏、毛巾、洗衣粉这些日用品，柯林就带他们来到附近一家最大的购物中心。
—你要买什么衣服？
—我想买一套运动服。
—这边儿就是。你看看这一套，样子、大小、长短都合适，而且打八折。
—颜色也不错。多少钱？是什么牌子的？
—价钱不贵。这个牌子没听说过。不过，是纯棉的。
—牌子不好不行，我想买名牌的。
…（讨论名牌 vs. 便宜、质量）…
—我买衣服的标准，第一是看穿着舒服不舒服，第二是质量好不好，第三是价钱合适不合适。是什么牌子的，样子时髦不时髦，我都不在乎。因为穿衣服是为了自己，不是为了给别人看。
…（付钱）…
—先生，付现金，还是用信用卡？
—我刷卡。
—先生，加上税一共是一百八十六块四。
essays.summary: Four friends go to a mall: Zhang shops for a tracksuit while the others buy daily necessities. The dialogue debates buying name-brand vs. cheap clothing, lays out criteria for choosing clothes, and ends at checkout (cash vs. card, tax added to the total).
essays.why_study: Practical register for shopping, expressing discounts (打八折), describing fit (样子/大小/长短都合适), tactful disagreement, and a checkout exchange. Useful sentence frames for everyday transactional Chinese.
key_devices: 无论…都… to dismiss every option at once (style and color both no good)
…什么的 to cap a list of examples
Partial-agreement frame 便宜是便宜，可是… for tactful pushback
Criteria listing 第一…第二…第三…
Transactional set phrases: 打八折, 付现金/刷卡, 加上税一共是…

essays.title: 丽莎的心事 (Lisa's Worry) — Lesson 6 opening narrative
essays.text: 丽莎这几天好像有什么心事。昨天林雪梅问了她好几次，她才说她跟张天明吵架了。…天明人很好，很开朗，学习也不错。在兴趣上她跟天明不太一样，天明是个球迷，电视里一有球赛，他就非看不可，丽莎是个歌迷，一有演唱会就去听。…林雪梅想来想去，想不出他们到底发生了什么事儿。…两个星期前我跟他约好去看一个演唱会，没想到他那天晚上一直玩儿电脑，把演唱会的事儿忘得一干二净。…他不停地给我道歉，说对不起，态度特别好。这让我觉得他心里还是有我。…实际上，柯林跟天明差不多，常常忘这忘那、丢三落四的。
essays.summary: A third-person narrative plus dialogue: Lisa has been troubled because she quarreled with her boyfriend Zhang Tianming over his forgetfulness (he forgot a concert date). Lin Xuemei reassures her that being scatterbrained is different from not caring, noting her own boyfriend Ke Lin is similarly forgetful.
essays.why_study: Rich source of personality/feeling vocabulary and high-frequency colloquial idioms for describing forgetful, easygoing people and emotional states. Models how narration and reported dialogue interleave in a story.
key_devices: 一…就… (一有球赛就…，一有演唱会就…) for habitual immediate reactions
非…不可 for compulsive behavior (他就非看不可)
想来想去 / 想不出 reduplication + potential complement to show pondering
Vivid set idioms: 忘得一干二净, 丢三落四, 忘这忘那
原来 to mark a realization; 到底 to press for the real story

essays.title: 张天明太马虎了
essays.text: 张天明人很好，很开朗，学习也不错。他是个球迷，电视里一有球赛，就非看不可。可是他非常马虎。两个星期前丽莎跟他约好去看一个演唱会，那天晚上他一直玩儿电脑，把演唱会的事儿忘得一干二净。上个星期六丽莎让他一起去看电影，他很高兴地答应了。八点的电影，丽莎等到八点一刻他还没到，给他打手机，才知道他跑到另一家电影院去了。这个天明，真马虎！
essays.summary: A short profile of Zhang Tianming: likeable, cheerful, a sports fan, but extremely careless. Two anecdotes prove the point—he forgot a concert date entirely, and he went to the wrong movie theater—ending with an exclamatory verdict.
essays.why_study: A textbook 'Keep It Flowing' model showing how to build a coherent paragraph around a single trait (马虎) using concrete examples rather than mere adjectives. Demonstrates topic-sentence + supporting-evidence + closing structure.
key_devices: Topic sentence stating the trait (太马虎了), then proving it with two parallel anecdotes
Time phrases as connectors: 两个星期前 / 那天晚上 / 上个星期六 / 八点的电影
Pronoun 他 substituting for 张天明 to avoid repetition
一…就 (一有球赛就非看不可) and 才 (等到八点一刻…才知道) as cohesive adverbs
Complement of result 忘得一干二净 to intensify the description
Exclamatory closing (这个天明，真马虎！) echoing the opening for a frame

essays.title: 张天明是个电脑迷
essays.text: 张天明是个电脑迷。每天在网上看新闻、查资料、玩儿游戏，有时候还在自己的网站上写博客，一会儿都离不开电脑，常常一上网，就忘了时间。他写文章的时候，喜欢上网查资料，觉得网上很多资料很可靠很有用。别人说他玩儿电脑上瘾了。他说现在是网络时代，当然离不开电脑。
essays.summary: A compact portrait of Zhang Tianming as a computer addict: his daily online activities, his reliance on the Internet for research, and his self-justification that in the Internet age one naturally cannot do without a computer.
essays.why_study: A model for the 'define-a-type-then-illustrate' paragraph. Shows how to list activities economically with a serial 顿号 structure and how to close with the subject's own voice (他说…) to round off a description.
key_devices: Opening label sentence (X 是个…迷) that the rest of the paragraph unpacks
Serial listing with 顿号: 看新闻、查资料、玩儿游戏
Time-frequency adverbs as connectors: 每天 / 有时候 / 的时候 / 常常
一…就 (一上网就忘了时间) showing habitual cause-effect
Reported judgment (别人说…) vs. the subject's rebuttal (他说…) for a balanced close
一会儿都离不开 emphatic negation for vividness

essays.title: 张天明想打工 (Lesson 8 opening)
essays.text: 张天明的父母希望孩子们能受到良好的教育，所以孩子一生下来就开始给他们存教育费。虽然张天明父母的收入不少，但是供天明和姐姐上大学，经济上还是有些压力。张天明觉得自己已经是大人了，应该找工作挣钱来减轻父母的负担，所以就上网看看有没有适合自己的工作。这时候丽莎和雪梅走了进来。
essays.summary: Sets up the part-time-work theme: the parents save for education from birth and, despite decent income, feel financial pressure supporting two children in college; Tianming, feeling grown-up, goes online to look for a suitable job to ease their burden, just as Lisa and Xuemei walk in.
essays.why_study: A model of how to open an essay or story by establishing background (cause), motivation, and an action, then handing off to dialogue. Good template for the 'situation → reasoning → decision' setup move.
key_devices: 虽然…但是… concessive frame to acknowledge income yet assert pressure
所以 / 应该…来… chaining cause to intended action (找工作挣钱来减轻负担)
一…就 (一生下来就开始存) for immediacy
Scene-handoff sentence (这时候…走了进来) to transition from narration into dialogue

essays.title: 电脑和网络 (Lesson 7 dialogue)
essays.text: 对不起，对不起，我在网上下载了一个软件，又查了一点儿东西，结果忘了时间了。——查什么？——我要写一篇文章，查一些资料。——查资料？我们的教授不让我们用网上的东西，一定要用书或者杂志。他说网上的垃圾太多了。——你的教授太落伍了，网上有很多资料很可靠很有用啊。网络世界又大又方便，你可以叫外卖、购物，你可以租房子、买车，衣食住行，什么都查得到。——哎，丽莎，你还在生我的气吗？——你说我该不该生气？你每次都迟到，老是害得大家等你。——对不起，对不起，是我不好。下次不敢了。——好了，好了，不说这些了，我们到底去哪家卡拉OK啊？
essays.summary: Friends confront Tianming for being late again because he lost track of time downloading software and browsing online. The chat ranges over the reliability of online vs. print sources, the convenience of the Internet for everyday needs, an apology exchange, and a pivot to change the subject.
essays.why_study: A model of natural intermediate dialogue: apologizing, debating a point, and tactfully closing an awkward exchange. Useful template for register-appropriate spoken connectors and reduplicated softeners.
key_devices: 结果 to mark an unintended outcome (结果忘了时间)
Reduplicated apology/pacifier: 对不起，对不起 / 好了，好了
Rhetorical question to scold gently: 你说我该不该生气？
又…又… parallelism for praise: 网络世界又大又方便
Catch-all enumeration 衣食住行，什么都查得到
Topic-change pivot phrase: 好了，好了，不说这些了

essays.title: 中国地理
essays.text: 中国的面积很大。西边是高原，那儿有世界上最高的山，而东边是平原和大海，所以中国的河流，大多是从西往东流。因为东部和南部自然条件比较好，所以人口主要在这些地方，大城市也都靠海或者离海不远。而中国的西南，高山、高原多，西北呢，沙漠多，自然条件不太好，所以人口比较少。
essays.summary: A concise expository paragraph describing China's geography: the high plateau in the west contrasting with eastern plains and sea; eastward river flow driven by terrain; concentration of population and major cities along the coast due to favorable natural conditions; sparse population in the mountainous southwest and desert northwest.
essays.why_study: The textbook presents this explicitly as a 'Keep It Flowing' model, highlighting its cohesive devices. It demonstrates how to use conjunctions, anaphoric place pronouns, causal constructions, and the topic marker 呢 to chain short sentences into fluent expository prose. The register is clean, semi-formal, and directly transferable to academic writing, geography presentations, or any topic requiring logical spatial/causal sequencing.
key_devices: 而 as contrastive conjunction between parallel clauses (西边是高原…而东边是平原和大海)
那儿 as anaphoric place pronoun linking back to the previously mentioned location (西边是高原，那儿有…)
因为…所以… explicit cause-effect frame making the logical chain transparent
也 as additive connective within a clause (大城市也都靠海)
Sentence-initial 而 to pivot to a contrasting new topic (而中国的西南，高山、高原多)
X呢 topic marker introducing a parallel comparison (西北呢，沙漠多)
大多 as formal adverb for 'mostly / for the most part' (大多是从西往东流)
Parallel repetition of 所以 to build layered causal structure

grammar.pattern: 了 (IV) — occurrence marking with quantified / definite object
grammar.explanation: The dynamic particle 了 signals that an action has occurred. It appears: (a) after the verb before a quantified object; (b) between verb and object when a second sentence-final 了 follows; (c) between verb and object when a further clause follows; (d) between verb and a definite/known object. Adding 了 does NOT automatically make the verb past tense — it can mark future actions (e.g., 吃了早饭去机场). When no time phrase is given, 了 implies the immediate past.
examples: 昨天晚上我看了一个电影。 (quantified object — last night I saw a movie)
我给小李打了电话了。 (double 了 — I called Little Li)
张天明买了机票就回家了。 (followed by clause — after buying the ticket he went home)
昨天我在学校里看见了小王。 (definite object — I saw Little Wang at school yesterday)
明天我吃了早饭去机场。 (future action — tomorrow after breakfast I'll go to the airport)

grammar.pattern: 了 (IV) — consecutive actions: V₁了 + V₂
grammar.explanation: When the first verb phrase in a sentence carries 了, it signals that V₂ begins only once V₁ is completed. The two verbs are in strict temporal sequence. This pattern is compact and avoids explicit time words.
examples: 我下了课再去找你。 (After class I'll come look for you.)
昨天我搬进了宿舍就去餐厅吃饭了。 (As soon as I finished moving in, I went to eat.)
他下了课回家就睡觉了。 (As soon as class ended he went home and slept.)

grammar.pattern: 是…的 construction (I) — focusing on circumstances of a known action
grammar.explanation: When both speakers know an action occurred, 是…的 highlights HOW, WHEN, WHERE, WHY, or WHO performed it — not whether it happened. 是 is optional in positive sentences but obligatory in negatives. 的 can appear at sentence end or before the object. Do NOT use this construction to assert that an action occurred; use 了 for that.
examples: （是）什么时候来的？ — （是）昨天晚上来的。 (When did he come? — Yesterday evening.)
张天明（是）在美国出生的。 (Zhang Tianming was born in the U.S. [focus: location])
我不是在中国学的中文，是在美国学的。 (I didn't study Chinese in China — 是 cannot be dropped in negatives)
你是在哪儿上的大学？ — 我是在纽约上的大学。 (Where did you go to college? — In New York.)

grammar.pattern: 除了…以外，还/也… vs. 除了…以外，都…
grammar.explanation: These two structures look similar but mean opposite things. 除了X以外，还/也Y = 'In addition to X, also Y' (both X and Y are included). 除了X以外，都Y = 'Except for X, everything/everyone else does Y' (X is excluded). The crucial marker is 都 (all/everything) vs. 还/也 (also). In speech 以外 can often be dropped.
examples: 他除了学中文以外，还学日文。 (In addition to Chinese, he also studies Japanese. — both included)
除了小柯以外，我们班的同学都去过中国。 (Except for Little Ke, every classmate has been to China. — Ke excluded)
我住在校外，除了想省点儿钱以外，还因为住在校外自由。 (I live off campus not only to save money but also for the freedom. — both reasons included)

grammar.pattern: 有的…，有的… — presenting parallel contrasting cases
grammar.explanation: 有的人/有的 + clause A，有的人/有的 + clause B presents two distinct groups or situations without assigning judgment. It is a compact, neutral way to frame a two-sided issue. In formal writing it appears as 有些人…；另一些人…
examples: 有的人喜欢住在学校宿舍，觉得又方便又安全，有的人喜欢住在校外，因为校外的房子比较便宜。
有的同学选理科，有的同学选文科。 (Some students choose science, others choose humanities.)

grammar.pattern: 再说 — discourse connector adding a further reason or point
grammar.explanation: 再说 (lit. 'and furthermore') introduces an additional point after the main point(s) have been made. It is more colloquial than 另外 or 此外 and signals a casual afterthought or supplementary argument. Common in spoken and informal written Chinese.
examples: 我住在校外，除了想省点儿钱以外，还因为住在校外自由。再说，住在校内也不见得很方便。
今天我不想出去。再说，天气也不好。 (I don't want to go out today. Besides, the weather is bad.)

grammar.pattern: 不见得 — soft negation / polite counter-assertion
grammar.explanation: 不见得 (not necessarily; it's not certain that) hedges a counter-argument, softening it compared to 不一定 or 不对. It implies 'I'm not so sure about that' rather than a flat denial, which is important for maintaining conversational face.
examples: 住在校内也不见得很方便。 (Living on campus isn't necessarily all that convenient.)
贵的东西不见得好。 (Expensive things aren't necessarily good.)
他说得那么好，不见得是真的。 (Just because he said it so well doesn't mean it's true.)

grammar.explanation: Chains multiple reasons for fluent extended speech: 因为 introduces the cause, 除了…以外，还 adds a further reason, and 再说 tacks on a final supporting point. The whole set can be replaced by ordinal enumeration 第一…，第二…，第三….
examples: 我住在校外，除了想省点儿钱以外，还因为住在校外自由。再说，住在校内也不见得很方便。
妈妈告诉我当医生最好。因为除了好找工作以外，还挣钱多，再说家里人看病也方便。

grammar.explanation: An adverb placed before the predicate to politely express a dissenting or qualifying opinion — 'not necessarily so.'
examples: 在中国出生的人，中文不见得好。
报上说的不见得对。
开车不见得比走快。

grammar.pattern: X对Y（没）有好处 (X is (not) good for Y)
grammar.explanation: Fixed frame for stating benefit or harm; 好处 is a noun, 对 introduces the beneficiary.
examples: 少开车，多走路，对健康有好处。
这样做对你没有好处。
上过大学、会用电脑、会说外语，对找工作有好处。

grammar.pattern: Existential sentence: Place + verb + 着/了 + numeral + MW + noun
grammar.explanation: Describes what exists at a location, with the place fronted and an indefinite noun at the end. Verbs are limited to 有, 是, and posture/placement verbs (放, 摆, 挂, 站, 坐, 躺, 拿, 贴). 是 implies a single/sole item; 有 implies one of possibly several.
examples: 桌子上放着一本书。
书架上摆着三张照片。
门右边放着两个书架。
他身上穿着一件白衬衫，手里拿着一条红毯子。

grammar.explanation: Expresses a general, non-specific degree ('rather, fairly'). It must NOT be used for an explicit comparison against a stated standard (where 更 or 一点儿 is used instead).
examples: 这把椅子比较贵，你别买了。
我比较喜欢打网球。
周末学校宿舍一般都有一点吵，图书馆比较安静。

grammar.explanation: A degree complement after adjectives or cognitive/emotive verbs indicating an extreme extent; stronger than 很 + adjective.
examples: 大家都忙得很。
我们的宿舍安静得很。
张天明想家得很。
学生餐厅的饭难吃得很。

grammar.pattern: 一 + verb  /  一…就… (as soon as)
grammar.explanation: 一 before a (usually monosyllabic) verb marks a brief, momentary action and needs a following clause. 一…就… links two consecutive actions; 就 is required when actions are immediate, subjects differ, or the first clause states a condition; optional when there is a pause with an adverbial.
examples: 我开门一看，是送信的。
老师刚才说的话，我一听就懂了。
我同屋每天一回宿舍就做功课。
他一到，我们就走。
我一吃味精就得喝很多水。

grammar.explanation: Joins two simultaneous qualities (adjectives, which must be same polarity) or two concurrent actions (verbs).
examples: 觉得又方便又安全。
妈妈做的清蒸鱼又嫩又香。
孩子们又跑又跳，玩儿得非常高兴。
那个小孩又哭又吵。

grammar.explanation: Stressed 是 placed before an adjective or verb concedes/reaffirms a previously stated point, typically before a 'but' clause. Requires that the predicate was already mentioned.
examples: 住在这个宿舍是很方便，去上课，去医院，去餐厅都不远。
他们的上海菜是很地道，可是太贵了。
他是搬走了，他去了一个更有名的大学。

grammar.explanation: Given/old information is placed at the front of the sentence as the topic and then commented on. Common for objects already in discourse, replacing plain SVO order.
examples: 今天是周末，功课也做完了……
这本书你看完了吗？
这件事我早就知道了。
你去台北的飞机票买好了吗？

grammar.explanation: A frame giving a flexible/indirect answer, deferring the outcome to some condition.
examples: 什么菜好吃？这就要看你的口味了。
明天的考试难不难？这就要看你准备得怎么样了。
这就要看买得到买不到便宜的机票了。

grammar.pattern: 是…的 (focusing on circumstances of a past event)
grammar.explanation: Used to ask about or highlight the time, place, manner, or companion of a known past action.
examples: 你是几号回学校来的？
我是二十三号回学校来的。
我是在美国出生，在美国长大的。

grammar.pattern: 适应 / 对…适应 (to get used to)
grammar.explanation: The verb 适应 must take a direct object, or the object may be fronted with 对 placed before the verb.
examples: 你适应大学的生活了吗？
你对这里的天气已经适应了吗？
我们刚来美国，对美国的天气还不适应。

grammar.explanation: 'Regardless / no matter…' — the result stays the same under any condition. Must pair with a question pronoun (谁/哪个/什么) OR an alternative construction (愿意不愿意 / 在家里还是在学校). The 都 (or 也) in the main clause is obligatory.
examples: 明天无论谁请客我都不去。 — No matter who's treating tomorrow, I'm not going.
无论去哪个购物中心都可以。 — Going to any of the shopping centers is fine.
他无论在家里还是在学校，都在学习。 — Whether at home or at school, he's always studying.
你无论愿意不愿意都得跟我们去。 — Whether you want to or not, you have to come.

grammar.explanation: Categorizing adjectives (男, 女, 国际, 有机, 纯棉) normally only sit before a noun and cannot take 很/非常. To use them as a predicate they must be wrapped in 是…的.
examples: 李友的中国朋友是个男的。 — Li You's Chinese friend is male.
小李买的水果都是有机的。 — The fruit Little Li buys is all organic.
我穿的这件衬衫是纯棉的。 — This shirt I'm wearing is 100% cotton.

grammar.explanation: Adjective/verb + 是 + same adjective/verb, then 可是/但是 + a qualifying point. Concedes the other person's point before raising an overlooked aspect or a caveat. Milder, more diplomatic than a flat 但是.
examples: 学音乐好是好，可是以后找工作不太容易。 — Studying music is good and all, but it's hard to find work later.
这件衣服贵是贵，可是牌子好。 — It's expensive, true, but it's a good brand.
我去是去，可是会晚一点儿。 — I am going, just a little late.
质量好是好，可是长短不合适。 — The quality is good, but the length isn't right.

grammar.explanation: 'For / as far as (someone) is concerned' — flags that the statement holds only with respect to that particular person. The person word is stressed in speech; often combines with 觉得.
examples: 对她来说，今年最需要做的事情是选一个好大学。 — For her, the most urgent thing this year is picking a good college.
一年级的中文课，对他来说，听和说很容易。 — First-year Chinese is easy for him in listening and speaking.
对他来说，有工作比没工作好。 — For him, having a job beats having none.

grammar.explanation: A verb takes a complement showing the outcome of the action; best memorized as set phrases. Three types: result on the action (搬完, 选好, 写错), result on the subject (听懂, 写累), result on the object (打哭, 搬走, 洗干净).
examples: 我搬完家就去购物中心买日用品。 — I'll buy necessities as soon as I finish moving.
下学期的课你选好了吗？ — Have you finished picking next semester's courses?
老师说的话我听懂了。 — I understood what the teacher said.
你要把衣服洗干净才能去看电影。 — You must wash the clothes clean before going to the movie.

grammar.explanation: (1) Before numeral/demonstrative + noun = 'the other(s)'; (2) as an adverb before a verb phrase = 'in addition, also'; (3) sentence-initial conjunction = 'besides, moreover'.
examples: 一门电脑课，另外两门课还没决定。 — One computer course; the other two I haven't decided. (use 1)
另外再选两门电脑系的课，学分就够了。 — Plus two more CS courses and the credits are enough. (use 2)
请你给我订两张飞机票，另外，麻烦你再帮我买一个包。 — Book me two tickets; also, please get me a bag. (use 3)

grammar.explanation: 再 = repetition/continuation, normally pointing to the future; modal verbs (想/要/能/会) come BEFORE 再. 还 = increase in number or expansion of scope, can be past or future; modal verbs come AFTER 还.
examples: 麻烦您再说一次。 — Please say it once more. (再, future repetition)
明年要再去两次。 — Next year I'll go twice more. (modal 要 before 再)
历史课我选了一门了，还得选一门。 — I've taken one history class; I still need one more. (还, addition)
我点了…还点了一盘饺子。 — I ordered…and also a plate of dumplings. (还, scope)

grammar.explanation: Selective conjunction: 'either… or…' — presents (at least) two possibilities from which one must/will be chosen.
examples: 你要么学医，要么学经济，就是不能学文学。 — You must study either medicine or economics, just not literature.
（寒假）要么打工，要么实习。 — Over winter break, either work part-time or intern.
我想念研究生，要么上工学院，要么上管理学院。 — I want grad school, either engineering or management.

grammar.explanation: 'Must / have no choice but / definitely will.' Two senses: (1) inevitability of a bad outcome if X isn't done; (2) strong resolve/insistence on doing X.
examples: 你再不起床，上课非晚了不可。 — If you don't get up, you'll definitely be late. (inevitability)
这个病非打针不可。 — For this illness you simply must get a shot. (necessity)
张天明买衣服非买名牌的不可。 — Zhang insists on buying only name brands. (resolve)
小林吃鱼非吃清蒸的不可。 — When Little Lin eats fish, it has to be steamed.

grammar.explanation: Adverb 'have no choice but to / can only,' marking the absence of other options.
examples: 今天的票卖完了，只好明天看了。 — Tickets are sold out today, so I can only watch tomorrow.
服务员说不卖烤鸭，我只好点别的菜了。 — They didn't sell roast duck, so I had to order something else.
一看外边下雨了，只好不去了。 — Seeing it was raining, we had to call it off.

grammar.explanation: 'Otherwise' — introduces the dire consequence of not following the advice in the previous clause.
examples: 你得再买一条毯子，要不然容易感冒。 — Buy another blanket, otherwise you'll catch cold.
找房子不要找离马路太近的，要不然会很吵。 — Don't pick a place near the road, or it'll be noisy.
别吃那么多肉，要不然你会越来越胖。 — Don't eat so much meat, or you'll keep gaining weight.

grammar.explanation: Connective referring back to the previously stated fact/action, introducing the result it enables. Often 这样…就/才能….
examples: 我想申请离家近的学校，这样我就可以搬回家去住。 — I'll apply close to home, this way I can move back home.
学外语得常常听录音、念课文，这样才能提高听和说的水平。 — …this way you can improve listening and speaking.
买东西应该等打折的时候去买，这样才能买到便宜的东西。 — Shop during sales; this way you get bargains.

grammar.pattern: 只是 / 就是 (it's just that)
grammar.explanation: Mark a turn in thought, milder than 但是/可是. Appear in the final clause to point out the one flaw in something otherwise positive (first clause positive, second qualifies it).
examples: 我不是不同意，只是觉得太早了一点儿。 — It's not that I object, just that it's a bit too soon.
那件毛衣样子好是好，只是价钱太贵。 — The sweater looks good, it's just too expensive.
这个饭馆儿很好，也不贵，就是常常没有位子。 — Good and cheap, it's just often full.

grammar.explanation: Frames a comparison or topic by domain—character, interest, study, work, daily life, etc. Often pairs with 跟…一样/不一样.
examples: 在兴趣上，丽莎跟天明不太一样。
小林最近在学习上有很多问题，所以不太高兴。
在生活上，如果有什么问题，就告诉我。

grammar.pattern: Verb + 来 + Verb + 去 (repeated action)
grammar.explanation: A reduplicated frame meaning to do something back and forth or over and over, often implying no resolution. The same verb fills both slots.
examples: 你别在房间里走来走去，大家都睡觉了。
这个问题我们讨论来讨论去，最后还是没有办法解决。
妈妈叫我学经济，我想来想去还是选了化学。

grammar.explanation: 的 links an attributive to a noun (attributive+的+noun); 得 links a verb/adjective to a descriptive complement (verb/adj+得+complement); 地 links an adverbial to a verb (adverbial+地+verb). All read 'de' but differ in function.
examples: 你说的那位教授我不认识。 (的)
教授说我选课选得很好。 (得)
看完电视，爷爷奶奶慢慢地走上楼去休息。 (地)

grammar.pattern: 原来 as adverb and adjective
grammar.explanation: (1) Clause-initial, signals a sudden realization ('so it turns out…'). (2) Before a noun as adjective meaning 'former/original'—then it must take 的 (原来的). (3) Before a verb as adverb meaning 'previously, before a change'.
examples: 我早就听说有一个新同屋要来，原来就是你呀。
你还住在原来的宿舍吗？
他原来住在学校的宿舍里，后来搬到校外去了。

grammar.pattern: Four-character set phrases (成语/固定词组)
grammar.explanation: Fixed, non-literal expressions best memorized whole; they function like words but in limited slots. E.g., 一干二净 is adjectival but used mainly as a complement after 得; 丢三拉四 acts like a verb/predicate but takes no object or complement.
examples: 上个学期学的汉字，他已经忘得一干二净了。
他把房间打扫得一干二净。
你再这么丢三拉四的，你女朋友非跟你分手不可。

grammar.pattern: 一会儿…, 一会儿…, （一会儿又…）
grammar.explanation: Lists two or more alternating actions/states to show that someone or something keeps changing or is unpredictable. With three items, the third takes 又 (一会儿又…).
examples: 你怎么了？一会儿哭，一会儿笑的。
这几天一会儿很冷，一会儿又很热，不少人都感冒了。
一会儿叫他洗衣服，一会儿叫他出去买东西，一会儿又叫他做饭。

grammar.explanation: In questions, presses the other person for a definite answer ('what on earth / in the end'); implies the speaker has been agonizing over it. Also appears in embedded questions.
examples: 你到底去不去？
他们俩到底为什么分手？没有人知道。
毕业以后到底念研究生还是找工作，我还没考虑好。

grammar.explanation: Means 'at all, simply (not)'; typically reinforces a negation to stress that something is utterly not the case.
examples: 老师今天介绍的语法，我根本不懂。
他们俩吵架的事儿我根本没听说，你别问我。
你根本不认识他，怎么知道他的兴趣爱好是什么？

grammar.pattern: Potential complement: Verb + 得了 / 不了 (dé liǎo / bù liǎo)
grammar.explanation: Indicates whether conditions permit an action; mostly negative, conversational, and interchangeable with （不）能+verb. In a negative-form question, the asker already assumes a negative answer.
examples: 那个演唱会我去不了了。 (= 不能去了)
那栋楼没有水电，住不了人。 (= 不能住人)
那个演唱会你去得了去不了？

grammar.pattern: Time / place expressions and pronouns as cohesive links
grammar.explanation: In narration, time words, place phrases, and pronouns connect sentences—but only when placed at the very beginning of the clause. Putting them after the subject makes the prose choppy.
examples: …就给他发了一个电子邮件。十分钟以后，才看见天明急急忙忙地跑过来。
我昨天去购物中心买东西，在那儿看见了我好久不见的两个老同学。
我一叫他，他马上就来了。

grammar.pattern: 过来 / 过去 (directional, relative to speaker's standpoint)
grammar.explanation: 过来 = movement toward the speaker's vantage point; 过去 = movement away from it. The movement may or may not pass a location.
examples: 小张往我这边走过来，好像有什么事要跟我说。
小王从我身边走过去，没有看见我。
张老师很高兴地往她老朋友那边走过去。

grammar.explanation: Spans a range of time, space, or degree.
examples: 他明年暑假要到中国去实习，所以从早到晚都在学习中文。
学跳舞要从易到难，慢慢儿来。
从你家到我家坐地铁得四十分钟。

grammar.explanation: Conjunction opening the second clause to state the outcome of the condition in the first clause.
examples: 上高中的时候，他一天到晚踢球，不学习，结果高中念了五年才毕业。
他常常麻烦别人，可是一点也不愿意帮助别人，结果一个朋友也没有。

grammar.pattern: 害得 (to cause trouble so that …)
grammar.explanation: 害 'to harm' + 得 introduces a clause describing the negative consequence inflicted on someone.
examples: 你昨天晚上没回来，害得我等了你一夜。
弟弟把我的手机拿走了，没告诉我，害得我找了半天。

grammar.pattern: 看起来 / 听起来 (it seems / it sounds)
grammar.explanation: Express an inference from what is seen or heard. Can come before or after the subject; what follows is the speaker's interpretation.
examples: 李阿姨这几天看起来好像心里有什么事儿，到底怎么了？
听起来你不想去了？算了吧！我自己去。
听起来挺不错的，你就租吧。

grammar.pattern: Directional complements expressing result (下来 / 出来 etc.)
grammar.explanation: Directional complements often signal result/state rather than literal motion: 下来 = detachment/separation; 出来 = emergence from hidden to open, or nothing-to-something. Memorize them with their verbs.
examples: 孩子一生下来就开始给他们存教育费。
把你的问题说出来，我们大家帮你解决。
我看出来他生气了。

grammar.pattern: Verb phrase + 来 + Verb phrase (purpose, 'in order to')
grammar.explanation: 来 links two verbs; the second states the purpose of the first action.
examples: 应该找工作挣钱来减轻父母的负担。
大家计划开一个舞会来给他过生日。
应该用听录音的办法来提高汉语水平。

grammar.pattern: Optional 了 with completed actions (background vs. main event)
grammar.explanation: 了 is not obligatory for completed actions. When a completed event serves as background to the main narrative, the verb may omit 了 (e.g., 存 takes no 了 because it is background to 丽莎和雪梅走了进来).
examples: 孩子一生下来就开始给他们存教育费。(背景，存不带了)
这时候丽莎和雪梅走了进来。(主要事件，带了)

grammar.explanation: Interrogative in form but assertive in meaning, needing no answer. Affirmative-form rhetorical questions carry negative meaning; negative-form ones carry positive meaning.
examples: 他昨天根本没复习，能考一百分吗？(意为：不可能)
她怎么不想想，父母挣钱多不容易啊？(意为：她应该想想)
谁不知道你是父母的乖孩子！(意为：人人都知道)

grammar.explanation: 而 connects two clauses or noun phrases in contrast or complementary relationship ('while / whereas / but / and yet'). More formal and tighter than 但是/可是. Can appear mid-sentence or sentence-initially to pivot to a contrasting topic.
examples: 西边是高原，而东边是平原和大海 (The west is plateau, while the east is plains and sea)
而中国的西南，高山、高原多 (As for China's southwest — pivots to a contrasting sub-topic)
不是不想去，而是没有时间 (It's not that I don't want to go, but that I have no time)

grammar.pattern: 不是…而是… (it's not X, but rather Y)
grammar.explanation: Emphatic correction: denies one description and replaces it with the true one. Common in both speech and formal writing. More direct and emphatic than 不是…是…
examples: 这不是问题，而是机会 (This is not a problem but an opportunity)
他的成功不是偶然的，而是努力的结果 (His success is not accidental but the result of hard work)

grammar.explanation: Both halves present in formal/written Chinese, making the logic fully transparent. In speech one half may be dropped, but in expository writing keeping both signals rigor. Often chains multiple instances to build paragraph structure.
examples: 因为东部和南部自然条件比较好，所以人口主要在这些地方 (Because natural conditions are better in the east and south, most people live there)
因为签证还没办好，所以我只好推迟出发 (Because the visa wasn't settled, I had no choice but to delay departure)

grammar.pattern: X呢 as topic marker / parallel-contrast introducer
grammar.explanation: Placing 呢 after a topic noun or phrase signals a new parallel element for contrast or comparison — 'As for X, ...' or 'And what about X?'. Compact and conversational in feel but structurally elegant for building parallel descriptions.
examples: 西北呢，沙漠多，自然条件不太好 (As for the northwest, there are many deserts and conditions are poor)
这个问题解决了。那个问题呢？ (This problem is solved. What about that one?)
你的意见我明白了。他呢？ (I understand your view. What about his?)

grammar.explanation: Presents two mutually exclusive alternatives. Slightly more colloquial than 或者 and implies that one of the two is inevitable. Common in reasoning and debate.
examples: 要么坐飞机，要么坐火车，你来决定 (Either fly or take the train — you decide)
他这个人，要么不做，要么做得非常好 (He either doesn't do it at all or does it extremely well)

grammar.pattern: 非…不可 (must; there is absolutely no alternative)
grammar.explanation: Double-negative emphatic construction meaning 'must / absolutely have to'. Stronger and more literary than 一定要 or 得. The thing after 非 is what is required; 不可 closes the negation.
examples: 这件事非你去不可 (This matter absolutely requires you to go — no one else will do)
他非拿到那个奖学金不可 (He absolutely must get that scholarship)

grammar.explanation: Universal concessive: 'No matter X, the result is always Y'. The 都 in the result clause is obligatory. Formal register; 不管…都… is the colloquial equivalent.
examples: 无论价钱多少，我都要买 (Regardless of the price, I'll buy it)
无论遇到什么困难，我们都要坚持 (No matter what difficulties we face, we must persevere)

grammar.pattern: A是A，可是/就是… (concessive acknowledgment: 'it is indeed A, but...')
grammar.explanation: Acknowledges a quality before introducing a reservation. The repeated predicate softens the contrast and signals balanced judgment. Very common in spoken Mandarin; also effective in written commentary.
examples: 好看是好看，可是哈尔滨现在冷得不得了 (It is indeed beautiful, but Harbin is incredibly cold right now)
这个办法好是好，就是太费时间 (This approach is good, it's just too time-consuming)

grammar.pattern: V/Adj 得不得了 (extremely; to an unbearable degree)
grammar.explanation: Complement of degree expressing extreme intensity — much stronger than 很/非常. Pattern: Adj/V + 得不得了. Common in speech and informal writing.
examples: 冷得不得了 (incredibly / unbearably cold)
挤得不得了 (incredibly crowded)
高兴得不得了 (overjoyed)

grammar.pattern: 大多 (mostly; for the most part) — adverb
grammar.explanation: Formal/literary adverb meaning 'most' or 'the majority'. Modifies a predicate directly. More authoritative in written description than 大部分 + N, which is a noun phrase.
examples: 中国的河流，大多是从西往东流 (Most of China's rivers flow from west to east)
与会者大多是学者 (Most of those attending the meeting were scholars)
他的建议大多被采纳了 (Most of his suggestions were adopted)

grammar.explanation: Marks gradual intensification of a quality or action over time. Works with adjectives and some verbs. Common in both speech and writing.
examples: 随着经济的发展，城市越来越大 (As the economy develops, cities are getting bigger and bigger)
她的汉语越来越流利了 (Her Chinese is becoming more and more fluent)

grammar.explanation: Emphasizes that even an extreme or unlikely case is included, implying all less extreme cases are also true. 都 and 也 are interchangeable here.
examples: 连基本的汉字都不认识 (Doesn't even know basic Chinese characters — implying their level is very low)
连她都觉得太难了 (Even she found it too difficult — implying she is highly capable)

grammar.pattern: 说不定 / 不见得 (hedging expressions for uncertainty)
grammar.explanation: 说不定 (maybe, hard to say) softens a claim about a future or uncertain outcome. 不见得 (not necessarily) pushes back on an assumption. Both are more colloquial and nuanced than 也许/可能.
examples: 说不定他今天不来了 (Maybe he won't come today after all)
有钱不见得就幸福 (Having money doesn't necessarily mean being happy)

grammar.explanation: The 把 disposal construction (preposition, Lesson 15) fronts a definite, affected object before the verb to show what is done to it or how it ends up. The verb must carry additional material: a result complement, direction, 了, or a quantifier. Signals intentional, completed, or deliberate action.
examples: 把行李托运了 — check the luggage (dispose of the luggage by checking it)
把房间整理好 — put the room in order (result complement 好)
把东西放好 — put things away properly

grammar.explanation: Passive marker 被 (preposition, Lesson 18) introduces the agent. Often carries an adverse or unexpected sense in Chinese. The verb usually has a complement or aspect marker. Agent can be omitted when unknown or unimportant.
examples: 东西被人拿走了 — things were taken away by someone
行李超重了，被罚了钱 — the luggage was overweight and a fine was paid
他被选上了 — he was chosen/selected

grammar.explanation: Correlative conjunction pair (Lesson 11): 'not only … but also …'. Escalates from the first clause to the second, which is stronger or adds new information. Subject can precede 不但 (same subject) or follow 不但 (different subjects).
examples: 不但方便，而且安全 — not only convenient, but also safe
不但价格合适，而且质量很好 — not only is the price right, but the quality is also good
她不但学习好，而且体育也很棒 — she excels not only academically but also in sports

grammar.explanation: 'Besides / in addition to X, also Y.' (Lesson 8). With 还/也, it adds information. Without 都, it means 'in addition to'; with 都, it typically means 'except for X, all Y'. Extremely versatile in both speech and writing.
examples: 除了中文以外，她还学日文 — besides Chinese, she also studies Japanese
除了这件以外，我都喜欢 — I like all of them except this one
除了想省钱以外，还想要自由 — besides wanting to save money, (he) also wants freedom

grammar.explanation: Conditional: 'if … then …' (Lesson 9). 如果 opens the condition; 的话 is an optional closing particle that softens and clarifies the conditional frame. The main clause often uses 就 for correlation. Polite and common in both speech and writing.
examples: 如果你想住校外的话，我可以帮你找地方 — if you want to live off campus, I can help you find a place
如果价格合适的话，就买吧 — if the price is right, go ahead and buy it
如果你有时间的话，我们可以一起去 — if you have time, we can go together

grammar.explanation: 'More and more [adjective/verb].' (Lesson 15). Describes progressive change over time. The adjective or stative verb directly follows; no additional 很 or degree adverb.
examples: 越来越重要 — more and more important
越来越忙 — busier and busier
电脑在我们生活中越来越重要 — computers are becoming increasingly important in our lives

grammar.explanation: 'Even … also/even.' (连 as preposition, Lesson 17). Emphasizes an extreme or unexpected case. The element after 连 is the most extreme/unlikely member of a set, which is still included.
examples: 连家具都没有 — there isn't even any furniture
连他也不知道 — even he doesn't know
他忙得连饭都没时间吃 — he was so busy he didn't even have time to eat

grammar.explanation: Simultaneous actions: 'while doing X, also doing Y.' (一边 as adverb, Lesson 8). Both verb phrases describe actions happening at the same time by the same subject. Common in narrative and descriptive writing.
examples: 一边听音乐，一边做作业 — while listening to music, also doing homework
一边吃饭，一边聊天 — eating and chatting at the same time
他们一边走一边说话 — they walked along talking

grammar.explanation: Progressive aspect: action is currently in progress (Lesson 8). 正在 emphasizes that the action is ongoing right now. 呢 at the sentence end reinforces the in-progress sense. Contrast with 在 alone (less emphatic) or 着 (ongoing state rather than dynamic action).
examples: 他正在上网 — he is surfing the internet right now
她正在做饭呢 — she is in the middle of cooking
